user_comment: **TRUE PETITE SIZING** Okay sooo……. I am super pleased with these scrubs. I am a new grad RN, I am 5’0” tall, and 95 pounds. I wear a 00 in jeans. I have been on the hunt for some TRUE petite sized scrubs. So far I have tried white cross, barco, Adar, and these HeartSoul. None of the others have even come close in comparison to how good these scrubs fit me! I got the XXS petite in the pants and the XXS in the top. I do have a little booty for my size, and these pants actually kind of show it off 🤣 which I never can say in scrubs because in all other brands i get the absolute smallest size and they’re still baggy on me. I may could have gotten just an XS petite in these pants and they would still have fit me good. Very pleased with the true petite fit. I haven’t wore them for a shift yet so I’ll update later on their durability, but so far the quality seems good.

Not bad, could be better though Not bad! They are definitely comfy and stretchy. The material feels very thin though and there aren’t as many pockets as I’d like. Also, maybe this is just due to my body shape, but there is extra fabric that bunches up right above my butt (see pic). Once the matching top comes in I’ll see the two together and decide if I want to keep the set. Overall these aren’t bad but I like my Cherokee and Amazon Essentials brands more for now. I’m about 175 pounds and usually wear a 12 in jeans/ pants and ordered a L in these which worked well overall.

5’9” tall 210 Fits just like heart and soul sold in scrub shops. I’m wearing an xl in pants which is My regular size and a 2XL in the top cause I like them roomy. I usually wear an XL in T-shirt’s. Was afraid ordering online would be a disaster but so happy these fit perfect. And are long enough without having to order the tall. Being tall it’s always had to find pants long enough. Will definitely buy from them again! Also have washed them several time since and still holding up great. I don’t dry any of my scrubs so I don’t know how they hold up on the dryer.

I LOVE these scrub pants! I am a die-hard Greys Anatomy scrub wearer but these are a game changer! I love that they are not low rise (the description oddly says they are). As a nurse I do a LOT of bending over and popping crack is not cute! I normally wear a medium-tall in Greys Anatomy but I ordered a large-tall in these & they fit perfect. For reference I have a big butt and athletic thighs I am 5'6" and 160lbs I bought a large tall but I could have gotten away with a medium tall I think. The talls are really long like 34" but the regulars would have been to short. A++

Mostly great scrubs These pants fit great and feel great, but I just noticed this small separation in the waistband. I've only washed these twice, and didn't put them in the dryer either time. If it wasn't such a small area to reinforce, I'd return them and be on the hunt for a different brand. But I can deal with it and will probably continue to order these pants. I'm 5'1 and wear a size 10 or 12 in most pants, and the medium petite fits me perfectly.

Perfect for petites Love love love these pants! I'm 5'2" 105 and can never find pants that fit and look like I'm not swimming in them. I ordered xxs petite and it fits perfectly. Fitted at hips legs and knee which I can never find. Only down side is they are a little thin like most scrubs are so definitely have lines. Pewter color is more of a dark charcoal but that's what I was looking for. Definitely buying more

Best scrubs ever! These are honestly the ONLY brand and style of scrub pants I wear. I have multiple pairs in every color. The fit is perfect! I will say for me personally the sizing is a little odd. I’m 5’4 and fluctuate between 130lbs & 140lbs, I wear a 5/27 in jeans and other pants. In these scrubs I usually buy the XXS or XS. I like them a little more form fitted and because they stretch if I go bigger (I’ve tried small and mediums before) they literally fall down if I have my phone in my pocket. I wish they had more colors to choose from! You won’t regret getting these, just make sure you look at the size chart!

Ceil color is too short Let me start off by saying, I am a loyal heart and soul customer. All of my scrub bottoms are from heart and soul. They are comfortable, have plenty of stretch, and the petite joggers fit me perfectly (I’m 5’3). I own 8 pairs, that’s how much I love them. But the ceil blue color is an exception. I ordered 2 pairs: one in teal, and one in ceil. Both are size medium petite. But the ceil is about 2 inches shorter and it looks ridiculous. They are the exact same style and size, just different colors. I don’t understand it, and will be returning. The rest of the colors are great, but avoid the ceil blue color.

Perfect if you are petite—UPDATED REVIEW Height: 4'11” Bust: 34B Waist: 23-24 in Weight: 95 lbs Original 4/2018 Review: I'm a XXS petite in both Cherokee and Grey's Anatomy scrub pants, and they are always still a little big on me. I’m small chested and not “curvy”. This is my first time purchasing HeartSoul scrubs. I love the jogger style of these pants. The fabric is definitely more of an activewear type fabric compared to my other scrubs. They aren't see through. Not too thick or thin, in my opinion. They're not warm like my other scrubs, but I live in Southern Arizona, so it's perfect. If I wear the waistband slightly below my waist, it's still a little scrunched at the bottom, but no big deal. I work as a nurse in a hospital, and these are still professional looking. It's about time I found a pair of scrub pants that don't drag on the floor. In the original photos (first 4), I have the pants paired with the HeartSoul Pitter Pat top in XXS. UPDATED REVIEW 1/4/22: I had purchased two pairs of these joggers in 4/2018. They are still going strong. I added one new picture. I’ve washed them sooooo many times, and they have not even lost their color, it’s crazy. No exaggeration. Side pocket zipper, drawstring, etc. are all intact and functional. I have tried the Figs and the Cherokee infinity joggers and have found Figs to look weird on me because I’m short and the Cherokee infinity was way too suffocating on the waist. These are very comfortable and an hesitant to buy any other brand.

But, I like everything else about it so I’ll be trying the regular size this time and returning this. Still 5 stars because everything else is perfect! I am 5’10 but have longer legs than other 5’10 ers LOL. My inseam is 36 for over the shoe wide leg pants and 32 for ankle length pants
Runs big Purchased these for nursing school. Love them, but they run very big. I’m a size 6 pants. Ordered a small and could’ve fit an XS. I am 5’4” 145lbs here for reference.

Color not as expexted These were very comfortable and a medium petite fit my 5’2” 155lb frame perfectly (even small petite fit well but the pants were snug). But the Hunter green color was just a little lighter than I’d hoped. Picture doesn’t do justice IMO, but I tried so y’all could see.

Petite Size is Actually Petite I’m 5’0” and 112 lbs. I have a rectangular-shaped body with not much for curves, so it’s hard to find something that fits my height but isn’t too tight at the waist. First time trying to find jogger scrubs.... Ordered a Small Petite jogger scrub in this brand and several others (Barco One is the only other scrub that arrived so far). As you can see from pic, the HeartSoul brand (Royal blue joggers on top) is about 3 inches shorter in the inseam and is about 2 inches narrower on the legs compared to the Barco One brand (Caribbean colored jogger on bottom). For other very short ladies out there, the HeartSoul seems to fit best so far. Very lightweight and flexible fabric!

GREAT FIT I am so happy I tried these scrubs. I have been looking for a set of joggers that were not overly baggy. I was worried the top was going to be boxy, but it is form fitted and cute. The material is stretchy and breathable. The drawstring on the pants is just for decor which is kind of a bummer when your pager and phone pulls them down a little throughout the day. I ordered xxs pants and xxs top. I am 5'4, 110 lbs I did have to return the first top I ordered due to receiving the wrong size, they were very fast and easy to return. I will order more in the future.

Flattering, but some flaws They’re so cute, but there are a few downsides and ultimately I will be returning. They do run small, I’m 5’3, 136lb (typically a medium, size 8ish), and the XS petite was my best fit. Some things I don’t like about this product - not for people with thighs!! You can’t move one inch without them making the swooshing sound. I’d lucky if my thighs didn’t start a fire by the end of a 12 hour shift. The front tie also doesn’t do through the whole pant for some reason? I have a skinny waist and big hips, so after tightening it as much as possible, I still had to awkwardly pull the pants up every 5 minutes as they made their great migration down my leg. I really wished they worked, they’re so cute, but the annoyance from these pants is just too much.

Warm/Cozy Yes, my mirror needs windex lol, sorry in advance. I was looking for a pair of joggers that weren’t super thin or silky, these feel more substantial compared to most others. They’ll be great for walking across campus in the winter months and just something comfy to wear in general. I ordered medium and they fit relatively well, I am between 5’7” - 5’8” tall, 150-155 lbs athletic/average build. I do wish there was about an inch or so more on length, but I kind of expected them to be a bit short since I am on the taller side. The pockets are nice and roomy/deep and there seems to be a lot of wiggle room around the hip area, I wish they were a little more slim fit but that may take away some of the comfort idk. I could roll the waist band down and wear them lower, or keep it up and wear around my natural waist if I wanted. The band does feel just a tiny bit tight right now but expect it to loosen up a bit after being worn a few times, at least I hope so bc I always feel they’re more comfortable that way. All in all they are nice, and I’ll be wearing them. I took off one star for length and fit in the hip area.

So cozy These are a win. Last year I had another brand of these and I loved them. I over the last year I have lost about 50lbs and now they no longer fit and I really wanted another pair for this coming fall and winter to keep me warm. I'm glad I gave these a try. Based on the size chart I fall right in the Medium based on weight and measurements. I am 5ft between 135-140lbs and wear a 6-8 in pants. The last pair of these I had ran a bit big and I had a medium that fit perfectly when I was 50lbs more so I decided to try these in a Small. I am glad I did. The small fit me perfectly. These leggings have a high waist, are very stretchy and opaque, they will pass the squat test and they are thick and fleece lined to keep you warm in the chilly weather. I haven't put the water resistance to the test, but based on the feel of the fabric I believe it. It feels like it would shed water instead of quickly absorbing it. I love the side pockets and the addition of a zippered pocket is a huge bonus in my opinion. I think the little loop for keys is a bit unnecessary and not super functional as it is pretty small and would be difficult to clip your keys to or feed the keyring through.

Very nice quality, size up if you are between sizes. I am 5'7" and 164lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size L in bottoms (between size 10 and 12). Based on the size chart I should have been a size L, but I size up to an XL because I hate it when the waistband on a legging cuts in to my stomach. The size XL fit great, I could have probably worn a L as well.These do have a thin elastic band around the top of the waistband, so if that sort of thing bothers you or pinches you I would size up.This is a wonderful midweight fleece, that is super soft on the inside and has a nice sleek exterior. The color is true to the product picture. I love the two deep drop pockets on the side and love even more the fact that one of these has a zippered pocket on front of it. I also really like that there is a loop to hang a badge or keys or whatever off of. This is a nice feature. I often wear these types of fleece leggings with a dressier tunic and boots to work and always struggle with where I should clip my badge.I would highly recommend them, they are very nice. I have only washed them once so far but I didn't have any issues with shrinkage or pilling.

Comfortable and form flattering I love fleece lined leggings especially in the cooler months. These are warm but breathable so even in mild temperatures they don’t overheat. These leggings can sit high on the waist which emphasizes hip curvature and a narrow waist. The deep pockets are perfect for storing a phone especially if using during a workout with earbuds. There’s also one zipper pocket that is shallow but could store money, cards in case one doesn’t want to carry a purse or wallet. I’m 5ft about 108 lbs and the XS fits true to size. The material is smooth and stretchy but can pick up fine hair such as that of pets. The seaming in the back discreetly flatters the shape of derrière there’s also a small fabric tag on the front hip I assume to attach keys. Nit exactly a safety feature but I appreciate that the design keeps the active wearer in mind. With the material being moisture wicking, it’ll be nice to choose these leggings regardless of the precipitation. The value for the price is reasonable as this will be a well loved pair.

Helped Loads With the Cold! These leggings are very cute and functional. I needed fleece leggings as I work with kids and the mobility of leggings with pockets is essential for me. They’ve kept me very warm during cold weather and compress on the tummy nicely without hurting. They’re snug so I definitely recommend considering a size up from your usual, which worked well for me. I’m barely 5’1 and they bunched up at my angle, so I’ll need to get them hemmed. It’d be nice to have a petite option, but it’s not a major issue for how much warmer I am at work. I’ve only had them for a couple weeks, and I’m curious to see how well the material will withstand pilling at the thighs. Will update if there are major issues.

Good fit but too long The fabric makes for excellent tummy control. Good stretch makes it very comfortable. I’m 5’ 175 lbs. Large is a perfect fit, except for the pant legs are too long for me. It’s not too bad with sneakers. But they drag if I’m barefoot or wear completely flat shoes, sneakers, or sandals. I have a short torso, so they are high waisted and fall right above my bellybutton and waist, which is what I like for better tummy control. I only wish they were about an inch or two shorter. The side pockets are perfect for my phone and spending money or debit card. Highly recommend.

I talked myself down to 3 stars. I followed Amazon’s recommendation for sizing and ordered a small at 5’4” and 120 lbs. They were too big and I ended up returning them for an XS, which fit much better. It’s hard to find flare leggings that also have side pockets, so I was excited to find these. I carry around my phone and a vape pen, so pockets are pretty much a necessity. These pants aren’t that soft to me—more of a cheap cotton feeling despite not having any cotton in it at all. They may be a little too warm to wear when temperatures rise. My biggest gripe (aside from value) is that the side pockets don’t really lay flat. I noticed it on the first pair but thought it was because they were too big for me, but the smaller pair is like that as well. The bottom part of pocket sticks out like it’s already stretched out. Almost like they didn’t lay that part completely flat to sew the pockets on. Obviously not that visible with something in the pocket, but I’m worried carrying around things will cause the pockets to stretch and stick out further. Are they worth the $28 I paid (on sale, I might add)? Meh, no. They feel like cheap pants you’d pick up at Dollar General. We’ll see how they hold up in the long run.

Style is great but quality could be better I am 5'1, 166lbs. I originally ordered the medium 29"inseam in black a few weeks back and they fit perfect. I just ordered another pair (medium) in the navy blue and gray pink and since they dont offer that color in 29", I ordered the 30" for both. They arrived at the same time and it was instantly apparent that there is a quality assurance issue. The gray pink is a large and at least 32" inseam if not longer even though the label reads (M). The blue came looking exaclty like the first black one I ordered. As you see in the picture, there is a huge difference in the two even though they are ordered in the same size. The other thing I've noticed on the black one is the fabric is pilling on the inside near the front of the ankles. I really love these pants for the squat proof aspect, the tapered waist, and the comfort however there are some improvements that can be made to the quality of the fabric and the quality assurance of sizing. I am more than happy to pay extra for a longer lasting material but beggars can't be choosers so wish me luck on my next order!

Decent Quality These are a nice pair of wide leg linen pants. The fabric truly feels like linen fabric so if you're expecting the softness of organic cotton, these aren't for you. That isn't too say they're uncomfortable though. I like the weight and feel of the fabric. The pants are also well made. I haven't seen any uneven stitching or loose threads. I wear an 8 to 10 and ordered a Medium. They fit well throughout the hip but they're a bit snug in the waist. I'm 5ft 3.5 inches and the length is fine. I think with a low heel I'll be fine, I have relatively long legs. *Update* DO NOT, I repeat DO NOT put these in the dryer. Hang them to dry, otherwise they will shrink. I can't even wear them now as they look and feel like children's pants. Maybe on someone shorter, they'll be okay but its now a no for me.

Flattering, Well-Fitting, Heavyweight, Warm, Comfy-Cozy Slacks I was not expecting such a high quality, heavyweight pair of trousers when I ordered these! I'm blown away! These would be perfect to travel to colder climates in or simply to wear on cool days in more moderate climates. They are really comfortable and they look very nice on...like an expensive pair of business trousers. The back half of the waistband has elastic which could easily be concealed by simply running a belt through the belt loops. I found no fabric content tag on or in the garment, but the listing states that the fabric is 70% polyester/30% wool. Laundry instructions were attached to the trousers on a tag connected by a string (which you can see in some photos I've attached). It does not specify dry cleaning. It suggests washing by hand, avoiding high heat, and not wringing them. I ordered size Medium in Black and they fit me perfectly (without much extra room) except that they are about an inch or so too long. For reference, I'm 5'4" and long-waisted (therefore somewhat shorter-inseamed). My waist is about 29", my hips are about 38", and I'm about 124 lbs.

Cute, warm, thick These pants exceeded my expectations. I feel like I've walked out of a dark-academia mood board with these pants combined with fall styling. The pants are thick in material, soft, and easy to clean. They're not transparent or thin, but they do have enough fit in the back to show undergarment lines so I'd recommend seamless undergarments. The band hits my waist and holds up securely, that's usually an issue for me with pants so I'm very thankful for the fit! My only note is that I'm short so they're a bit long on me unless I'm pairing them with boots, so if you're 5'4 or below like me, expect to wear something with heels. I'm 5'4, 110lbs, a 24in waist, and typically between an x-small and small for fit reference.

True to size- on the fence about these.... Update: I wore these last weekend in 30 degree weather 20-30mph winds walking around the city. These jeans are warm! They are not super cute. Wear a long top to cover the bulky pocket area and tuck them into tall boots and they will look fine.(if it's really cold you'll prob be doing this anyway.) If the ankle was skinnier it would be ok. Wish they were a tad longer as I have long legs. Good for the price and very warm!!! Im on the fence with these. I ordered a medium. I'd say they are true to size. I am 5'7, 135lbs. I picked medium because I'm on the bigger end of size 6 and I'm tall. I never wear a size 4 but sometimes I could be an 8 if the jeans run small. My favorite jeans are size 6 long from american eagle. These fit fine, they're a little thicker but I wouldnt say make you look overweight. If you're buying these jeans I'm assuming fashion isnt your #1 priority but you dont want to wear boyfriend cut jeans from eddie bauer either. That being said my main problem with these jeans is the pelvic area. Not very fashionable. The zipper feels weird and the pockets pull showing the inside a bit near the belt loop. Check out my pics. The seam on the left leg wants to pull inward too. If I'm wearing these jeans for fashion, I wouldnt recommend but if youre wearing boots and a big sweater or coat then I say yes buy them. Wear a long top to cover the bulky pocket area and tuck them into tall boots and they will look fine.(if it's really cold you'll prob be doing this anyway.) If the ankle was skinnier it would be ok. Wish they were a tad longer as I have long legs. Good for the price and very warm!!! Im on the fence with these. I ordered a medium. I'd say they are true to size. I am 5'7, 135lbs. I picked medium because I'm on the bigger end of size 6 and I'm tall. I never wear a size 4 but sometimes I could be an 8 if the jeans run small. My favorite jeans are size 6 long from american eagle. These fit fine, they're a little thicker but I wouldnt say make you look overweight. If you're buying these jeans I'm assuming fashion isnt your #1 priority but you dont want to wear boyfriend cut jeans from eddie bauer either. That being said my main problem with these jeans is the pelvic area. Not very fashionable. The zipper feels weird and the pockets pull showing the inside a bit near the belt loop. Check out my pics. The seam on the left leg wants to pull inward too. If I'm wearing these jeans for fashion, I wouldnt recommend but if youre wearing boots and a big sweater or coat then I say yes buy them. They feel like they're going to be super warm. I'll update after wearing in the cold.

Review from the plus side The reason I gave these jeans a try was bc in the description, it says they changed the sizing to US sizes. I’m a true-ish size 16 (US), if stretchy can fit 14. I stand about 5’ 11”. I really like these jeans! The length is perfect IMO, they hit right above my ankle bone. They have a decent amount of stretch and are jean material (not jegging or legging material). The fit is perfect except for a few things which may just be bc of my body type. Now in the pics you can see the pulling of the pockets, and the zipper fell throughout the day, but the fit is still pretty good for me. Big butt and thighs are hard to dress. I’m worried that if I go a size larger, they will be too loose in the waist (& possibly everywhere else) and fall. The reason I knocked off a star is bc the lining isn’t like pictured; it’s not velvety like shown. It’s basically the lining in fleece lined leggings. Still soft and should be warm, but still.

Way too stretchy and loses its shape I have pros and cons for these jeans. Pros: definitely warm (which is why I bought them) Pro: Xs small fit great I’m 5’4 105lbs Pro: comfortable but only for a time which leads me into the cons Con: they are verrryy stretchy. So while they are comfortable and they fit good that only lasts for a while before they stretch out and your butt is super baggy. Washing them seems to put them back into place until you wear them and a few hrs later your back to baggy. Con: very high fit! You have to yank these things way up past your belly button for them to fit good. So I’d buy them again for the warmth part but I was really hoping to look good while being warm. Not sure why this isn’t a very popular type of Jean to make because the warmth is such a relief, I’m a little thing so I’m never warm. Hopefully I run across another pair that checks all the points.

True to size I am 5'6", 136lbs, 28" waist, & 40" hips & the medium fit very well with a little give. I wear about a 6 in jeans usually. I have a 33" inseam & these are plenty long so if you have longer legs they shouldn't be too short. The inside is soft & seem warm & cozy. I bike to work everyday & have to wear dark jeans there so these were ideal. I don't know how they wash yet. I will update once they're laundered & tested in 20° & below windy temps. They are a little squarish in the bum but that is fine with me as I'm wearing for warmth not style. Easy to move in unlike layering under pants. Update: Wore these to work & they were warm enough to bike in 20° without my legs freezing like they usually do. They also weren't so terribly warm that I was sweating once running around at work. They washed & dried just fine but they did shrink a bit. I'd suggest going up a size if you're in between sizes so when they shrink they aren't too small. Be sure to wash at least the 1st few times with dark colors too because the dye will run. For what I need them for they are good. I would also wear for outdoor fall activities or even winter if temps aren't too extreme. They did hold up to wind a lot better than unlined jeans but in high winds I'm sure it won't make much difference. If they were 25-30 bucks I'd buy several more as that is a better price point for these.

Cozy!! Normally I wear a size 26 or 6, my weight fluctuates a lot and I knew it was stretchy- but I didn't want to risk buying anything tight. A lot of leggings have a problem with being too tight in the crotch area and skinny jeans have problems with too much grip around the ankles. But these are great!! I ended up getting the small but the pants themselves were pretty long (I can cuff them and they don't feel overly tight). I would feel fine in these after a buffet and in a cold room! I wouldn't say they're crazy warm but I'm definitely comfortable and insulated. If you're over 5'5 these will probably fit you best. Anyone shorter try to buy to your waist and keep in mind you might have to cuff the ends. EDIT: After about a year and being in the washing machine multiple times, it fits! Letting you know these shrink! Still warm though.

Better than expected When I took these sweatpants out of the packaging, the first thing I noticed was their weight. These pants have a substantial feel to them, which is a sign of better quality compared to other sweatpants from Amazon that feel flimsy and are made from cheap material. These sweatpants look exactly as pictured and fit me very well. I am 5’4 and usually wear an inseam of 28-29 inches and these pants just hit my heels without touching the floor. There are no issues with the stitching or cut, unlike other sweatpants that are often cut crooked, fit oddly, or have seams coming apart. While these pants are on the thinner side, the material is not see-through. The inside isn't as soft as some other sweatpants, but it's still comfortable. The only major complaint I have is the design on the front. The item description calls it "elevated seams," which appear subtle and neat in the picture. However, in reality, the seam is crooked and doesn't look good. I don't think ironing would fix this issue, and it would be a hassle to iron sweatpants every time you wear them. I have another pair with this design that looks much better and doesn't have this problem, as shown in my pictures.

Cute and comfortable These pants are so cute an I’m in love with the shade of pink. They are lightweight and are really soft (material is 65% Polyester, 35% Cotton). I would recommend these joggers to others looking for a pair of lounge pants for Autumn.

Good looking pants I like to be comfortable, but I also like to look nice. I work from home so I spend a lot of time lounging around the house, but sometimes I need to run a quick errand. These are perfect for that. I actually wore them on a flight recently because I wanted to be comfortable, but not sloppy. These look nice and fall really nicely. I bought a large because that's my go-to size, even though I'm probably between a medium and small. I'm 5'8" 124#, but I like my clothes loose. Generally, with yoga-type pants, this works fine, but these are definitely big. They have a drawstring, so that's nice. At least I can keep them up! I also love that the drawstring is elastic. The only reason I deducted one star is because the fabric has a really weird synthetic feel, but not itchy. It's soft, but weird. It reminds me a lot of the polyester uniform I used to wear when I worked fast-food as a teen back in the 80s.

Cute pattern. Thin. Overpriced This pair of pants fits well and is comfortable. I ordered the XS. I am 5’4”, 110 lbs. It’s flattering. And maybe even a little long, which would make these great for taller people too. For me, it just means I can dry it in my dryer with abandon. The material is thin. Maybe just a tad thicker than t shirt weight. I tried to take a picture to show the texture. It’s *almost* a French terry feeling, but thinner. It’s not thick enough to have the loops. It does have a tag that reads 85% cotton, 15% poly. The tie is thin. Even for this type of shoelace tie. I probably am just noticing it more because it stands out so much against the black. These pants are great worn on their own. I especially like the ability to wear them over thermals. I think the price could be better per the materials.

Decent sweats I got these sweatpants for my daughter. She's 5'8" & about 125 lbs., I usually get her a small but I decided to grab a medium in hopes they'd give her a little more length. Over all the fit is okay; the waist fits securely but you can see from my picture they're a little "hippier" than she needs. Luckily, the hips don't stick out too much & they're not as noticeable with movement etc. Other than that, the material is of medium thickness. It's not the soft fuzzy kind inside but more of a terry cloth feel, they're comfortable & not scratchy though. I've machine washed & let them hang to dry & they come out fine. I like the pockets, but these are really just a very basic pair of sweats.

Cute and Comfy These are the cutest sweatpants material pants. The material has the right amount of stretch, so it hugs the body but stretches to fit curves. The pants have a fold over top which can be worn folded over but can also be worn up. when both folded over and not folded over the top is stretchy but not enough to indent your middle section. I am trying to nicely say it doesn't give me a muffin top no matter how I wear the pants. When worn folded over the pants are a bit lower waisted. My backside felt a little exposed, but they were still comfy. The sweatpants are a straight wider leg pant that is a bit long for my 5'5" frame. The straight leg design of this pant was made so that even with my larger thighs and backside they fit comfortably. The color of the pants is a little more faded than the picture which, in my opinion, makes them even cuter.These are the perfect everyday lounge pants or even some workout/dance class pants. The material is like sweatpants but not lined sweatpants. If you get hot easily, they may be a bit warm for physical activity. Fits well. The pockets are lined I like a lot about these pants. I agonized over sizing. I fell in between the suggestions. I am 5‘4“ and 110 lbs. I went with the XS. They fit me well. The waistband could be the slightest bit looser, but it’s not uncomfortable. They are a little long too. Which I also don’t mind. The split at the ankle looks cool. My favorite part of these pants are the lined pockets. That puts them in another stratosphere for me. They’re 85% cotton and brushed on the inside. So it feels soft. The fabric is medium weight. The striped waistband is also attractive. They’re nice sweatpants.

Some of the Best Sweats I've Ever Owned I'm 5'1 and weigh around 108 pounds, normally a size 4/small in everything. I ordered a small in these and they are PERFECTLY OVERSIZED! If you like your sweatpants to be more roomy and comfy, then order these in your normal size! Of course they are longer on me (since I'm so short) but the cuffed ankle doesn't allow that to be a problem. Pic is taken upon immediately opening them from the package. Color looks as advertised for the dark grey. The material is on the thinner side so you can wear them outside without burning alive, but they're still plenty warm for the cold! I keep it pretty chilly in my house (66°F) and wore these with a sweatshirt in perfect bliss. These are genuinely up there with some of the comfiest sweatpants I've ever owned. (The only sweatpants that slightly beat these in comfort is some old men's Nike sweats I yoinked from the bf.) Needless to say I will be purchasing these in more colors!!

Warm, Boyfriend Style, comfortable pants, slower shipping When I ordered these sweatpants the word "jogger" was used in the title and description so I'd like to be clear the pants I received are not true jogger style bottoms if that is what you need. [If you have worn Victoria's Secret or PINK sweats these remind me of the original Boyfriend fit that had the elastic ankle bands vs being straight open] However these are lovely, stark white, warm sweatpants that will hopefully last at least a season! I put them in the dryer on high heat for 40 minutes before trying them on (as a policy) and didn't notice any shrinking or "pilling" inside. I will update after a few washes. These do have an elastic waist band that stretches several inches as do the ankle bands.. i am kind of short and on the petite size so they were almost too large for me but I like the baggy look when wearing light coloured sweats. Please excuse the photos but I needed to get the review in and was solo trying to take a full length picture. I wanted to take a star away for shipping time since I ordered with Prime and it took nearly two weeks but I'm trying to leave my petty side out of it ;) For reference I am almost 5'3" /105 lbs/ 24.5"waist and bought the Small (there was no extra small)

Some of the Best Sweats I've Ever Owned I'm 5'1 and weigh around 108 pounds, normally a size 4/small in everything. I ordered a small in these and they are PERFECTLY OVERSIZED! If you like your sweatpants to be more roomy and comfy, then order these in your normal size! Of course they are longer on me (since I'm so short) but the cuffed ankle doesn't allow that to be a problem. Pic is taken upon immediately opening them from the package. Color looks as advertised for the dark grey. The material is on the thinner side so you can wear them outside without burning alive, but they're still plenty warm for the cold! I keep it pretty chilly in my house (66°F) and wore these with a sweatshirt in perfect bliss. These are genuinely up there with some of the comfiest sweatpants I've ever owned. (The only sweatpants that slightly beat these in comfort is some old men's Nike sweats I yoinked from the bf.) Needless to say I will be purchasing these in more colors!!

Yes Yes Yes 5’9 135lbs - Size Medium OK, I have a lot to say about these joggers because I am on the tall side and have always struggled with finding a nice versatile jogger that fits me in a flattering way. I love everything about these joggers. Material thickness: Perfect. I’ve worn these joggers to the gym, out to dinner with a lil jean jacket and some sneakers and have proudly lounged quite comfortably around the house/doing errands. Washing: 0 problems or leakage of color. Stretchiness: PERFECT. Waistband does NOT dig into your stomach. You can roll them down or keep them up and they stay put. Colors: Light grey, dark grey, black (awful with lint though), cream, olive green. 10/10 you can legit buy 3 of these for the price of 1 nike jogger and honestly, nike joggers just suck these days.

Comfortable! I got these pants from the HeSaYep store in size X-Large, as I usually wear, and they fit well, except that I wish they were a little longer for my height. For reference, my weight is 225 pounds, and my height is 5 feet and 10 inches. The material is 90% cotton and 10% polyester, and it is soft and comfortable. The pants washed well in the washing machine in cold water, normal cycle, followed by drying. They have both elastic and drawstring, but the elastic is very loose, and they are held mostly by the drawstring. I may change the elastic with a tighter one since it is inconvenient to tie the drawstring often. Overall, I am pleased with these pants and I would recommend them to a friend!

Overall Review First of all, ignore my mess! I have been searching for the perfect affordable legging for a long time. I pretty much live in them. They are definitely too long so I ordered the 25 inch. I’m thinking they are going to be perfect. The functionality is great when stretching and working out. They stay up when I’m running because I went with a small. Going with the small holds everything in better for me and hides my dimples. I don’t have to worry about any overspill because again there is no elastic in the waste band!

Average leggings that don't quite live up to expectations Let's start with the basics. I'm 5'4", 150 pounds. Ordered the large-xlarge and the fit is fine. The length is also good; it's full length as long as you don't bend or squat. And, despite how thin they are, they did seem squat-proof in the mirror. They are comfortable. Although they are polyester, they do feel breathable. However, that's really where the pluses end. They have no compression or tummy-sucking power at all, despite claiming "tummy control"; every bump and lump shows up, even in black. They do have pockets, but they are just up to the wrist in length, and just barely fit my Samsung galaxy 9 - a larger phone will stick out. Also, since they do not compress, my phone bounces out of the pocket with any sort of jumping around. They also have some fleecy lining, but it is SO THIN! These are appropriate for a cool indoor environment, but not for any sort of outside cold weather. Overall, they are good lounging leggings, but not appropriate for actual exercise-wear or outdoor warmth.

They are very high waisted and stay up pretty well. I’m about 5’4 and 125 pounds and I got the small/medium. It definitely fits well, but isn’t a super tight snug fit. They are really stretchy so if you wanted a tight fit you could probably get away with sizing down. They are a little long in the leg for me but most pants usually are. With the material how it is you can just pull it up and disperse the fabric and then you hardly notice. I have a white dog who sheds a lot, and they definitely get some hair and lint stuck to them, but not nearly as bad as some of my other leggings. The quality isn’t as good as a high end leggings but they are great workout or lounge pants for the cheap cost.

Nice, roomy, comfy cords These are very nice! Be aware that if you are short like me, (I am 5 feet 4 inches), you may find the legs a bit long and have to take them up. Almost everything anymore is too long for me on the legs, so I am used to having to do this. No points deducted for that.

Good material, but size up I am 5’ 10” and weigh 145lb and I ordered the size 6 regular which is my typical pant size. Unfortunately, I did not like the way these fit and would prefer a size 8 long. The material feels comfortable and lightweight and is quick drying. There is an elastic string inside the waistband with an opening in the front if you need to tighten the waist. This elastic opening also leaves two weird bulges in the middle of the waistband. I think the ankle cuff should have been slightly larger around to prevent the excessive bunching happening at the bottom of the pants. I plan to use these pants for hiking and kayaking. I do suggest that others size up in the waist size and length. Overall, the material is nice, but I recommend others size up in waist and length size. I also think there are a few technical issues with the design.

Well Crafted, Stylish but Short These jogger pants are really well-made and look stylish. They feature two side pockets and two cargo-style pockets—one with a zipper and another perfectly sized for a phone. The waistband is elastic and includes a string inside, although its function isn't clear. Additionally, there's a back pocket. The material is a lightweight windbreaker-type fabric.However, the length is a bit disappointing for me. Standing at 5’7” and weighing 130 lbs, the pants hit about 2+ inches above my ankle. When I bend down or jog, they tend to ride up further, ending about 3 inches above my ankle, which can be annoying.Despite the length issue, I really like these pants. They fit perfectly everywhere else. I'm considering cutting off the cuffs to convert them into capris.

Lightweight joggers but short on me! Fits more like capris. The Women's Cargo Joggers are lightweight and quick-drying, making them suitable for hiking and other outdoor activities. The fabric is not as comfortable as yoga pants. It is more lightweight, kind of noisy when you walk but it does have a decent stretch, allowing for easy movement. I appreciate the seven pockets, which are useful for carrying essentials. However, the fit could be improved. The length is not for tall people. I am 5'9" and weigh 124 pounds and got a size 4. so the sizing also runs a bit small, so it's advisable to order a size up. Additionally, the material, while quick-drying, feels somewhat flimsy and might not hold up well to rougher conditions. Overall, these joggers are functional for light outdoor activities and travel but have some shortcomings in size, fit and durability.

Look and feel great These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

These are great! These are a true Legging. Thick black, go up to your actual waist vs your hip bone and are not sheer at all. I love them! I’m 5’9 about 137 and I ordered a small, but sent back for XS only because they were a little loose. I am never an XS but these are a lot better. You can see the length is great for taller girls too. Don’t hesitate, buy them, you’ll be happy! The only reason I took off a star is because the bottom of the leggings is not fitted. They are a little loose. I would prefer they were a little bit more tight around the ankle but that doesn’t make me want to send them back because overall the rest of them is perfect. The waist band is a solid 2 inches in length, so it really holds them up while on your body. Definitely recommend.

Finally, the PERFECT Legging These leggings are amazing. I honestly love everything about them. I'm a relatively short (5' 5"), chubby (size 14) woman, so finding leggings that flatter me is not always a simple task. It's especially difficult to find flattering leggings that don't also feel like they're restricting the blood supply to my feet. These are the perfect balance between holding everything in and cutting off my circulation. The feel nice and firm and secure, and they do an excellent job of smoothing out lumps, bumps, and bulges. At the same time, they're also really comfortable. The fabric is soft and the wide waist band keeps them from digging into my torso. They also come up to my natural waist, so they act almost like a pair of Spanx and they don't cut me in half or make a weird muffin top situation. I've worn them a couple of times (under dresses), and the fabric seems to be holding up well. I haven't noticed any pilling or ripped stitching at all. While they are definitely more expensive than other leggings I've purchased, they are also well worth the cost. I love them, and I'm buying them in every color.

user_comment: Need smaller size Update: purchased size small and they fit great. They wash and wear well. I own multiple pairs and wear them all the time. They hold up nicely. They are very easy to move in and I enjoy wearing them to the park to play with my toddler. They are also nicely made and good quality. I think ladies with a curvy bottom half (like myself) will enjoy these! The accommodate your butt and thighs in a flattering way. The waist doesn’t dig into me or cause muffin top. They don’t bag out or sag during the day in size small. Original review: I’m 5’4” 130-135lbs (pear shaped) and normally wear a 4-6. The size medium was too big. They felt like they where going to fall down during the day and the crotch was baggy. Overall just not a snug fit. They look ok in pics, but def are not snug enough. Like the color and style. They came up to cover my belly button. Def a true high rise! Will need a size small! They have a generous amount of stretch. If between sizes I would size down. I’m sometimes a small and sometimes a medium in leggings. More often than not a medium in leggings. They are what I would call a jegging. They aren’t as thick as a true Jean material, but not so thin they show all your lumps and bumps. I like that they have actual pockets on the back. You can wear them on their own with any shirt or also as a traditional legging with a tunic or longer top. They don’t look like leggings so I believe the black or dark wash (or whatever conservative colors) would be appropriate to wear with a nice blouse and dresser shoes for work or a nice restaurant. In my opinion at least, for most occasions.

Great pants, just not right for me So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

Thick hips and booty approved I'm 5' 5" with a smaller bust/waist, but LARGE hips and a** (dictionary pear-shape), ON TOP of being plus sized. Other pants I've bought have a gap in the back, every time, without fail, sometimes as much as 3"-4" between my back and the pants themselves, solely due to needing the wideness of the larger sizes for my more curvaceous areas. Even with belts, it's a struggle. These pants do not have a gap. None. Flush against my body all the way around, even in the back. I was FLOORED. I am a US18W (while my tops run closer to a smaller US16/larger US14) just to scale just how much of a size difference there is when it comes to waist vs hips), if you're built like a pear you understand how much of a pain the gap fight is. There's just enough stretch that even at my widest points, they're comfortably snug. Bit of a problem area in my belly after losing weight, front of pants fit comfortably without pressing into/floating over my lower torso area. If you're unsure about buying pants online because you have curvy-girl problems, buy the pants. Just put them in your cart. I bought two pairs of this brand, I'll be replacing all my old jeans with additional pairs soon. The struggle is real, ladies, let these pants handle your curves like a boss.

Jegging Black is NOT black I am a 5' 8" tall, thin girl with mostly leg. These pants fit me absolutely perfectly; hugging the right places without suffocation and going low past my ankles but not dragging on the floor. However, looking back at the original order, I noticed that I bought the jeggings black, and my current pants were blue. I ordered them anyway to compare old vs new. Pants on the right is the SAME exact pants as the one on the left after owning them for about 6 months. The new pants are also not black but a very dark navy blue. Real usable pockets! (: But wrinkle prone ): I was really happy to find out that there were real actual usable pockets in the front instead of the fake pockets that they always put on women’s pants. However, the pockets in the back are fake. Cant win ‘um all, I guess!The pants are very stretchy, so they’ll give a very slimming fit with a tighter appearance. However, you can expect to still be very comfortable in these pants due to them being so stretchy. I’m about 5’7” or 5’8” and about 180 pounds and purchased a size XL, and I’d say they were a good fit and perfect length. Also, I carry most of my weight in my hips, butt, and thighs, and usually I feel like my hips look just too big, but these pants really gave a nice and natural slimming affect (or effect? Ahhhh I’m always forgetting the difference!) for me and my body type.Also I love that these pants have belt loops! these pants look amazing with a nice tucked in blouse or sweater with a cute belt. Just keep in mind that the belt can’t be too wildly thick because the belt loops are still just a tad bit smaller than average.There’s one con with these pants though, and it’s that they are very susceptible to wrinkles. If they come out wrinkly from the dryer, that’s really not a big issue because once you put them on, they’ll stretch around you so nicely that the pants won’t have wrinkles anymore. The issue is that once you’ve been wearing them all day or for a couple of hours, they’ll start to wrinkle and crease around the knees and crotch area. The pictures I’m sharing is after I’ve worn the pants all day at work, so you can see what I mean about the wrinkles. I find this to be a common problem with a lot of pants and honestly these pants are not as bad about it as some others because some other pants can start to wrinkle up so bad around the crotch area that they start to give the appearance of a camel toe, but these don’t ever get that bad. There’s an easy solution for you though if you’re worried about this happening, just buy them in black. The black won’t show the wrinkles like the colored pants. I’m probably going to buy a pair in black too because these really would be the perfect pair of just plain professional black pants to have around. However, I still really love the green pair that I purchased even despite the wrinkles, and I will continue to wear them! These pants are still so super flattering, and that’s why I just couldn’t take a star off for the wrinkles.**UPDATE**So I did end up buying the black pair of these really soon after I had purchased the green pair. Both of these pants have become my absolute favorite professional pants to wear. I literally wear the black pair almost once a week because they’re just such an easy professional go-to and go with almost everything. The vibrant coloring for both pants has never faded. However, I’m coming back to deduct a star because I have had to restitch all the belt loops on both pants. The stitching quality on these pants is downright terrible. For that, these pants should at least be cheaper because if we are all going to have to spend an hour restitching these pants then we all deserve a serious discount for them. Now, maybe it’s just because I have a bigger butt and hips, and I’m always having to pull up my pants by the belt loops, but still, I don’t have to restitch the belt loops on my other pants. Also, I’ve noticed that the stitched seam that goes down the butt looks like it’s starting to loosen and might eventually break or rip soon. I really hope that doesn’t happen because I love these pants so much. They’re so comfortable and such a great fit. I’ve honestly been debating buying more pants in different colors, but I haven’t yet because I don’t know if it’s worth it paying like $26 + having to restitch the belt loops again & the pants may not even last a whole year as I’m worried about with my black pair. Ugh, again, I can’t express enough how much I love these pants and how they look, but I hate that the quality is greatly lacking with the stitching so purchase at your own risk knowing you’ll have to re-sew them ):

I love these! The fit on these pants is much better than I expected... they don't cling right to my hips and butt like a lot of pants like this do; they're just a tiny, tiny bit looser. That being said, you still need to order the same size you normally would. I'm 5'8" and 155, and wear a size 12. Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

5' 8" and 150 lbs, got the medium. I have been looking for nice work pants that didnt break the banks. I found these and they had good reviews and weren't expensive so I decided to give them a try. I ordered the tan and white (with pockets). The white pants were cut more like straight leg pants than skinny (which isn't a huge deal) and were bit smaller in the waist. I actually like the fit of the white pants better BUT they are see through. Definitely need to wear a long tunic or sweater with the white pants. The tan weren't see through at all but they were a bit shorter than the white pants. I couldnt get a good picture with the tan pants on but I attached a picture of them side by side and you can see the difference. They fit more like ankle pants. Both fit well around the waist but were a little hard to get over my thighs and butt. Overall I just didnt love them, but they didnt fit bad enough for me to return them. I'm 5' 8" and 150 lbs and I got the medium in both pairs.

Okay so I was really hesitant to buy these. I'm 5'8" and was really worried about the length. But I took a risk and assumed I could wear them as capris, worst case. Well I'm glad I bought them. Hips are 41" around and waist is 32" so it can be hard to find pants that fit my ass that actually stay up around the waist. These pants fit my body great! They're a little snug to pull over the booty but once they're up- they're a dream. They're slightly tighter in the waist than most pants so if you are apple shaped or have a wider tummy than booty, these may not be a good fit for you. I do have muscular legs and calves and they are not uncomfortably tight in these pants. In fact, they're appropriately loose even when sitting. No tight pulling and has slight looseness and give when seated. I ordered a size medium based off reviews and they fit exactly how I expected. Large would have been too baggy and not flattering on my body for my personal taste. They do have quite a bit of stretch so I'm glad I sized-down. I'm sure they'll stretch as I wear them and I don't wash my pants after every wear so a large may have been problematic. Apologies for the crappy angle on pics. I don't have a full length mirror and had to make do with the mirror I have! I even reccomended these for my sister who is 5'10". 6ft sister MAY be okay in a large if the hem is a bit longer. They'd definitely be more classic ankle pants. At the ankle, no rolling. They're seemingly petty wrinkle resistant which is awesome. Tried them on right out of the bag and there are no creases from packaging. I will likely buy these in other colors. I purchased navy and black. Black is pictured. See no roll, one roll, two rolls. Also tried to get a pic of the waist band and rise of pants. Ordered a Medium. 5'8", 170lbs, hourglass. Great fit.

Cute! Shrank but still fits. I am 5’9” and weigh 190 lb. I typically wear a US size 12-14 and ordered an XL. The photos were taken before they were washed, and one leg was slightly shorter than the other, maybe 1/2-1”. The first time I washed them I only let them dry about 15 minutes on low temp then hung them up to finish drying. They needed to be ironed afterward and did not shrink. The second time I washed them I forgot about them, and they went through a full dry cycle on low temp. They came out wrinkle free but shrunk 2-3” in length. They also shrank in width but still fit. They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

Order up if you want to wash! I gave these pants five stars because I like them a lot once I figured out what to buy. I usually wear a size 2/4 depending on the brand and am 5’6” tall, 120 pounds. The first pair I ordered were on this site called “ The millennium”. I ordered a small but received a medium and didn’t realize it. (they might’ve sent it to me because Company knew they were small ?) They were a little big when I tried them on and then I washed them and they fit perfect! So order up.I bought some more colors which I thought were the same, but weren’t. They were called “Point Pants”. Same brand and looked the same in the picture but weren’t the because they had more nylon in them than the millennium. I ordered a small and they fit perfect and didn’t really shrink. They were much thicker which would be good for winter, but it is spring and I wanted a thinner pant so I am keeping the medium size millennium pants so I can wash them. I hope this makes sense because these pants are great for winter and spring and summer. And they are also a great price!PS. I would’ve liked to have them a little shorter, but they were all out of the “short”pants.

Great pants for the price! These are great! I'm a college student with a 40-hour/week summer internship where I can't wear jeans, and I don't own a ton of business casual-type clothing. I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

White pull up pants Edited review. At first I loved how these pull on pants fit. As I looked into different makers of pull on pants. I decided on these. I loved the price. I then read the comments. Yes, buy 1 size down. I am 5'8" and weigh at average 205. These size 14 pants fit perfectly fine and comfortable. I am satisfied with my purchase. Now that I have used these pants a few times, I have discovered that when wearing cotton undies with this pants it causes for my undies and pants to slide down. I am finding the need of pulling the pants up every so often. Unfortunately, I can't return them anymore. I ended up sewing in the side seams by 1/4 inch each side.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). To take care of the leggings I recommend washing them in a Delicates Laundry Bags , with COLD water and avoiding sitting on surfaces such as gravel, this way they will last longer. In the long term ( 1 year if you use them ones a week) and since they wear out on the butt with rubbing (and white elastics appear). I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

Very comfy/inconsistent sizing I really like these pants. They are comfortable but also nice enough to wear to work. My only complaint is that their sizing is very inconsistent. I bought 4 pairs in different colors, same size and they all fit VERY differently. I also bought one color in 2 different sizes and received 2 completely different pairs of pants. I need you to understand I bought seven pairs of these pants I love these pants. I purchased them for work, and they are so. Comfy. They feel like I’m wearing leggings, but they definitely don’t look that way. The colors are very bright and I really appreciate that they are stretchy. I’m not tiny, and I carry my weight in my tummy and thighs, and these pull up high enough to hide my belly a little, while also being loose enough in the leg that it doesn’t look like the pants are strangling me. However, they are also formfitting enough to be fashionable. I literally bought seven pairs of these pants. I have always wanted colorful pants, but because I’m not really skinny, I didn’t think I could wear them and look good in them flash be comfortable, and I am so happy. I would definitely choose a longer shirt with these pants Especially if you are dressing for work. I purchased the 2X and they are super comfortable and relatively flattering to my body. I’m 5’6 and they are just a smidge too long, but not quite long enough to cuff. I decided I don’t care, but if you are a little bit shorter, Like me, it may be an issue, but they are so comfortable everywhere else that it’s not bothering me

My new wardrobe staple! Slimming and versatile Perfect pants! I have the bright blue, black, charcoal and houndstooth. True to size, I'm 5,6", 145lb, 29' waist, 39' hips, 32" inseam, hourglass shape (& Med fits perfect! No idea how people can say they run small. Based on reviews I went up a size the first time & bought L but they're too loose. The other three I bought Med. People always compliment them (especially the houndstooth and blue). Literally the most versatile pants I have. Flattering and slimming, (long enough to hit ankle even on long legs, fitted enough to be supportive and slimming without pinching or creating muffin top. Soooo comfy, stretch, very slimming! Not constricting or rigid like typical work slacks that crawl up your lady parts and make it look like you have thunder-thighs. Skinny slacks mean length doesn't matter and I can wear with flats or heels. UPDATE: the grey pants didn't wash well; the waist stretched, the legs shrunk up past my ankles and thread in left ankle came undone.

Just okay For reference, I am 5‘8“ tall, 155 pounds and typically wear a US women’s size 6 to 8. I got these pants because I love the color of them, I work for a fortune 100 company and spend time in office giving presentations and attending meetings regularly. It took a while for them to arrive, but I was very excited when they did finally get here. I have to say after opening, I was very disappointed. The pants had an odor upon opening. The color is much brighter than shown. The material was thick but not high quality. Similar to a Costco brand walking pant. The fit was not flattering on someone with wide hips. They are also very long and very high waisted. Even at 5’8”, the bottoms were dragging on the floor. These definitely look and feel like cheap pants. I own plenty of Amazon off brand items and love them. These however were a miss and I won’t be wearing them. I do not recommend .

I bought multiple colors starting with XXL after baby to now Large. These pants are great for work or casual depending on how you accessorize. Pro: - the pants are slim but not so Skinny - perfect for me as I have "big legs" - lots of stretch - Color does not fade overtime Pro or con depending on shape: - wait is slim and great for hourglass figure but has also lots of stretch Cons: - Durability, Not great! it starts to have the little elastic thread coming out of fabric after a little while and it's worst on the black with white thread version (Blackwhite-p44897sk). For the price and how the pants fits for me its acceptable. Just know that you will not keep this for years. I hope this helps. Good luck!!

Love this! I got this in a S, M, and L to see which one fit the best! So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

I usually wear a medium, or 8, but after reading other reviews, decided on a large so that they weren't as tight (5'8", 160 lbs). Although they do stretch out a bit more than I like during the day, I love the feel and the sizing. So I ordered a large in the plaid. WAYYYYY smaller all around. The tags are identical for size and blend, but the waist and length are clearly different. I'm going to try again. They would have been 5 stars except for this frustration. UPDATE: Added pics show the last pair ordered. Both size 6, one 6 long. HUGE difference. If you don't mind sending things back, then I would recommend. However, the seller needs better quality control of the product.

Great first try on I haven’t worn these out yet, but first impressions are good. The color is rich and the material feels sturdy. I’m 5’6”, ~140 lbs and the Medium fit my waist and legs great. There is a slight bunching of the fabric near the crotch but nothing crazy. I could have gone for an inch or 2 more in length, so if you have long legs, you may be getting high waters with these. The high waist and stretchy fabric make them comfortable to sit in and pretty easy to move around in.

I was SUPER skeptical of these jeans but they are really great. I like them A LOT. I gave them 4 stars only because A) the material is such that if you have cellulite or rolls of any kind, it will probably show (the material is a little thinner) and B) because if they offered an extra small option I would probably need it but it’s not available. For reference I’m 5’5” weigh 108 lbs and my dimensions are 32-24.5-35.5. I wear a small or extra small in most clothing. I often have a hard time with pants because despite not being super tall all of my height is in my legs. So the typical 28-29 inch inseam provided by most woman’s pants is just too short. (I look for 32 inseams whenever I can find them!) But these fit great! I’d prefer they be a little bit snugger around my butt and upper thighs but all things considered these pants are a win for sure. I would say pay close attention to the photo provided by the manufacturer of the pants listing the dimensions. They seem to be pretty accurate. Seriously, give these pants a shot. Especially if you’re someone who has a hard time finding them long enough. They aren’t the longest pants I own BUT they are longer than average for sure. And they are very stretchy. I can see now why people reverence being able to fit into smaller sizes quite comfortably. I’m going to buy a few more pair in different colors :) UPDATE: Love LOVE these pants!! I got them in more colors and they all fit great. They seem to wash well too. So far the colors haven’t bled at all (though I have washed them with like colors) and they have not shrunk. I have not but them in the dryer though. I did see that some of the sizing is inconsistent but nothing significant. Also, the white pants are a bit see through so be prepared to purchase nude undies for when you wear them. Otherwise I am so super happy with this pants and I can’t stress enough how hard it is to find them that fit right (especially online). TRY THESE!!! You won’t regret it.

Legs are tight The thighs are too tight. I am 5’1” and 130 lbs. I ordered a large which fits the waist and butt, however the legs are so tight they make these ridges and looks terrible. Fabric isn’t as soft as I expected either. Returning it.

Sizing is way off, Quality and color is great I was so bummed, I was hoping to find my new favorite jeggings in multiple colors :-( Material, quality of workmanship and color is really good. I always wear leggings and jeggings in a U.S. large (That has been my go to size for years). I'm 5'3.5 in tall and 157lbs. I have been wearing time and Tru jeggings forever. When I took these out of the bag I was shocked at how small the waist was. I haven't worn anything that looked anywhere near that size for over 25 years! I tried them anyway since I heard they were super stretchy and couldn't do them up, massive muffin top! Unlike my Time and Tru's which are very comfy. They also are not high waisted at all. I've attached pics of my standard Time and Tru jeggings and these. There is a 5 inch difference! (There was no way I was going to post a pic with my belly hanging out). I might re-order in a couple of sizes up, because everything apart from the sizing was great. If you have a very small waist for a large (vs. a belly like me), a large 'might' work for you, but I'd still consider sizing up by at least 1 size.

Seriously, my New FAVORITE pair of jeans I am 5'3 and about 110 lbs, I purchased a size Medium: my legs are long and I guess for a short person my thighs and calves are bigger than the normal 5'3 person.... I'm just un proportionate... But these jeans are long enough to not look sloppy, look super cute with booties and not short enough to look like flood water pants (like the rest of my jeans). They don't ( or at lest not yet) get loose around the knees, and they have pockets! FRONT AND BACK POCKETS! which is nice b/c then my bootie doesn't look as flat as it is, and a front pocket is nice to have. I love these jeans in the fact that they are causal enough to wear to work/church and casual enough to wear to my daughters soccer practice without looking like I'm wearing leggings. I live in these jeans! Usually when i find a pair of pants, they will fit my waist and be too short or too long, or they fit my legs, but not my waist, or they are long enough and fit my waist but are too tight on my thighs... (un proportionate) These jeans are super stretchy and so comfortable that when I get home I am not dying to get out of them. They hug and fit in all the right places. I only gave 4 stars b/c the zipper doesn't like to stay up. Other than that, I want every color, I purchased black, wine and charcoal and cant decided which color is my favorite.

Amazing jeans but a little short I have been looking for colored jeans for work that are comfortable and stretchy. I'm currently in ED recovery so I'm slowly gaining weight and my sizes are all over. I LOVE that these are similar to real jeans instead of legging material but have a lot of stretch to them like leggings do. The color is great as pictured! The only downside is that they are short in length. I am 5'3, 240ish pounds and ordered a 1x. They stop right above my ankle which is a lot shorter than I normally pick but I rolled them into capris and I'm sure they will look great with fall boots! Another downside is that they are thin. I have a tattoo on my butt that if I am not wearing covering underwear can be seen a little through the material. But that's not enough to keep me from loving them! Definitely buying more colors!

Great Quality I needed these last minute for a themed dance and my daughter is between sizes as she is too tall for girls sizes without them being too large in other areas and too short for a lot of women’s and junior sizes. Her normal size is 00 or 0, she is 5’1” and 80 pounds. These are like really soft stretchy jeans, she loves the way they feel and although they are a little loose in some areas, they fit perfectly in the waist with room to grow and the length is perfect. I did wash and dry them before wearing and they did not shrink at all, nor did the color fade. Very nice pants, I would recommend!
