Pretty great, maybe size up I really like these pants and man are they stretchy. I’m 5’9’ and about 150 lbs. (I’m usually an 8/10 in pants) I originally bought a large, but since I’m pear-shaped (W: 27.5”, H: 42.5”) they were tighter in the legs than I was wanting, although the waist fit perfectly. So I ordered an x-large and that was overall a better fit. I have to wear a belt to avoid slippage in the waist (story of my life) but the legs are not quite as a snug. (Except the calves…they are still really tight. Maybe I have big calves?) So I guess my advise would be to order a size or two up if you don’t want your pants to feel/look painted on. They seem pretty good quality. The material is a bit strange, but the color is a beautiful maroon. I’m pretty sure I’ll order another color sometime soon.

I had to order these 3 times to get my correct size. First I bought large since another review said they were very stretchy I decided to look at them as leggings and L is my size. These were extremely small. So going by that size I sized up to 3XL short. These where extremely big. Tried one last time and did a 1XL short. These fit. But I could probably do an XL short. I did not like that they are a bit wider at the ankle than I would like. I’m hoping they fit a bit better after I wash them and some shrinkage. I’m also hoping they don’t fade in the wash. I will update after I have worn them. But they are very comfortable and I’m very happy with the color and stretchy material. Just what I was searching for. Price is awesome too. For reference I’m 5ft 3inch 199lb

I want a pair in every color. Let me start off by saying I hate buying pants. Doesn't matter if it is online or in the store. I can try on 20 pairs of pants in a store and maybe, MAYBE walk out of the store with 1 pair of pants. I have a high waist, little to no eh-hem rear, and thicker thighs. This can make it very difficult to find pants that I love and that fit well. To make matters worse I am a size 7 or 27 which I have found is an increasingly hard pants size to find when you are a frugal shopper. For reference I am 5' 4" and 133 lbs. I came across these and decided to give the size Medium a shot. These pants are all I've ever wanted in pants. So far I have bought two colors: Dusty Rose and Burgundy. The colors are gorgeous. The material feels similar to jeans, but it is much softer. They are super stretchy and they fit like a glove. I really feel beautiful in them. They can be dressed up with a nice work blouse for the office or dressed down with a band t-shirt for a concert. I want to own a pair in every color and toss all my poor fitting pants. I just hope these stay available for long enough for me to buy them when I have funds. Edit: By the way... They have pockets. Real pockets. Deep pockets! Women's pants never have pockets. These are the perfect pants.

i used to love them... updated: 4 months in and you can now see the fibers that some reviewers had mentioned. The stretchy fibers are fraying and mine developed in my butt area. I work a desk job so not sure if its because I sit too much or it just happens from stretching over time. Again, only had them 4 months and they're getting to the point that I can't wear them because I'm afraid they'll be more noticeable as time goes on. I only wash them occasionally so its not from over washing. They were the perfect go to work pants up until now :( the pic shows what I'm talking about.... it may not show up on other colors but certainly does on black pants :( yes thats a close up lol but eventually you wont need to be that close to see the light colored fibers against a black background original review: so far so good! they are a little higher waisted than I'd like but thats just a weird pref that I have. They go all the way to my ankles, aren't too tight anywhere, and so far day 3 have held their figure and stretchiness. Haven't washed them yet so we'll see how they hold up. I'm 5'5 and 126 lbs, athletic build and these do the trick! smaller waist but enough stretch for my butt and calves so I'll be ordering more if they hold up!

The sizes have changed- they’re now smaller :( But these are still acceptable I have two pairs of these pants in black and they’re amazing. They’re a cross between jeans and jeggings. The problem is that they have a lot of elastic fibers woven in which makes for a really comfortable wear but over time and washing the elastic fibers break through the fabric and you’ll see little white strings all over your pant legs. BUT they’re so comfortable that I’m willing to overlook it and just buy more when that happens (hence the four stars). My main problem is that after purchasing two of these in black, I purchased the gray plaid pair and they fit completely different than the black pair! I’m 5’7’’ and 203lbs (16) and the XL was more than roomy for me (maybe even too roomy after a full day of wear). So when I ordered the gray plaid XL you can imagine I was more than surprised when I couldn’t even get them over my butt! (even lost a couple lbs recently, so that’s not the culprit). So beware that not all colors fit the same (for some reason?) Update: I took another star off, because I bought another black XL pair this week and it fits the same as the gray plaid pair. BE ADVISED: I’m assuming this company has changed the sizing for their pants (without indicating so)- size up!

Great pants, size is forgiving, order your true size. I was looking for some comfy pants to wear during spring and summer. I’m in love with these pants, like the rest of the reviewers. I’m two months post partum, 5’9 and 220lbs. While the reviews said there was lots of stretch, I was hesitant to order my true size (XL). I ordered a 2x and it was too big. I can take them off without unbuttoning the pants! I ordered a white in 1x and fushia in xl. The xl definitely fits and is super comfortable, but as the pictures show, there is more bunching in the legs so I think the 1xl fits a little better out of the dried but the xl is better at the end of the day because it doesn’t stretch out as much. I washed the pants according to the directions and I did not notice shrinking. My pink pants did not bleed either. The white pants are see through so wear a long shirt and light underwear. Can’t go wrong with this price or these colors!

The xl definitely fits and is super comfortable, but as the pictures show, there is more bunching in the legs so I think the 1xl fits a little better out of the dried but the xl is better at the end of the day because it doesn’t stretch out as much. I washed the pants according to the directions and I did not notice shrinking. My pink pants did not bleed either. The white pants are see through so wear a long shirt and light underwear. Can’t go wrong with this price or these colors!

Improved I bought these a year ago in a 2 short, which is supposed to be a 29” inseam. On that first pair I bought, the inseam is only 27”. Although they were a little too short I liked them because the pockets are large and the pants are form fitting yet items don’t bulge when inside them, they’re like, magic pockets - perfect for work. After a year the button popped off. Considered sewing it back on, but figured I needed a new pair anyway as they’ve been through a lot in the last year. Got another 2 short (a regular says 32” inseam which is way too long for me) ideally I’d like 29”, which is what is advertised. Got my new pair, and the inseam is 28”. Still an inch short of what they claim it is, but it is an inch longer than the pair I got last year so that’s an improvement. The material changed. My pair from last year, the fabric is thicker. I can feel more cotton in the pants, they are heavier, thicker when you pinch them. New pants feel like more nylon than cotton, thinner to the touch, lighter. The old ones feel like they were probably better quality however I actually prefer how they are now. I work in the hospitality industry and spend a lot of time running around a restaurant/kitchen which gets hot, and the thinner material will be more cool to work in I think. They still feel nice, just lost some weight. Great for work!

Not consistent with length. The white pull in are 32” inseam but too short so ordered the 34” black.. way more than 2” difference. The black are made with better material than the white. I’ll hem them. They fit well… true to size since they are women’s sizes.

Exactly What I Wanted I bought these to wear for boarding day on an upcoming cruise to match some custom shirts I'm making. The first thing I was attracted to was the rich color. It is exactly as I'd hoped and true to the description. The color is vibrant and these are well made. The fabric is thick so I wouldn't advise wearing in warm weather. I sized down per the reviews and it worked for me. They are are a tad bit long on me even though I ordered short but I am only 4'11. I love how these hug me and the stretch is good enough. These look high end and I appreciate the tailoring. There are small things that stand out for me from other brands I sometimes come across like the fact that threads and buttons match the pants. These seem like they will last a while in my wardrobe. I totally recommend.

Comfy and long enough for tall women I am 5'9", 170 lbs and normally wear a 33 inch inseam. I ordered the 6 tall and they fit great. I am wearing a 2 inch heel in the photo. The most flattering and comfortable dress pants! When I first opened the package I was afraid that the pants might be too tight, too short, or too baggy since they leg of the pants looked really big. I tried them on to see how it would look and holy shit! The waist is stretchy and it hugs my waist and compliments all my curves. For reference my waist is 25”-26” and usually a size 2 so I got the size 2. I’m 5’4” so I got the 29” inseam. The inseam length is perfect so that it perfectly hands between my heel and ankle, enough for my liking. Although they’re supposed to be bootcut, they weren’t baggy at all and actually looks really flattering. If you’re looking for dress pants that are not only flattering and comfortable but also stretchy and will compliment all your curves, don’t hesitate! This is your sign to get them!

Where do I start. I have never purchased pants online but these popped up on a search and I must say they are amazing. The material is soft, a nylon/spandex blend. They have VERY good stretch including the waist band that also has belt loops. For those wanting specifics, they feel like a cross between scuba and legging material. I will be purchasing other colors expeditiously. Height 5' 7 weight 199, purchased size 12 length 32 so great for heels. I would order your true size and just keep in mind your body type when looking at references on sizes because all are not shaped the same but have the same measurements.

Excellent work/dress pants BUT only two pockets, rear ones are faux iChosy makes a high quality dress pant. I need this type of pant for work. I like a slimmer leg and these are a really good fit, they are a modified bootcut just as they claim. The material is a good sturdy blend, feels very supportive without being too binding because of the stretch. They are a little too high rise for me (12”rise) but are still comfortable enough. I usually need a 33” inseam and these are 32”, but somehow they are long enough for me. Another thing I like is the button closure, I hate those slide hook types that are pretty common in dress pants. Unfortunately, there are only two functional pockets, not four as stated in the description...the back two are faux pockets. For work I need four functional pockets so I won’t be able to use these for work. I took off a star in the rating because of that. Here are the actual measurements of the size 10: Waist 29” Hips 38” Rise 12” Inseam 32”

So comfy These are great pants. They are super comfortable. The fabric is a decent, mid weight cotton/nylon blend. The front pockets are real and I can stick my hands in almost entirely. Faux back slit pockets. They look like dress pants but feel like yoga pants due to the stretch. They come up quite high on the waist so they are great for hiding a mom belly. Notes about sizing. I tried size 6, 32. Apparently, 32 is the length for regular unless I was accidentally sent long. The overall fit was comfortable but they will need to be hemmed a bit, even for heels. For reference. I am 5'2, 28" waist, and approximately 128 lbs. I wish I had ordered a short for convenience but I have longish legs despite my overall height and sometimes short is too short on me. Not a big deal but if you normally wear short sizing then short will probably work well for you.

iChosy Women's Straight Leg Dress Work Ankle Pants Business Office Casual iChosey Women's Straight Leg Dress/Work Pant I chose was Navy Blue in size 6. The material content is 53% Cotton, 39% Nylon and 8% Spandex. It is supposed to be an ankle pant but being I am 5' 4" and 122 lbs the pants come longer on me. I would say that this is a pair of pants just right for work and for women that need a pair of pants that "hold" back some extra weight and/or bulges. Functional pockets are on each side of the hip. I personally like a pant whether for work or dress to have a natural flow to it and there is no flow that graces ones shape with this pair of slacks. Thank you.

Thick and stretchy. Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 but ordered the size 6 from iChosy *based on past purchases from the brand. I’m pleased with the fit. The waist has some stretch but doesn’t did into me while seated at my desk. The length is pretty much full on my shorter legs. After wearing for 8 hours, the fabric did loosen so I think my normal size would've held my shape better by the end of the day. *Past experience with iChosy: I ordered the high waist wide straight legs in size 4 short and loved them. The length was just right for me to be able to wear with flats. The waist was a little loose, which I prefer to skintight. I promptly ordered 2 more styles-Bootcut and Barely bootcut-in the same size 4 short. Both of them were much tighter, uncomfortably so, and I'm not sure if it just the brand inconsistency or the different styles of pant. I will end up donating them due to the sizing issue though, both of those fit more like a 2. So I decided to order a size up this time…. Description: I keep ordering iChosy brand work pants because the fabric is thicker and has a lot of stretch. Anyone who works in a business dress setting can appreciate having a work pant that looks professional but feels ready to lounge. My other pairs have held up well in the wash and delicate dry cycles. Another great feature is the functional and deep hip pockets that will actually hold a phone. The waist is higher than my navel. The only negative is the sizing issue based on my 4 pairs. Definitely recommend trying but maybe order your usual size and 1 up to compare.

The waist is tight, but not the rest of the part of the pants. Overall it is a good pants. Maybe the cut is just not for short curvy people.

I think these deserve more than five stars. Slam dunk. These are some seriously GREAT pants. They are made of cotton, spandex, and nylon. This gives them a great weight and a fabric that doesn't wrinkle easily. They are stretchy including the waistband so they are comfortable. I got 8 which is my normal size. If you are between two sizes, get the bigger one. You have the option of choosing short length or regular length. I chose regular. I am 5'4" and this is just right. It would even be floor-length for someone a few inches taller. The pants are somewhat formal so I am curious to know how the shorter version would look, it is a personal preference. These pants will be your go-to due to how easy they are to wear. AND they are washable!!

Nice quality dress pants These are very well made from a heavier fabric with a lot of stretch. Heavy elastic in the waist band to help keep the shape without looking like a stretchy band. I love that they have a short length choice. I am very pleased with my new favorite pants. I am 5’1” and 135 lbs. ordered Size 8 short for a loose comfortable fit.

Expensive looking material!! I usually don’t write reviews, but these pants are incredible! I’m 5’9, 145 lbs, 26.5 in waist measurement and I got a size 2 with 32” inseam. They are perfectly long enough, fit nice and snug in the waist and are loose through the thigh/leg. I will absolutely be buying another pair!

Size 0 inseam not as long as advertised It’s hard to find size 0/XS work slacks with a 32”+ inseam. These were advertised as having a 32” inseam even at that size. I measured them after I got them after thinking they seemed short and they are in fact 31”. I love everything else about these pants, but I wish they were as long as they claimed to be. I wanted to buy them in other colors, but I’m scared they are all going to be shorter than 32” too. :( Update: after washing them in cold water and drying them on the lowest heat setting, they still shrunk an inch and a half and are now like 29.5” and too short to wear at all. :-/ super disappointing

Good Fit in Waist, Made for a Tall Person The gray color of the pants is very nice. The weight is surprising - the material is heavier than I expected. The waist fit is accurate to my size but the length is made for a tall woman (5'8" and above). At 5'7", I can only wear these slacks with very high heels.These pants are not designed to flatter one's figure. They lay straight down without any contour or styling. The sewing craftmanship is good. Overall I would give the quality an 8 out of 10 and the fashion design a 6 out of 10.

High quaity material and craftsmanship but VERY long! These were just a bit wrinkled when they came from the packaging, but right away, I felt they were really high-quality fabric. They are thicker pants with sturdy stitching, and they ironed out very easily with a quick steam/poly setting on my iron. After that, they did not wrinkle even when I grabbed them and balled them up, so that's great news. They will travel well, and when I arrive at my meetings, I won't be a wrinkly mess. The main issue is they are VERY long. I am 5'7", and these have at least two extra inches that will need to be hemmed up. I would say though, the quality of these pants is so good that I am willing to hem them rather than return them. As far as the other sizing - I wear a 16 and they were perfect. They don't ride up in the back and they are nice and flat in the front. Once hemmed, these will be my go-to pants for meetings and travel.

Finally, cotton blend work pants! Materials/Design/Construction - I don't like wearing work/office pants in general, just constricting and uncomfortable. I also hate polyester which most work pants seem to be. Saw these in gray, noticed the cotton blend, and the flat waistband, no crinkled bunchy elastic. AND, there is stretch in the waist even though it is flat, proving it is possible to create a flexible waistband without looking frumpy. Straight leg, sleek, not too wide. Material feels so much nicer than stuffy polyester. No complaints with construction. Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6, long torso. Amazon sells globally so I never just order my typical U.S. size, but I couldn't find a size chart so used my general Amazon store rule which is, go 1 size up from U.S size. Ordered size 8 (regular, not short) and like the fit; a little roomy in the crotch and thighs but I like how relaxed the waist feels so wouldn't go down. Some reviewers said pants were too long but I'd actually appreciate another inch or two, so consider your own height, shoes and preferences. Photos taken right out of package, the little wrinkles on the fly could easily be steamed or ironed out, otherwise rather wrinkle-free which is impressive.

Extremely Comfortable & Pretty Pants I was really pleasantly surprised when I received these pants and put them on. They are very flattering and they are extremely well made. The sewing is excellent and the way they’ve put the pants together is quality. I am 5’2” and I ordered size 10 short and they are the perfect length for me. I usually wear a size 8 in stretchy pants, but I wanted to be sure that I’m going to be comfortable and that was a good decision. They fit perfectly and the cut is very good. The black color is very nice and attractive. I’m planning to purchase additional colors of these same pants. Zipper quality is smooth and well sewn too. Comfortable and great for taller ladies! These are surprisingly awesome pants for the price. The material is thick and has a lovely drape. They are soft and stretchy, and they don't feel at all like you're wearing slacks. I'm 5'10" and I rarely order pants online because everything is too short, but I took the risk because I saw reviewers saying they were long, and I'm happy to that they fit my 34" inseam perfectly (they just brush the tops of my feet)! The pants are very flattering and I feel good in them. I was nervous about size so I ordered up to a 16 because there wasn't a size chart when I ordered, and they're definitely a size too large for me in the waist , so I'd say they fit true to US sizes. I will have the waist taken in, which is easy to do, but I like the fit on my hips and thighs--they feel more modern than the skinny fit of all the pants from 10 years ago. I would definitely order these again. They wash well--I use cold, delicate--and I just hang them to dry. They don't even require ironing. They feel and look a lot like slacks I have from Banana Republic that I paid $98 for, so they are an absolute steal IMO. I will likely order a couple of pairs in other colors (and I'll order the size 14). For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape.

Great material but waist band runs small and doesn’t have much stretch I bought these in a size 6. Size 6 women’s at stores like JCPenny normally are my ideal size. The waist on these is absolutely Too Snug and tight , it is very uncomfortable. Normally I’m either a size S or M depending on the brand. These pants are 14 inches across the band when I measure them with tape (per see the picture attached), which seems like it could fit especially with these being advertised as stretch, I don’t think the waist is stretchy at all for this type of pants, I would probably have to size up to an 8 but I’m not sure I will be as these I am going to return due to the band being non stretchy imo. For reference right now I’m weighing around 130 lbs , my height is 5’1.5 inches my waist measures approximately 28-29 inches and my hips are 40.5 inches, athletic build.The material is smooth and pretty thick and feel comfortable on the skin as the fabric is 53% cotton 39% nylon 8% spandex , so material feels professional and soft. Front pockets are real and back pockets are fake which I think looks good because it doesn’t look bulky on the backside but smooth and sleek on the bum. Overall these pants could be great , and the inseam options are very good for different heights, but with 8% spandex I would expect way more stretch and these don’t have that. I hope this helps someone as I have a hard time buying clothing. On my experience stretch pants usually can accommodate 8-10 lbs loss or gain weight and these just aren’t forgiving and won’t stretch to your size with weight fluctuations.

Seriously good pants! I am ALWAYS afraid to order pants that I can't try on. I went by the size chart and ordered a 12 (For reference I'm 5'8 and my measurements are 41-31-42). Typically I wear an 8 or 10, but I wanted to be safe. I definitely could have ordered a size down. BUT I still seriously LOVE these pants. The material is thick, but sooooo stretchy! And they are truly high waisted, which I love. (No having to pull them up when I sit down so my kangaroo pouch isn't squeezed in half) There is a little bit of "puddling" at the ankle, but I'll usually be wearing boots with these so I don't care. And there's pockets! Good ones that I can actually put my hands all the way in! (The back pockets are not real pockets, but who cares when you have real front ones!) And although I haven't tested them to see if they are 100000% opaque, I'm pretty sure they're going to pass a lot better than some others I've tried. 1000/10 recommend.

Material that moves with you I ordered these black stretchy work pants in 14short since I'm 5'4" so I tend to need pants on the shorter side. Even though I ordered the short version, I still find I have to roll the cuff of the pants under once to give me a clean look, otherwise the pants look baggy on me- I included a photo with one pant leg cuffed and one pant leg as is. My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

Fantastic Pants!!! I LOVE these pants so much that I bought three different patterns. I wound up in the hospital from pneumonia and woke up (after ARDS and a coma) with Critical Care Myopathy, unable to move. Plus we were way out of town and I needed pants (and tees but that's another review) for physical therapy ASAP, so when I saw these and how lovely the prints were, we went out on a limb and ordered them. They arrived super fast (I was in the hospital for two months total, so a couple days was perfect), fit beautifully, and they gave me the boost I needed to get moving again. And oh, I got SO many compliments. Literally my new favorite pairs ever, glad I bought more than one pair. Just for size reference, I'm 5"5 and in the pictures I weigh around 110 give or take I think. I'm now around 115 and the medium still fits exactly like I wanted it to--loose and baggy, but not ridiculously so. I had to relearn how to walk again these, so when I tell you they're comfortable, I'm not kidding, lol!! Word to the wise: Put a knot at the end of each tie, or your drawstring will come out in the wash. Save yourself the headache and do it ahead of time.

Overall great, just a few minor complaints These came out great, they feel heavy duty and the definitely do the job, they are pretty hot to be in during hot weather but that's to be expected from pants like these, I took these out for a 2 day hike and they held up great, I felt like I could abuse these as much as I liked and they would still hold up. There are a few specific aspects of the pants that I will go over: 1. The knee pads: Trying to insert the knee pads will cause you to re-evaluate your entire life but once you understand the technique it's not a big deal, other than that they function great and really protect your knee when kneeling, only complaint is that they are very hard and inflexible so they can get uncomfortable when sitting. 2. The size: I measured my waist before ordering and ordered a 34" waist size, but when I tried the pants on, they were too loose on my waist even after tightening the waist adjustment mechanism, so I was forced to use a belt (which I was planning on using anyway so it wasn't that bad). 3. Overall comfort when walking: While walking in these pants after a while I noticed that the extra reinforcement material on the crotch area in the back of the pants would fold into itself and would bother me so I had to readjust it every once in a while, also the material of the pants is not very stretchy but I didn't find it bothering.

Love the pants, but apricot color is very unforgiving I ordered these joggers in the color "apricot" in an attempt to diversify my wardrobe,but I'm so sad I didn't go for one of the darker colors. In this light color, these joggers are pretty see-through, and seem to accentuate every nook, groove, or bump - making it so I don't think there is a pair of underwear that would work and it seems as though you can see my cellulite/all the bumps of my legs. With that said, these are not going to work for me. However - I suspect these are a fantastic pair of pants if ordered in another color that is a bit more forgiving. When trying them on, they were buttery soft - like I didn't want to take them off despite hating how they looked on me. They also fit fairly well - I used the size chart provided and ended up ordering an XL and at 5'2" I did the 30" inseam (love that they've got inseam options). The waist went a bit higher than I expected, but with wearing it high waist it was still comfortable and looked like it fit well. The pockets were a good size, and I love the zipper pocket option as well. Overall, I'd say avoid the apricot color but get these pants!

These are nice leggings, they are very thick but comfortable to wear. They run true to size and offer a mild amount of compression. They were easy to pull on and this was my first pair of "scrunch" leggings in the rear area and I was pleasantly surprised how flattering it was however I was unable to pull them up in the back as high as I prefer due to the cut. The waist is a thick band that didn't dig in or roll down on me. I wouldn't consider these "gym/working out leggings" due to their thickness, but they are great for casual wear, errands, or lounging around. I'm 5 feet tall and these were long on me as most standard leggings are, but this wasn't a big issue for me.

Feels like nothing on Update: I bought these on Nov 28. It is now Dec 24, so not even a month in and already the material is all messed up, I've added a picture to this review. These were worn maybe a total of 5 times, washed once and I don't want to wear them again as they don't look good anymore. On the plus side, it's still early enough to return them so return them I will. Original 4 star review: I've bought several of these over the years from different vendors. The first being ultra comfy in a green then a black pair that was disappointing because it was kinda shiny. I took some time before committing to another pair after the disappointing black shiny pair went out of commission (the first green one with the leafs on it is still in great shape even though it's a year older and is used more - maybe the addition of cotton is to thank for that? But the cotton blend is double the price). This black one here is somewhere in between the two I already own. It's not that shiny weird material but also not the comfy soft cotton feeling of my first pair. They are comfortable though, very much so. Feels like I'm wearing nothing, they are that soft. Flattering too, and thin which is what I was looking for. If I lived in a place with winter and snow I would never wear these outside, but in Florida they are perfect for 10 out of 12 months. One small complaint, they are slightly too short on my quite average 5'6 height, in M size. Since they are just lounge pants I don't care as much but I would be a little peeved if I planned on wearing these out. Overall, for the price, I am very pleased with the purchase.

Agree with all the tall girls . . LOVE them As another reviewer said, finally sweats that are long enough and don't come from the men's department. They are plenty long and don't have extra room for a package I don't have. I am 5'11", 170 lbs. with an athletic build. I got an XL, with the 34 in. inseam. I meant to order the 36 in. inseam as much of my height comes from my legs but as you can see these are plenty long. The pictures were taken after the sweats had been washed and dried. Although the tag says not to put in the dryer, I did and they came out fine. I tend to wear my pants down on my hips a little probably from a lifetime of not having pants otherwise long enough and you can see that the drawstring remains in the original position so they fit very comfortably around the waist. I didn't know what to expect with all of the reviews having contradictory information on sizing. They are super soft and kept me warm walking outdoors in freezing temps (that's not hyperbole the temperatures were below freezing and my legs didn't even get chilled after walking nearly a mile). My dog who oversaw the making of this review thinks they look really comfortable and will be great for many long walks.
color_display: baby polar fleece, dark heather grey

user_comment: SIZE UP I have such strong mixed feelings about these pants. I'm 5'8", about 150 pounds, pear shaped, and all legs. I wear size 6 pants and I'm almost always a very solid medium. I work at an outdoor school where the temperatures are in the 20-40s(F) for months. I don't need to win any fashion awards, my main goal is warmth and comfort. I LOVE that these pants come in multiple lengths because that's incredibly hard to find for sweatpants and my legs are long and I need my ankles covered because it's cold. I took the advice of everyone and sized up and bought a large in 34in the first time (black pants in pictures). The waistband, even sitting on my natural waist, is still incredibly tight. You can see in the pictures where it's making my tummy squish out the top. I love the way the rest of the pant fits and the length. I decided to order a second pair, this time an XL in the same 34in length (grey pants in pictures). This waistband fits a lot more comfortably and I can wear it lower on my hips which I find more comfortable, but they are so baggy everywhere else. They are also at least 2 inches longer than the black pair labeled the same length, which I find weird. Another thing I'm not a fan of is the line across the top of the butt. I'm not sure its purpose because it's thick, unflattering, and I can feel it through the pants if I'm not wearing a base layer. Despite the flaws, they are thick and warm so I'm keeping both.
color_display: baby polar fleece, light heather grey

user_comment: Serious Problems With Sizing I ordered these pants (Small, 28 inseam) and they fit perfectly. See the pair on the left. Amazing fit and nice quality I was skeptical buying pants for work online without trying them on and read tons of reviews first before buying. I am definitely going to check out the other colors. I love the chunky waistband, it gives some personality without being over the top as I don’t ever wear belts. Overall very happy and impressed for the price.

Cute swimsuit cover-up I tried the flowy, split leg, Palazzo pant in size medium. Not sure yet if I will keep the two pairs I purchased (sky blue and white) but I am for sure returning the white because it is too see-through. For taller girls above 5'8", this will be a capri length for you. Definitely not an ankle or maxi length. Totally comfortable, but not having pockets on the shorts for phone or keys is a huge swing and a miss for me. The tiny pocket at the back of the waistband is not sufficient for much more than a credit card or keys. You might be able to really squeeze a phone in there, but it's gonna stick out. This is a garment I would not feel comfortable in for a day of shopping or to go to a nice restaurant, Only because it shows so much leg, I might as well be running around in booty shorts or a tennis skirt. But it's suitable for poolside, beach, outings, cruise that kind of situation or lounging at home. Material is fairly see-through and is like a stretchy rayon. The drape is lovely. You'll need to steam it when you purchase it to get the wrinkles out though.

LOL I'm size 14/16 XL/1X 5' 5.5" 205lb and suggest following the size chart. Pretty accurate. Pick the larger size if between sizes but not necessarily a size up product. They just grazed my ankles. Love the built-in-shorts so I never had to worry about a wardrobe malfunction.

Run big I'm 5'6 150 lbs , the mediums are looser than I expected. They fit ; but slightly loose/baggy in the knees and groin. I definitely want a snug /tight fit; so I will be ordering a size small . I'm keeping the mediums for my " fat days " - lol !The material is not see through, which is great. They seem like they will not collect lint , and probably not likely to pill or collect sweater balls unless washed with towels etc- will maybe collect on the seams if so. I wear black stretch pants almost daily. These are definitely comfortable , they are high rise to my natural waist , are breathable yet thick enough to not be considered thin. The pockets are great perfect to carry my phone or small items in a pinch. I think the smalls will be more smoothing + snug for me . My hips/butt are 42" and my natural waist is 32" . My butt fills out the pants , my waist fits perfectly, my thighs fit , legs could be more snug... And the pants are loose at my knees - and are loose at the groin . If you have bigger thighs + hips , and knees than me- you will fill the looser areas out . The pants are definitely stretchy and long enough they reach my ankles .

Great material, stitching, fit and love the dual leg pockets! 💙 I purchased my regular size L. I’m 5’4” athletic build, 160 very muscular legs, these fit very comfortable, like a glove but not compressed. I treadmill, hike, do weights, push ups, etc. They stay put, minimal adjustments needed. So great I love these leggings. The color is bright and very warm red. They are so soft! The correct word for them is "buttery". They fit quite tightly, so you can see the underwear lines. For me it's usually the issue with the gym clothes. I would say they are true to the size, just use the table. I am 138lbs and 5'5", the size of the leggings on the picture is M. With this size on me they fit well, but are not too tight. I have another pair in peacock blue (amazing bright color!!!), and the underwear issue is less prominent with them.

A pleasantly surprised tall girl. First things I must note: I'm 6' 2" with a 37" inseam, 29 1/2in waist, 42" hips and I ordered a dark gray medium. Not going to lie, I opened up the package and thought I might give these to my sister who's 5' 9" since I ordered the black ones as a gift for her, but I decided to try them on thinking, maybe they'll stretch or they'll just be nice cropped yoga pants. Lo and behold they did indeed stretch out! (Thank goodness.) Still a tad short but they turned out much longer than they initially appeared (holding them up they come to about my mid shin). They're also pretty high in the waist for pants not specifically designed for tall women. I did still have to hike them up a bit and, once I did, they stayed in place pretty well if I was just walking around but they definitely wiggled their way down as I did some light test stretching up and down (see progression of pictures). That might just be how yoga pants are but as someone who doesn't typically wear crop tops but is exploring the idea, this is something I pay attention to. Fabric-wise, they are super soft and feel like they're snug enough to give you that smooth, flattering, firm, shark skin feel. There is a seam at the very top around the waist that makes them feel like they're digging in a bit more there (see last picture) but it doesn't make them uncomfortable, just maybe slightly unflattering if they slide down and you don't have a completely fatless stomach like me. I also decided to try on the black ones (just in case, you know) and they fit very similarly but I'd say a touch longer in the legs and a touch looser in the top waist seam. The black fabric is also very soft but also slightly less velvety soft, but not by much. I am a very tactile person and I tend to buy/keep clothes purely on their softness so I notice these things. Overall, I am very pleased with these yoga pants and plan to purchase at the very least a pair in black for myself, if not other colors.

Overall, I am very pleased with these yoga pants and plan to purchase at the very least a pair in black for myself, if not other colors.

A pleasantly surprised tall girl. First things I must note: I'm 6' 2" with a 37" inseam, 29 1/2in waist, 42" hips and I ordered a dark gray medium. Not going to lie, I opened up the package and thought I might give these to my sister who's 5' 9" since I ordered the black ones as a gift for her, but I decided to try them on thinking, maybe they'll stretch or they'll just be nice cropped yoga pants. Lo and behold they did indeed stretch out! (Thank goodness.) Still a tad short but they turned out much longer than they initially appeared (holding them up they come to about my mid shin). They're also pretty high in the waist for pants not specifically designed for tall women. I did still have to hike them up a bit and, once I did, they stayed in place pretty well if I was just walking around but they definitely wiggled their way down as I did some light test stretching up and down (see progression of pictures). That might just be how yoga pants are but as someone who doesn't typically wear crop tops but is exploring the idea, this is something I pay attention to. Fabric-wise, they are super soft and feel like they're snug enough to give you that smooth, flattering, firm, shark skin feel. There is a seam at the very top around the waist that makes them feel like they're digging in a bit more there (see last picture) but it doesn't make them uncomfortable, just maybe slightly unflattering if they slide down and you don't have a completely fatless stomach like me. I also decided to try on the black ones (just in case, you know) and they fit very similarly but I'd say a touch longer in the legs and a touch looser in the top waist seam. The black fabric is also very soft but also slightly less velvety soft, but not by much. I am a very tactile person and I tend to buy/keep clothes purely on their softness so I notice these things. Overall, I am very pleased with these yoga pants and plan to purchase at the very least a pair in black for myself, if not other colors.

Mid-Waist, Great Quality Yoga Pant As an 8 months PP, stay at home/work at home mama, I live in yoga pants. The proportions are not good These sweatpants are thick, warm, have pockets, and the elastic around the ankles which I prefer. My biggest issue with these are the rise; it is ridiculously high. I can’t even recommend them for someone tall because the rise would still be too higher, and the legs would be too short. If you like rolling the waistband on your sweatpants then you will like these. Personally, I don’t like rolling mine, and in order for these to fit decently, I need to roll the waistband down twice which then cuts off access to the pockets. I don’t know why I didn’t look harder for well fitting sweatpants. I spent over a 100 dollars on these and it’s too late to return them. I’m one of the photos I’ve attached, I folded the pants down from the crotch area to show that the rise of the pants is almost as long as the inseam which is comical and crazy. I ended up finding perfectly fitting sweatpants in the children’s section at Target for 9 dollars each on sale. For reference, I am 5’3, 110lbs and I bought smalls. At Target, the XLs (14) in the girls section fit me perfectly. They’re on the fitted side without being tight.

Not exactly cute but they're warm and comfy I'm 5'4" and 200 lbs. I have a 38" waist and a 48" hip, so I ordered a XXL. These pants fit fine but they're not oversized and I probably would have preferred an XXXL if they had it. Despite "fitting" these sweats have creases and folds in the crotch that are super unflattering. I feel like the crotch is just not quite shaped right for my body--like I needed a little less material in the front and more in the butt. Regardless, I got these for walking the dog in the cold, dark mornings so I'm not too worried about how they look. They are thick and warm which is what I was after. The inside was so soft and fluffy when they arrived but after the first wash that went away and they feel like normal sweats on the inside. They produced a TON of lint so I recommend washing them separately for the first time. I think they'll be perfect for what I need so I'm giving them 4 stars but there is definitely room for improvement.

Cozy - but NOT gray! These joggers are comfy and cozy. The material is heavy, with a soft fleecy texture on the inside. Thick and cozy! I got these pants from the IUUI store in size XX-Large, although I normally wear X-Large. The fit is great, and I am glad I sized up. For reference, my height is 5 feet and 10 inches, and my weight is 225 pounds. The material is soft with a thick lining. It is a blend of cotton, polyester and spandex: 65%, 25% and 10% respectively. They washed well in the washing machine in cold water, normal cycle, even when using the dryer. The pictures show the pants after washing. I am pleased with these pants, and I would recommend them!

Cute comfy warm sweats I love my nylon cargo pants & got these cargo sweats for the winter. I was worried ‘wide leg’ meant bell bottoms or flared but these are thankfully more straight legged bcz flare or wide leg makes me look fatter & shorter (5’3”/150-55) . Got the black in medium and it’s a comfy & roomy fit. They’re a little long for my height but they may shrink a little bit once washed. If not, then I can do a quick hem. I have to hem a lot of my pants so it’s no big deal.These have upper, flat angled pockets and lower cargo side pockets. There’s an elastic waist and a drawstring tie as well. They have a very roomy and comfortable fit & look a little dressier than plain sweats too.The outside fabric is smooth sweatshirt material and the inside fabric is very plush and fuzzy. Can’t wait for it to get cooler (can’t believe I’m saying that 🤦🏻‍♀️) so I can wear these out & about.

Great pair of pants from fall right into the deep of winter These are a rich cocoa brown, heavy-weight fleece lined... I hesitate to call them sweatpants -- maybe level-above-sweatpants. They're a little more crisp than ordinary sweatpants -- really nice. The waistband is wide and lays flat and the tie is on the inside, so there's no bulk or added visual detail at the waist. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs, wearing a size Large and they are barely long enough for my taste. But these are really, really nice pants; they'd look amazing on someone of average height.

Extremely Comfortable, But Fabric Pilled and Split I bought these pants to take with me on a long vacation. They were so amazingly comfortable that I was about to order a second pair. However, when I took a closer look after wearing the pants for about the 10th time, I had noticed pilling in the area near the crotch on the pant legs, and upon closer examination the fabric was thinning. Maybe if you don't have thigh that rub together when you walk they will be fine, but if you have thick thighs like I do, be warned.5'6", 165 lbs, Size Large - Fit was great (loose and comfortable)

Very nice They are thick linen pants. Not see through. They are high waisted. Deep pockets. I really liked them. I wore them to a fall festival. I’m 5’1 and got a small. I washed them in cold water and dried them in my dryer no problem. They are comfortable. I recommend buying them

Great summer pant I love these pants. I own around 4 pairs of cotton linen pants and the combo is great for summer and humid weather. Linen wicks off moisture better than any other fabric. I should know since I’m a fashion product developer. I’ve studied all textiles and I’m in love with natural fabrics such as linen, cotton, and rayon. I also can’t deal with synthetics like polyester and most clothing on Amazon is polyester crap mass produced and private labeled. The length is perfect for me and most pants are short for me. I’m 137 lbs and 5’ 7”. I have an issue with almost all pants being too short if they are made by cheap Chinese brands and also many cheaper department store brands. I will probably buy more of these in other colors.

Update, Love pants are the business!!! Update: I probably could have kept the medium size as they stretch after wearing. Extremely comfortable, do wrinkle but not as much as 100% linen. I wore mine literally all day, by end of day they look very big on my body.I'm 5'1 145 lbs. Initially ordered a medium they fit just a tad tight, returned them and ordered a Large, perfecto! These are mostly linen, with some other material. It says 100% linen, but when ironing they smooth out very nicely, and when wearing they don't wrinkle as much as 100% linen. I love them. Ordered black, khaki and tan colors. I followed the instructions, wash cool, barely rinse then laid flat. Ironing was effortless. I would not fold over a hanger, instead, will hang by the waist area. Very well made, love the ankle gathering, and decent size front pockets, faux back pockets. I'm very pleased with this purchase.

Wore out in less than 7 months. So disappointed. 😞 I really liked these pants. They fit well and were very comfortable. It is difficult to find clothes that are made of 100% natural fibers, so I was willing to pay to get what I wanted. These pants arrived at my house on October 18th 2020 and on May 4th 2021 the khaki pair ripped on the inner thigh. As you can see from the photographs above, the fabric is threadbare! For $30 a pair, I expected a little more longevity. Extremely disappointed and feeling cheated. Update: Unfortunately I tried one pair and liked them so I ordered a number of others in different colors. On June 13, 2021 the Navy pair ripped.

TO ALL MY TALLER GIRLS Me: 5’10ish 140 lb woman. I’m typically a small-4/6 34 inseam 27 pre pregnancy 33 postpartum I bought a small- before babies Bought medium- postpartum To all my tall girls that feel out of place… MY TALL GIRLS, THESE PANTS COULD BE FOR YOU! These pants can definitely be dressed up or dressed down for a more chic look. They are thin and perfect for spring/summer weather. The material isn’t high end material but it isn’t trash either. The stitching is nice through out and clean. Normal trousers sit lower low but these are true high waist pants. The bottom is cuffed up and its sewed to keep in place(see picture) If you carry your weight in your midsection I can see these highlighting that area for you. Consider wearing shape wear to smooth everything out. I noticed it was less forgiving on my postpartum lower belly but pre-pregnancy I did not have that problem. The inseam seems to be approx 32 but because it’s cuffed and only sewed on 2 opposing sides- it slightly drapes adding a some additional length. If you’re short, these will be way too long for you. Pros: THEY FIT TALLER WOMEN!!!!!! I put these on with a low block heel and also platform sneakers. Classy/chic look can be achieve getting the most usage out of styling these for different occasions. Iron/steam easily Cons: They aren’t wrinkle free and hold a bit of wrinkles if you stay in a position for way too long. It doesn’t wrinkle nearly as bad at linen. 👍

Stylish but casual pants For reference, I’m 5 feet tall and 120 pounds, and I ordered a medium. The waist and legs fit perfectly, but they were extremely long, so I had to cut off 4 inches and hem the bottoms to prevent them from dragging. The ad describes them as "women's casual high waist straight leg pants, stretchy button business dress work pocket trousers." However, these pants are much more casual than the description suggests, and I wouldn’t wear them to work. The pictures make the fabric seem thicker, but in reality, they’re more like thin yoga or exercise pants. Also, the buttons are purely decorative—they don’t actually fasten. You just pull the pants on. That said, the material is very soft, comfortable, and has a nice stretch. For $14.99, I would recommend them, just be aware that they’re quite thin and very casual.

Plus Size > They're okay for around the house I'm being brave enough to post a picture with my shirt tucked in so you can really see the fit. First, the fabric is nice enough, comfortable and fairly soft, but it doesn't lend itself to giving you and shape, support or figure really. Second, l was hoping these wouldn't be too fitted through the knee as I have an issue with fat upper legs in form fitting pants. Not a good look on a plus size gal 😧 These have a bootleg type break from the knee down, which I like better than a tapered pant leg, but they just aren't super flattering above the knee. Third, the pants are a bit long and I'm 5"8. I'd be okay with this but if you're shorter than me you'll have to hem these! All in all, I'd keep these for around the house lounging or house cleaning, but I was looking for something I'd feel a little more comfortable wearing for a walk or running errands. These don't quite make the cut. But I DO appreciate any company that at least tries to give us plus size gals an option.

Good, inexpensive yoga pants Update 2022: the quality of the fabric has dropped significantly. The fabric is see through and only a few weeks later there are holes / runs in the inner thigh area. Good quality for the price. I have two pairs of the Jockey capri-length pants that are a similar style to these pants that I love. I've noticed that the fabric (it is cottony) is starting to wear a bit and the seam up the back feels like it is going to split apart. These aren't that tight, so I wouldn't expect the back seam to fail. I am not sure if it is because I am sitting on things and that is causing the fabric to stretch and wear, but I do not have the same kind of wear on the shorter-length pants. They have what I would consider a high rise. I am giving them an extra star because the length is perfect. I am 5'6" and I cannot stand it when my pants drag on the floor, or when I am trying to practice yoga and I am tripping on pant legs! These would be perfect if they were made out of a different material that did not wear as much!

I've noticed that the fabric (it is cottony) is starting to wear a bit and the seam up the back feels like it is going to split apart. These aren't that tight, so I wouldn't expect the back seam to fail. I am not sure if it is because I am sitting on things and that is causing the fabric to stretch and wear, but I do not have the same kind of wear on the shorter-length pants. They have what I would consider a high rise. I am giving them an extra star because the length is perfect. I am 5'6" and I cannot stand it when my pants drag on the floor, or when I am trying to practice yoga and I am tripping on pant legs! These would be perfect if they were made out of a different material that did not wear as much!

CHEAP!!! I purchased these pants for my boyfriend and he’s 6ft tall so i ordered a XL. I’m not a big person i’m 5ft and weigh 120 but typically order pj pants in a bigger size(typically large or XL). I tried on the pants i got for him to reference what size i would plan to get and they didn’t fit my wait what so ever, which is expected but the strings don’t hep tighten it, not to mention these pants go to my ankle so they aren’t long at ALL!!! If this was the only issue I had I wouldn’t be as upset as I am right now however, they are also extremely see through and the material is god awful. It’s itchy and for it being go thin it makes you uncomfortably hot, and I feel this is a product that might be using cheaper dye based off of everything so far and the print/ color. Shrinkage I thought the pj pants had arrived in good condition and looked really long. I washed them in cold water before wearing and when I pulled them out they had become capris'. I was able to stretch them and then I air dried them but they were a still a little short (I'm 5'11). Was excited to try them out . . they are comfy, light weight, cotton pjs. Unfortunately the next morning I noticed that one of the legs had three holes in them so that was a little disappointing.
Shrinking 1st wash shrunk these pajamas into capri length. I am only 5'4" so that is pretty difficult. I have purchased this brand before but I am extremely disappointed this time around.

So cute and comfortable, sizing runs small in 100% cotton I love these pajama pants. They’re so comfortable. I have another pair from this same company that I have worn into the ground for the past year. I decided to buy a few new pairs from the same company in the same size I’ve been wearing. For reference I am 5’5” 118lbs with 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips and got the size small. I ordered 3 pairs of pajama pants from this company but for some reason these lemon print pants run smaller and is a different material. The lemon print pants are 100% cotton while the other 3 pairs I got are 98% polyester + 2 % spandex. The lemon print cotton pants are much lower rise. Because they are 100 % cotton I have no doubt they are going to shrink considerably. I would highly suggest if you get a cotton pair to size up. The weird thing is the listing didn’t say it was cotton. I thought I was getting the polyester ones which wash and dry great with no shrinkage. So be advised the lemon print pants are all cotton and you should size up. I hope that helps!

Soft, but poor quality There are two issues with this product for me: fit and quality. The 3X has about a 31" inseam, when you lay them flat is about 15" from the crotch to the top of the band, and is loosely 34" in circumference when elastic isn't stretched. It'll be different for everyone's body, but on me they hit just about my belly button, which is just about where I want them. The issue for me is that they aren't secure up there. I've been wearing them for daywear 2-3 times a week since I got them for Christmas 3 weeks ago. I've had to keep hiking them up because they keep slipping down, feeling especially precarious on the sides. One of the big issues causing this is the elastic. It's a relatively thin band, compared to a couple other pairs of fleece pajama pants I own and regularly wear. The elastic is definitely weaker as well, not holding as securely to my body. This wouldn't be quite so much of an issue if the pants hit a little higher on the curve of my stomach, but gravity pulls them right down. Additionally, the tie that is threaded through the waistband is far, far too small. It is long enough to tie into a single knot on me, but not long enough to be a bow or have a long enough tail to feel secure, and the woven fabric on it feels like it will wear through after less than a year of use. When the garment is at rest, the tails only extend 3.5 inches. The rest of the pants are so voluminous though that if I went up a size, I'd have to do some serious tailoring to get them to fit. I would have to hem them quite aggressively (which is understandable when a product doesn't have optional pants length adjustments) but also take in the sides. The real issue with these pants though is the quality. The third day I was wearing them, I noticed that a hole the size of a nickel was developing about 10 inches down the side of the pants, at the seam. The rip is not the seam coming apart, but the fabric itself ripping at the seam. This has never happened on a pair of fleece pajama pants I have owned, including a pair that has the same dimensions as this one, which I have been wearing for 3 years. The fleece fabric they use simply isn't up to the weight of the seam. The pants are quite loose on me there, so it isn't an issue with width of my thigh pulling extra hard on the seam. I would recommend against buying these pants, and instead getting a pair that actually fits securely and doesn't rip apart within 3 days.

The fleece fabric they use simply isn't up to the weight of the seam. The pants are quite loose on me there, so it isn't an issue with width of my thigh pulling extra hard on the seam. I would recommend against buying these pants, and instead getting a pair that actually fits securely and doesn't rip apart within 3 days.

Soft, but poor quality There are two issues with this product for me: fit and quality. The 3X has about a 31" inseam, when you lay them flat is about 15" from the crotch to the top of the band, and is loosely 34" in circumference when elastic isn't stretched. It'll be different for everyone's body, but on me they hit just about my belly button, which is just about where I want them. The issue for me is that they aren't secure up there. I've been wearing them for daywear 2-3 times a week since I got them for Christmas 3 weeks ago. I've had to keep hiking them up because they keep slipping down, feeling especially precarious on the sides. One of the big issues causing this is the elastic. It's a relatively thin band, compared to a couple other pairs of fleece pajama pants I own and regularly wear. The elastic is definitely weaker as well, not holding as securely to my body. This wouldn't be quite so much of an issue if the pants hit a little higher on the curve of my stomach, but gravity pulls them right down. Additionally, the tie that is threaded through the waistband is far, far too small. It is long enough to tie into a single knot on me, but not long enough to be a bow or have a long enough tail to feel secure, and the woven fabric on it feels like it will wear through after less than a year of use. When the garment is at rest, the tails only extend 3.5 inches. The rest of the pants are so voluminous though that if I went up a size, I'd have to do some serious tailoring to get them to fit. I would have to hem them quite aggressively (which is understandable when a product doesn't have optional pants length adjustments) but also take in the sides. The real issue with these pants though is the quality. The third day I was wearing them, I noticed that a hole the size of a nickel was developing about 10 inches down the side of the pants, at the seam. The rip is not the seam coming apart, but the fabric itself ripping at the seam. This has never happened on a pair of fleece pajama pants I have owned, including a pair that has the same dimensions as this one, which I have been wearing for 3 years. The fleece fabric they use simply isn't up to the weight of the seam. The pants are quite loose on me there, so it isn't an issue with width of my thigh pulling extra hard on the seam. I would recommend against buying these pants, and instead getting a pair that actually fits securely and doesn't rip apart within 3 days.

Good purchase I’m 5’6” and 220#. I’m 10 weeks postpartum. I could wear a 2x but I’m happy with the 3x. They don’t put pressure on my stomach but they do rise high and hold me. I love that they hit below the knee but above the ankle. Good buy for the price.

Perfect plus-size workout capris! Fantastic fit! Well made but not too thick. I am 5 ft 8in tall and the capri length is perfect. Higher waist with a wider and so they don't roll down. They stay in place for he entire workout.

generously sized, fits at true size but could go size smaller if need more compression These are quality leggings (I have two pair, about to have three). I haven't had them for long, but I've worn them several times all day long under various circumstances--so far so good. I put them on and forget about them. The product description mentioned a draw string closure. No such thing. It is just a regular pair of (quality) leggings that are pulled on. No need to fuss with anything else. They are real comfortable, not binding anywhere, and they stay up. I am not pulling them up constantly, or making any adjustments. Generous sizing. Go with your size if you dont want too much compression, or try a size smaller if you want more compression. These stretch for days :) I am ordering a size smaller to see how much they differ. I will back when I receive those.(I did this, my update/edit is below) A little about my body: I am 5'10, 300 lbs, and have a belly "apron"- lots of loose skin that makes sizing awkward. I have an hourglass (leaning pear shape)- for the most part my body is in balance --save the for the large flappy skin hanging at my belly. I will include pics of these on me. Because of this belly, no matter my size, I use compression to hold that in place. It is less to do about look, and more to do about feel. The loose flapping skin apron is uncomfortable to walk around with if I am wearing a loose pair of shorts or pants. Also makes it more likely that my shorts or pants will fall off. So I go for compression. Looks better, feels better. I say all this because I had to really consider which size to select based on their size chart. I wear about a 2x, and I think I am in size 22, maybe closer to 20. I went with a 2X which correlates with a size 20, because I remember most of the leggings I have purchased over my life have fit a little large. This remains true here. The size 20/2xl in these leggings provide a fair amount of compression and the overall fit feels great. They have a lot of additional stretch without feeling loose or too big. However, as mentioned earlier, I am ordering a 1X to see how much they differ. These fit a little looser that what I am used to. But they do fit well and are comfy! *******EDIT*****: I purchased the 1XL (blue) and they fit as comfortably as the 2XL (gray) but clearly with a more snug fit, yet doesn't bite in, roll, or smash down my butt too much (but smashes down my belly nicely), AlSO, the color doesn't stretch away to nothing (some colored leggings if they have a white, or light colored interior, that color will come through when the leggings are stretched, which looks awful. These dont do that, and for that I am doubly impressed). I like either. I am now going to buy the purple, and I decided to go with that in the 1XL again. For me the added compression just makes me feel even better. But both sizes work, and I am super pleased with this style from Just My Size. There are pics of the gray pants and the blue pants included here, but they look similar in the pics and may be hard to tell which is which. High rise, waist stays up, leg length hits me about mid calf. Leg stays in place too. 88% Polyester, 12% Spandex I wash my leggings in cool or cold water. Directions say to wash in cold water. Anything with spandex should not be washed in hot water, or dried on hot. Maybe medium setting at most. Heat kills spandex over time. These will not shrink in wash. NO PILLING so far!

These pants are great!! These pants are sooo cute!! I was a a bit nervous of the quality, if they’d look cheapy, but they don’t! They’re really really cute and fit nice! Waistband is fitted close to body (so cute with body suit) but not too tight giving muffin top. The fabric texture is like a biiiit of a swooshy type material, but not full on. And not really loud or weird sounding. Just a tiny, fine swoosh. Elastic tightener things at waist and ankles🤌🏽 they’ll be cuuute with flat sandals and cute sneakers. They don’t seem see through at all, but if one of you got the white, they might be. I ordered the small, black, and I’m 5’1, 105lbs for size perspective. The black has a pretty, very faint type of little sheen to the fabric, which I think adds to the look of it being more quality. Not a matte black. The material is a bit thicker but not super thick. I think they’ll be perfect for fall and spring when there’s a tiny bit of chill in the air with a body suit or a T-shirt. Winter might work too with a turtleneck and a cute jacket. Summer humidity and heat, you’d prob feel a bit sweaty. I really like these pants and I’m super happy I got them! I sent pics to my best gf group chat and a few of them already ordered their own☺️ If you’ve been thinking about bringing back the parachutes, I feel like these ones are the move👌🏽☺️

UGH. These go back immediately I rarely write reviews, but these are so bad they deserve the effort. I was really looking forward to receiving these as the design looks comfortable and flattering. Not. At. All. These are made of a stiff, cheap cottony material. It doesn't drape well and just looks cheap. Cheap, I'm saying. Cheap. The sizing is HUGE (I'm 5'2", weigh 130lbs, ordered a M and these are enormous on me, they literally reach the floor). But worst is the sewing/waistband as the photo shows -- puckered, bunchy, weird, ill-made and just ugly. Do not waste your money by ordering these or your time in having to return them -- unless your goal is to look like you just fell off the pickle boat.

Soft and comfortable My details: 5 ft 7 in, 145 lbs Ordered: Brown, extra large Likes: soft, comfortable, dark brown color, feel like pajamas but look like outdoor clothes Dislikes: sizing a little off (I'm between medium and large but large is a little snug on waist and extra large waist is loose) - still keeping

A cute style and the print with a “log cabin” kind of feeling to it. These pajama pants are soft and comfortable, but thinner fabric than I hoped for. I live above the snow line and winters can get chilly. It is not unusual for outside temperatures to remain below freezing all night. That said, after trying these on, I think that for a climate like mine, these pajama pants are best suited for spring and fall months - not for colder wintery weather or snowy nights. I do love the pockets and the waistband is comfortable, not too tight. These pajama pants are LONG. I will need to hem the length (I am 5’2”) a few inches.

They're okay, could be better I had high hopes for these sweatpants and they left me disappointed. They are advertised as a baggy fit however I am a true size Large and I sized up to the XL for an extra oversized fit but there really isn't much wiggle room. They are also not nearly as long as they appear in the pictures. I'm average height (5'5") and the cuff sits right at my ankle with almost no scrunching. The cuff is also very wide for some reason and almost falls over my heel. The color is good, just like the photo. The fabric is okay, heavier weight and soft, but doesn't feel as high quality as some of the better-known name brands in a similar price range, likely due to the high polyester content. Overall, I'd give 3.5 stars. They are wearable, but I wouldn't buy again unless they lowered the price. At almost $35 I had higher expectations.

LOVE THESE SWEATS! Quality: Thick and fluffy. These sweatpants look and feel like they will be around for awhile. Versatility: You could wear these sweatpants to dress down around the house, or more casual to run errands or weekend festivities. I would not try to dress them up. They have a cute little logo on them and the pockets are made deep enough to hold personal items. Comfort: The inside of them has a very soft fleece like material that doesn’t itch or bother your skin. Fit: I purchased them in a size medium wanting a more baggy fit. I am 5ft 5in, 150 pounds. They fit slightly baggy BUT they are made properly so they do not sag or look unflattering. Color: I have these sweatpants in 3 colors. Porpoise, Heather Grey, and Black. All of which were true to color as shown in the photos displayed by the seller. Warmth: The perfect fall/winter sweatpants. They will keep you very warm while also looking out together. I say they’re worth the price point. I definitely recommend. I intend to buy them in other colors.

Amazing quality BUT I’m 5’7 & sadly have gone from a healthy 157 to 130 due to being sick. I based buying these in a size Medium off other reviews. I’m happy with the size. I’ve had to buy basically all new stuff bc of the weight loss & it sucks, but these sweatpants definitely don’t suck at all. I have to keep my legs extra warm when it’s cold out bc of what I’ve got going on with my health; these sweats checked off all the boxes with keeping warm big time! I live in the North East & there’s never a consistent temperature, especially not in the Spring. It’s like 78 or more during the day & then low 40’s upper 30’s at night. It literally KILLS me with the super cold nights. The material is very thick, SUUUPER soft inside, I love that they don’t have a draw string & the length is perfect IMO. I read other reviews about them not being high waisted. Idk they seem normal to me, but I’m not entirely sure what to expect from high waisted sweatpants either 🤷🏼‍♀️ For these being a Medium & me being so frigging skinny now 😢 I was expecting to be swimming in them. I just ordered another pair today in a different color & almost went with a large because they don’t seem that baggy to me but whatever, I ordered another medium. The only problem I have with these pants are the stain on the left ass cheek. Like WTF is that about? They arrived 2 days ago & came out of the package like this. They definitely weren’t used by myself or anyone else, so maybe it was a defect Unsure what the stain is about, but I intend to exchange them for the same color & size. Arrived next day with prime shipping. Well packaged, no weird smell or anything; just the rando stain. If you’re looking to stay warm & comfortable + if you have curves, I think you’ll love these sweats. They’re definitely a high end quality.

I’d say they’re high-ish waisted I’m 5’4, 110lbs. I’d say these are high waisted. Or at the very least mid-waisted. Definitely not low. They sit right at/above my belly button, depending on where I set them, not sure how high people need their sweat pants. I haven’t noticed anything weird going on in the crotch area either, sits fine on me, though I do have to admit that the waist band is so bulky that it just does nothing flattering for your figure. So I’d pair it with a very tight shirt to create some kind of contrast

Comfy. These pants appear to be true to size. The fabric is soft, stretchy, and lightweight. The black color doesn’t stain light-colored panties and is not see-through when bent over unless stretched to its max. The high-waisted band covers the belly button but can be folded if preferred. My wife is 5’7”, about 190 lb, and wears XL in most clothes. These pants are very stretchy, and she probably would’ve been okay with size L, but she prefers to size up because L, in most cases, is tight on her waist, and she doesn’t like the feeling of discomfort around her belly. Also, she would rather hide lumps and bumps—her words, not mine. But I love all of it as it reminds me of what she went through to give me three beautiful children. We ordered XL, and the inseam is over 31’. My wife likes long pants that almost touch the ground with her shoes on. That said, it’s hard to get pants on a budget with the inseam 31-32 online. Most sellers don't post inseam details, and we end up having to buy and then return, which is inconvenient, to say the least. Just a word of advice. To ensure you get the right fit, Don’t go by the length of the pants; always go by your inseam.

Decent pants, good value, run large I don't love these pants, but they fit well and are a great value. The color is accurate. The waffle texture is delicate, making them look more like a tweed that's suitable for business/work. The fit is very good. I'm 5'6", 157 lb, 31" waist, 41" hip and the medium fits well (I usually wear a large). I don't love all the pleats which make the front look blousy and puckery (photo attached). Also, the fabric doesn't seem thin or clingy, but the faults in my thighs are still visible when I walk in them (not sure why). Giving these 4 stars for being comfortable and such a good value, but they're not going to be my favorite pants.

SIZE DOWN-RUNS BIG I love everything about these pants. The color is a beautiful deep burgundy. Material is soft. It has 4 real pockets. Length is perfect for someone of my height. I ordered a 6. For reference: I am 5.5 1/2 and 140lbs. I am currently in-between sizing. I usually wear a 7/8. Some 6's fit, but tend to be snug in the crotch area when sitting. So I figured it would be a safe bet to go with the 6 since they run big and they would not be snug and they are not snug at all. I probably could have ordered a 4 and would be completely fine. I may get the Royal Blue in a 4 and compare the fit of the two, but we will see. Overall I am very happy with these pants. I am giving it a 4 star rating b/c through out the day, they did tend to ride up a bit & are starting to bunch at the top of my boots, but that could be b/c these are big on me, comfortable, but big. I would definitely recommend getting these if you are on the marked for comfortable, burgundy pants.

Accurate to product photos. More for looks than comfort on me. I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

Awesome, awesome, awesome pants These KICZOY Corduroy Loose Mid Rise pants are beautiful and amazing looking on. My pup was cozy on my bed so he's included in my photos. For starters though, I have to address the one problem. There is stretch EVERYWHERE in these pants except the waist. The waist feels binding because it doesn't move and stretch like the rest of the pant fabric. IF, they had put some stretch in the waist, part elastic, all elastic, or a band that stretches these pants would have been 10+++++ star quality. The pink is a soft mauve and it is BEAUTIFUL. Not too light, not too dark. The corduroy is small pin corduroy and it stretches amazingly well. Stretches in every direction. The fabric is light weight, flows beautifully and feels extremely comfortable. The legs are straight but FLOW into a bottom flare. It looks gorgeous. Pockets are ample plus two back pockets. I LOVE these pants, they fit me extremely well EXCEPT for the tight, stiff binding waist. If I'd lose 5-10 lbs they'd fit my waistline. I can wear them now but they are tight. I'm 5'4" 154 lbs.

Very thin, poor fabric... but comfortable and pretty cute anyway I'm 5'10, a size 10, and sized up to an XL Long to be safe. They're still on the smaller side. The long length is definitely not long-- they're too short for me and haven't even been washed yet. My biggest problems are with the material. Because the fabric is pretty clingy, the pants don't want to hang as wide as the product images, but these pants are still pretty cute. It's neither as stretchy nor as nicely draping as you would expect. It's wrinkly in a way that doesn't look fully intentional. And it's so, so thin. You can easily see both of the front pockets through the pants even though the thighs aren't fitted. This means that even 'seamless' underwear has a high chance of visibility. I would not pay $36 for pants made with this material.
