QUITE happy with these pants! These pants are very stylish, comfortable, and fun! The only potential qualm I’ll throw out is they do seem prone to wrinkling; they came out of the bag with deep creases but I’m hopeful after drying them and maybe a light ironing will get those out. I actually really like the material, it’s a little thicker than what I’d expected but it prevents them from being see through. The material is also lightweight despite being a bit thicker, and it flows well. They are the perfect length for me, landing just above the ground with no shoes on. The waist part also feels great, and I’m 5’3” and 130 lbs. I got the medium/short size. I think they pair very well with heels and I’m excited to start wearing these regularly because they’re so comfy and functional! Will also mention they do have good sized pockets. I’m planning to pair with a black t shirt, black heels (see pic), black purse, and some gold jewelry. :)

Better than expected KICZOY Women Pants Drawstring Waist Wide Leg Palazzo Linen Pants turned out to be better than we expected. The waist has an elastic band along with a draw string to hold them in place. Model 5'3" / Size 2 / wearing 3" platform shoes

Very roomy flared-out pants I’m 118 lbs and a size 4, but these pants look huge and matronly on me. The style feels like a throwback to the late '80s with a very wide, flowy, loose fit. I would have preferred a more A-line shape with a gradual widening from the hips to the hem, as shown on the model. Instead, the pants flare out more at the thighs than I had hoped. Rather than my personal taste, I’m uploading photos to show how they look. The tag indicates the fabric is made of 95% polyester and 5% elastane (spandex). Despite my usual dislike for polyester, the material looks and feels nicer than expected. If you like roomy pants, you’ll probably like them.

Pants appreciation review. For the first time in a long long time, I put on a pair of pants and I didn't body shame myself. I jumped from a size 6 to a 10 after 2 kids and its very hard to find flattering clothes that make me feel better about myself. Let me tell you this, these pants are incredible. Lets talk material first, it was a beautiful fall to it, its not see through but you can see underwear lines. It has pockets which is a plus, but they aren't deep enough to be functional. The cut of these pants is so flattering, it doesn't accentuate the wideness of the hips, instead it looks chic. Because all body types are different, even though I'm usually a L, I ordered a medium because large usually runs longer on me (i'm 5'3'') The waist is a little snug on me but I don't mind, it fits me really well. Whoever designed this, kudos!

Like dressy sweats I wanted an option to still look like I put extra effort but still be relaxed if I just wasn’t feeling like taking extra steps to get “dressed”. If there’s a need for dress pants these would still work. But for now, I mainly pair these with crop tops, body suits and front knotted band tees. Very dressy and classy, while still wide and comfortable. Very nice looking pants, very dressy and professional. I ordered the beige. The word says beige, but the picture looks more like a very light sand, almost white. They are as pictured. I was actually hoping for something a little more beige because I’ve never had much luck with virtually white pants. They all seem to stain so easy. The first time I wear them they come home with an ink spot, coffee stain, or something. These did not! I was very impressed. I wore them to the office on a regular workday. I took the train into the city. Wore the pants all day. When I came home, they looked great. A few wrinkles around the hip flexors, which is normal for an office job. I was eating a breakfast bar at my desk and dropped a few chocolate chips on the pants so I went into the bathroom and just wiped them off with a wet paper towel and the chocolate came right off. It’s great to be able to have such a light colored pair of pants that don’t stain easy. And they’re so easy to care for. They were a little creased out of the package, as you can see by some of the photos. I tried them on right away. I was wearing a black and purple knee brace and I just happened to have on black underwear. Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

Short and curvy approved! Here are the details: The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

First: POCKETS!! The pockets are amazing! There are five of them, all very generously sized! My large Pixel phone fits in with room to spare. I ordered these in both Large and X-Large, as I measured between sizes. I usually wear 14-16, at 5'7" and weighing about 200lbs, with wide hips (5 kids!) and a large backside. They do have a "Slim Fit," if you are curvy I would suggest sizing up. I can barely get into the L, but the XL are very comfortable! Being 72% polyester / 21% rayon / 7% spandex, they do stretch. The stitching is really neat, no loose threads and are said to be reinforced. The soft waist and is really nice, too. They are machine wash cold, and wash up nicely. I line/hang-dry most of my clothes anyway, and these dried quickly. For $10, these just became my SAHM Uniform. These are perfectly stretchy and comfy for all the things: crawling around with the toddler, squatting down to tie shoes and zip coats, sitting criss-cross-applesauce for storytime, and just being in motion all day. Highly recommend! I actually sent these to a few friends already!

Comfortable and functional For reference, I’m 5’6” and my weight fluctuates between 130-140lbs. I got the size medium in ceil blue. The tag says it’s 72% polyester, 21% rayon, and 7% spandex. Overall the joggers feel comfortable and lightweight. Size medium on me is a relaxed fit, with more roominess around the upper legs and butt. The waistband is elastic with drawstrings. There are 5 pockets in total, 2 in the front, 2 in the back, and one on the outside of the right thigh which is the only zippered pocket. In comparison to other scrub bottom prices, these seem competitive and seem like a good deal.

I absolutely loveeee these scrubs. They’re great figs dupe. I have quite a few figs scrubs but the sizing is so weird. I got a size large and I’m 5’4 for reference. They’re stretchy and good material. Literally gonna buy more!!

The softest, most comfortable scrub pants -- could wear as regular joggers, too! I wish I could describe just how soft and comfortable and wonderful these scrub bottoms are!! I have tons of scrubs and none of them are like this. These are fabulous!!⭐The most noticeable difference is the fabric - this is a soft, super lightweight, silky, stretchy material. I could honestly wear these as regular leisurewear joggers just as easily as scrub pants. These are not your typical cotton scrub material.⭐ The waist is a wide, flat, silky soft, elastic band that has a bungie cord type tie run through it. I don't usually use the ties because they stick out under my shirt and look strange IMO, and these fit and stayed up just fine without my tying the cord (I usually just tuck it in to the waistband). The waist also sits right at my belly button -- so even with my fluffy mid-section, no "belly rolling over the top" issues.⭐The ankle cuffs are also soft and fairly loose, so they move up and down my ankle as I move rather than clinging to them so they will not constrict or leave marks on your ankles during the long hours of wear. Very very comfortable.⭐SIZING: I am 5'5", fluffy in the midsection and usually wear an 8/10 (L) US -- but I went up to the XL based on the Amazon recommendation and these fit perfectly. The waist, the length, the thighs -- all are exactly to my preferences.⭐Great cargo type storage: a pocket at each hip; 2 back pockets; on the left thigh there is a zippered pocket with a patch slip pocket sewn on top; on the right thigh there is a zippered pocket that has a little flap over it to protect the zipper. I love the mix of zippered and patch pockets -- some easy to access and others are more secure (can store my rings and such in during surgery without fear of them falling out).I am in love with these jogger/cargo style scrub bottoms and how incredibly comfortable they are. I highly recommend these both as scrub bottoms or as casual jogger pants! 👍👍👍

Very comfortable but not very attractive These lounge pants are cut big, especially in the crotch area. The colors are really nice and the fabric is very soft. The fabric is on the thinner side. I am 5'2 and 125 lbs. Too many clothing products from China runs small so I ordered based on my pant (waist) size which turned out to be a size Large, and this is too big for me because these pants have an elastic waist. I could have fitted a size Small and comfortably in a size Medium. These pants have pockets and a drawstring so I am able to sinched them for a tighter fit. These pants are great to lounge in but I would not wear them out since they are cut boxy and wide.

For size reference, I'm 5'4 and 170lbs wearing the size large. I've just dropped to a size 14 and have been actively losing weight, so I didn't want to size up on these. They're a comfortable fit, and exactly what I was looking for. If you're my size and looking for a baggier fit I'd probably get the XL. These pants are so soft, and have pockets in the front. They're perfect for lounging around the house. At 3 pairs for $25 I'd say they're a great value.

The material is a little thicker than I expect for pants with mesh but I guess it is “fashion.” Running in these pants will cause one part of your legs to be cooler than other which is weird. That being said I appreciate that the material is thicker because I have no concerns of them being transparent. I ordered a small and am 6’ around 140 lbs

5’9 170 pounds I purchased a medium. They fit perfectly as I assumed they might be a little tight because I have a bit of pooch -a little top heavy don’t let my chicken legs fool you!- on my belly but with them coming right to my belly button they comfortably slim me down and look and feel great! Also I’m about 66% legs and can never find ANYTHING long enough! These actually are long enough for me and even bunch up a little at the ankle. I suggested to make the mesh last longer -even though mine is still holding strong after 3 washes and drys!- to let them air dry just so the heat doesn’t melt the mesh .

Great Workout Pants I love these workout pants. They’re super soft and fit me perfectly. I’m 5 foot 10 and usually have a hard time finding pants long enough for me, but these are the perfect length. You can wear them high waisted or you can fold the waist over if you prefer.

Very comfy but much too long The waist on these pants and the legs are a good width for my body. The material is not really shiny but not totally flat either, if that makes sense. I really like the generous pockets on both sides. They are very wide legged as the description stated but the issue is how long they are. I'm only 5'3 and am used to having things long but I haven't had this issue with athletic pants before. I rolled them at the waist several times and the pants are still going to hang over and drag on the floor when I wear them with sneakers. If it werent for that long length I could see myself getting them in other colors because of how comfortable they are. I'm short legged and have a long torso, so I'm not just short but even have shorter legs than some other short people. But in general I think anyone who doesn't consider themselves tall will find these to be too long because they were long on me even rolling the waist three times. I unrolled them for the picture because when they were rolled it made the crotch area look weird because of how the fabric pulls when you roll them.

Not see-through! The perfect scrub bottoms!! These are the fifth pair of nursing scrubs I've bought from Amazon in an attempt to find WHITE scrub bottoms that aren't see-through. I don't know what kind of magic chant was performed over these pants but even though I was wearing black undergarments in the pictures you can hardly tell! The pants were a little baggy on me. I'm 5'2" at around 110 pounds, but I planned to wear some skin-tight leggings under them anyways because I get cold inside hospitals. With the leggings underneath they look absolutely perfect with no more bagginess. I did need to hem the bottoms because not even the Petite size fit my legs. After a long hard search for the perfect scrubs I believe I have found them. Five stars!! I recommended to anyone looking for white scrubs that are not translucent. If this review helped you make an informed purchase please mark it as ♡

Lightweight capri leggings Read a review that said these were more like pajamas and that's what sold me. That's exactly what I was looking for (lightweight leggings not baggy PJ pants). These were perfect. They are lightweight and not binding - they have spandex for stretchy comfort, but just right for pajama grade (95% Rayon/5% Spandex). I am 5'6" (160 pounds) and normally wear a size 10. I bought 2 pair, Med and a Large. Both fit fine. I prefer the Medium and it's not too tight at all. The waist fits high on your natural waist and I fold them down sometimes, although the waistband isn't too tight even though I seem to be in the middle of M and L. One review says they feel cool on, I agree. I am really pleased with these. Also another review said they rolled at the bottom after a days wear, and that happens when I wear these for pj's too. But if you are looking for lightweight leggings with just the right amount of stretch, these are perfect.

Size up for a relaxed fit These joggers are soft and comfortable. I love how they look with the matching sweatshirt. They looked like a slightly slimmer jogger so I sized up hoping to get a more relaxed fit. I'm 5'9, 170lbs, size large or 10 normally, but I ordered an extra large. I'm glad I sized up, they fit perfectly. I think my normal size would've been too fitted in the legs and tight in the waist. The length is not long enough for me to wear without scrunching them up on my calves. They're at that awkward length where they're too long to look cropped and too short to look like regular length. I am tall so that probably won't be an issue for most. The waist is a medium rise and they have a functional drawstring and pockets. The material is soft and it's fuzzy on the inside. It's the perfect weight, not too thin or too thick.

Material light weight & comfortable Bought my first pair at TJ Maxx and loved them. After a lengthy search, delighted to find them here. Hard for me to find pants long enough with my needed 36" inseam. Because of the draw string cuff, a shorter person can adjust them to the correct length.Edit: change in style, not for the best.I now own 7 pairs, same size, different colors. My weight is the same. The last 3 purchased are unfortunately shorter by several inches. They also have a little tighter fit, but still roomie and very comfortable. Also the pocket now gapes open when sitting. When walking the pocket again gapes open and often my thumbs get hooked in the pocket when hand swings forward with a sometimes painful result. See photo- the old style had a snap on the pocket. This is still a quality pant, but because of the style changes, I won't be purchasing more since I have found similar pants in tall.

Size ordered: Large (then exchanged for Medium) Height 5'5" Waist 34" NOTE: "Red Tie dye" pattern is not as described; mine were bright pink, all over the pants, and nothing like the picture. NOTE: The black pair's dye bleeds in the wash. Would not recommend washing with light colors 👍 Updated review: I've exchanged size Large for size Medium, as I found there to be a bit too much excess material throughout the legs. I am happy with the medium fit. The wasteband difference between the Large and Medium is minimal, and the primary difference is just a bit more snug throughout the legs (which I am very pleased with, as I love the non-restrictive wasteband) I've been searching for a flattering jogger style lounge pant, that has a similar silhouette to a skinny jean with comfy slight bagginess through crotch area, for what feels like forever. I love these so far. So soft and stretchy (think breathable thin spandex material) lounge style, but I don't find them to look frumpy, and they still have a distinctive jogger shape. The wasteband is surprisingly comfortable and non-restrictive, especially for a pair of pants with fitted legs. The pockets are too small for my liking, unfortunately, and my smartphone has to be angled and pushed in strategically otherwise it will fall out. Overall, happy with my purchase and have ordered more.

Purchased both medium and large sizes These joggers are great! I consider them to be one of those “Amazon Gems”. They are extremely soft, very fitted (almost like leggings), they have pockets which I love, and they have a drawstring with a very expandable waistline. I love that the waistline is nice and big w/plenty of room for my “mom belly”. They are super comfy and a great value, I only paid $12.99. I purchased both the medium and large in black because I wanted to see the difference. I am 5’6”and weigh 145 pounds, so I go back and forth between med and large sizes. There was barely any difference between the medium and large size. The only difference I noticed was the waist band on the large was slightly bigger than the medium. Other than that, there was really no difference. The length and width of the pants are pretty much the same size. I thought maybe the large would be a little bigger in the leg area and maybe slightly longer, but they weren’t. That’s not a problem for me, I can wear both sizes, but I figured I would mention it in case it could help somebody. Pictures: Size medium- The 3 pictures that I’m wearing a camo shirt and house slippers are the size medium joggers. Size large- The 2 pictures that I’m wearing a grey shirt and flip flops are the size large joggers

Perfect! First off, I'm a guy. I have been looking for joggers that fit like skinny jeans for a minute now and found these, ordered a black pair in medium. They fit perfectly!! Couldn't be happier. They do hug your legs so if that's not your thing you might want to look elsewhere for a looser fit, and I can see how some shorter folks may not appreciate the length of these, but I'm 5'8" and they fit like a dream. They are soft and don't lose shape like my old gilligan & o'malleys joggers, those would stretch after one wear especially around the knees. They did have an intense odor to them when I first opened as other reviewers have mentioned but you should be washing them before wearing besides a test fit anyway. I didn't know if I wanted a large or medium as I heard they run small but I went for medium in black and they fit just fine since the waist is properly elastic. Size and color difference I initially ordered these in medium in Olive Green (I'm 5'2", 125 lbs.), and they were too small. As leggings, they would have been close to perfect (and I would have given them a 5-star rating had they fit correctly), but I was looking for a looser fit. I then ordered the large because the color was perfect, and the fabric is so amazingly soft! The large size in the same color (Olive Green) not only were virtually the exact same size as the medium, but the color was closer to a dark brownish green, not the olive green I wanted. I probably would have kept them anyway if they'd fit, but generally, when I order a size larger, I expect the size to BE larger. The return for both was seamless (I'm a Prime member), but I think I'll be trying a different company to fulfill my needs. I give both an overall rating of 3 stars because they were made very well and the fabric is fabulous despite the size and color disparities.

These pants/ leggings are far better made than their price reflects. Want to get more of these in the other colors. The navy is a beautiful dark navy. 100% recommend.

The only weird part in the back pocket, rather than pockets on the sides of the legs like most leggings these have one on the back with a zipper to keep things secure in side. I might find that I love it and that all pockets are inferior, however at the beginning stages of my introductions to this new pocket placement I am skeptical and hesitant to open myself up to that potential love. They are soft and warm, the fabric isn't so thick that they're hard to put on, but they're soft and fuzzy inside and will work great on cool days.

Too long, too big, itchy, cheap These are small petite pants and I am 5"0. In this picture I have them up over my ribs and they are still too long. The restocking fee tells me that the company is aware of the size issue, but somehow benefits from people needing to return them for new sizes. The material is cheap and warm. Since these specific scrubs are required for my school, I have no choice but to endure them for the next 4 years. I feel like a prison inmate wearing them. I worry that if I a size down so that I look less like a clown, it will be too tight on my hips. I'll have to actually pay someone money to get these cheap pants altered. Ugh. $12 just turned into $30.

Very well constructed, quality fabric, and cool in our hot summer: thank you! I bought 3 pair in different blues and happy with my purchase. Excellent stitching and and crisp fabric looks great. Deep pockets. Loose fitting legs are drafty in summer heat, yay! I bought size Medium: 5'7" &125lbs with 28" waist. I find the waistband snug. They will not fall down! 😉 See my photo: the 2" wide, very well sewn elastic waist is just over 12" laid flat, so possibly 25" circumference. If you like a snug waistband then these will be great for you. Update April 2022: yes I gained back lost weight ;) and even now at 145lbs and 32" waist, these pants are still comfortable fit.

Kind of feels the same as OCP pants. The fit is loose in the knee and lower leg, tight in the thigh and butt, and fitted in the waist, but comfortable and easy to move in. I typically wear a size 8-10, depending on the style of jeans. I am 5’4” and these are slightly long for me. If you have ever worn OCP pants they fit the same, as in the height of the waist band is the same, the stretch of the waist band and pant material is the same except they’re not as thick and not as heavy.

Comfortable, practical put together These have many color options, I have about 7 pairs. These are a nice thick material and have pockets that are big enough to hold my phone. They are thick enough to be comfortable in the winter time but not too much that I'm uncomfortably hot while running around the house cleaning. I got a bit concerned about wearing sweatpants all the time. I'm a stay at home mom and I want to be comfortable but I also don't want my husband looking over from his office thinking I've given up and am not trying anymore. These are easy to match with a t-shirt, sweater or something a little dresser and I feel put together. It's easy to run out of the house and do quick errands, go to an appointment or the grocery store or drop something off at the school for the kiddos. I wouldn't wear these to go out with friends or a date night but they are suitable for more relaxed office wear and I love them for traveling. These pants are supper stretchy but are tight enough so they do not slide down through out the day. I am 5'6" and depending on if I'm working out or not I am between 122-130 pounds. It's great that these fit me well no matter how I'm doing on my eating and exercise. I got a small tall because after these are washed and dried they do shrink up a bit so you can't really judge the fit until that first wash is done. Hope this helps!

Not for Petite Frames 5’3, apx 110lbs, 28” waist, 36” hips. Could not find a size chart so I ordered M based off a similar pair of pants I had. I have been hunting for a nicer looking pair of lounge pants for a few years since the tie on my favorite Old Navy ones broke. Unfortunately they don’t sell them- or anything similar- on their website and I cannot access a physical store to check. Based on the photos these seemed like a good replacement; slimmer fit, pockets, drawstring(more polished than elastic) and slight contrasting stitching for interest. While the pants are very soft they were not as expected. The contrasting stitching was absent and while they stayed on my hips without being tied, the pants were huge. I measured and the rise was about 12”, where my Old Navy ones are 9”. I decided not to try sizing down though since they were not as nice as my Old Navy ones. Those I could pair with a cute tee and babysit, go to the mall, or a casual restaurant in. These I wouldn't answer the door in. Honestly I don’t think they would have fit well sizing down either as they just seem to be made for a curvier person. 3/5 Stars: They are soft and I like the drawstring and pockets. I can’t really criticize the sizing since there isn’t a size chart listed. But I think the photos are a bit deceiving in terms of fit and stitching color.

Comfortable, roomy pants in pretty color. I ordered these pants for lounging and sleeping. I think they will be great for that. They are made of fabric that is like a slightly heavier t-shirt. The fit is loose and comfortable and I expect to dry them on low heat- there is room for shrinkage. I am 5’6” tall and weigh 220. The 2X fit with room to spare. There is no need to size up. The color I ordered is steel blue and it is a true blue, not the teal color shown in the product description. The waistband is wide and made of a soft ribbed knit. There is a narrow band of elastic at the top edge. The drawstring is functional. Overall, I really like these pants. I spend most of my time in loungewear and these are a nice addition.

Sizing is off The quality of these pants were surprisingly good. But oh, the sizing—I’m 135 lbs and 5’11", usually a size 6 or 28 in jeans, and I decided on a medium, expecting some extra room for potential shrinking. Little did I know, I'd end up with a comedy of proportions: the waist was like a generous invitation, while the fit around my butt and thighs decided to put on a tightrope act. The length? A creative take on "keeping it high and dry!" They claimed a 41-inch inseam, but apparently, part of that took a detour above my knees, leaving me perfectly prepped for a stylish flood-ready look. On the bright side, if you’re tall or looking for capris these pants will have you poised to confidently conquer both fashion and unexpected puddles!

I actually ordered these for my daughter who is typically a small but I sized up to a medium bc she wanted the oversized wide leg fit in the model pics. I’m typically a medium so I used my experience for the review. The faux leather material is very soft & has a ton of stretch to it. It’s not all that thick on it’s on so it’s lined w a thin fleece that feels great against the skin & adds warmth. The fact that the material is thinner helps these drape properly though. When I first put these on I was shocked by how high waisted these actually are. I have a short torso so the super high waist creates a look in the front created by too much fabric. It looks like it’s pulling bc they’re tight, but they’re not. There’s just too much fabric for my waist. The hips & booty fit very nicely though. The pants hug my booty & then drape down in a straight leg style. They look cute on & the cut of the legs is great however the waist is definitely smaller than the size chart has listed. The waist of these pants looks completely different on the outside & inside. On the outside it looks like your average button w zip fly made from the same material as the rest of the pants. On the inside something totally different is going on. There’s a thick elastic band sewn to the waistband on the inside & it definitely doesn’t have the same stretch as the rest of the pants bc it feels tight on me & according to the measurements it shouldn’t. The size chart measurements show the medium waist to be 15.8” halved or 31.6” all the way around. I have a 29.5” waist on a good day & a 30” waist on a bloated day so the waistband should fit w room to spare opposed to feeling tight. Thankfully the smaller waistband will help these fit my daughter properly, but definitely something to be wary of when choosing a size. The length of these is also ridiculous. I’m short so most pants are long on me, but these take it to a whole new level. They’re not only too long when I’m on my toes they’re too long in over 4” stilettos. Thankfully my daughter likes to cuff pants that are too long & if that doesn’t work out I’ll hem them. Other than the waistband, the pants seem to be well made, but I fell the $40 price tag is a bit high. Definitely better to buy when on sale for $30 like now.

There’s a thick elastic band sewn to the waistband on the inside & it definitely doesn’t have the same stretch as the rest of the pants bc it feels tight on me & according to the measurements it shouldn’t. The size chart measurements show the medium waist to be 15.8” halved or 31.6” all the way around. I have a 29.5” waist on a good day & a 30” waist on a bloated day so the waistband should fit w room to spare opposed to feeling tight. Thankfully the smaller waistband will help these fit my daughter properly, but definitely something to be wary of when choosing a size. The length of these is also ridiculous. I’m short so most pants are long on me, but these take it to a whole new level. They’re not only too long when I’m on my toes they’re too long in over 4” stilettos. Thankfully my daughter likes to cuff pants that are too long & if that doesn’t work out I’ll hem them. Other than the waistband, the pants seem to be well made, but I fell the $40 price tag is a bit high. Definitely better to buy when on sale for $30 like now.
Cute but long This is cute and stretchy but it’s also long for short girls and you would need heels to make it work. I’m 5ft 153 lbs and medium fit perfectly

Two pairs and completely different quality…. I am 5’8 and 120lb. First I ordered a black jeans size 4 long. I got them, put on and was so happy. Great fit, perfect length, nice fabric, a little bit loose, but I don’t mind it. I was so pleased with the look! So I went to Amazon and ordered a blue pair. It was a big mistake… What I got was awful. The same model, the same size. The black pair was from Mexica, the blue one was from Egypt. Blue pair is almost 1 inch shorter and has a terrible cut in hips area. I was very upset. I am returning this blue pair. From my opinion it deserves only one star - for fabric. The black pair deserves all 5 stars and even more! I am highly recommend it!

Sizing inconsistent! I purchased these in Ellis, Black, and Seattle, all size 6 Medium. First off, they're not very high rise- more like a mid-rise. My Levi's all have a much higher rise, so if you're looking for a true high rise, look elsewhere. Secondly, you're going to want to order a few different sizes bc they're pretty inconsistent. The Black pair were so tight I could barely get them over my hips! The Seattle were slightly looser, but still tight, and Ellis was just right! All were a tad long (I'm 5'6 145lbs). Going to return for Ellis size 6S and Seattle Size 8 Short. The Black pair had a cheaper fabric quality, more like a jegging but the Seattle and Ellis washes are ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ quality. Will definitely try those two washes in a Short!

They are flared Lee has a perfect fit for me - Spandex is great and gives a snug fit without cutting off my blood circulation like some jeans do. That being said, these jeans don't fit from the knees down. They flare all right - like jeans I had in the 60s. Which wouldn't be so bad - if they were not so long. See the tag - It says 16 SHORT. I bought these in a "Short" because I wanted to replace a pair of jeans that were too long and frayed and tore at the bottom. These are even worse. In the picture of the jeans on me, I DO have shoes on. Ankles? Yes, those, too. I love LEE, but I wish they really could make a pair of jeans that are short. Not all of us are 6' and over. Not going to return them, but I am disappointed. BTW, they have been washed and did not draw up. But I may try again.

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Should have read the size chart better If you usually wear Levi's, your usual size will be too skinny and too long here. I wear a size 12 short in Levi's, and they're perfect. These Lee's are too tight in the waist, and ridiculously long. The inseam for a SHORT is 30 inches! I'm 5'4" ish, and I have to stand all the way on my tiptoes to get these to not brush the floor. The waist is about 15.5" across, which is super unflattering and uncomfortable on my post-baby belly. If you are more apple shaped and less hourglass shaped like I am right now, I suggest you size based on the waist measurement, not anything else. That all said, I gave these a 4 because they are excellent quality. They are soft and stretchy, and if it weren't for my own user error and some odd sizing, they would be great!

Run small, off-center: Factory defect? These run snug. I got the biggest size, 18, in both the dark wash and lighter shade and I’m trying to decide if they’ll break in enough to be comfortable. The first thing I noticed was that the zipper is off center on BOTH pairs. My guess is this is a factory defect and that’s why I was able to snag them for $16. It seems they cut one leg a little smaller because it definitely pulls to the left when you zip it. I like that they’re long enough for me and don’t ride up in length from my big butt or pull down in back at the waist, as I’m 5’11” and 220 lbs. They definitely don’t do my perimenopausal midsection any favors, but that’s just how I’m shaped now so I guess I have to deal with it until I make more effort to change. They were a little high in the crotch when I first put them on and I was afraid I’d have a serious camel toe. But they’re starting to loosen up the longer I wear them, so that isn’t looking like an issue anymore. Overall, I’d say they’re better quality than most jeans I’ve tried from Amazon but definitely not the unicorn I’m looking for in a pair of jeans. If you buy them, wear them around for a while with the tags on to see how they feel after they break in some. That will give you a better idea of the fit. I just hope they don’t shrink in the wash because then they probably will be too small.

Actually fits properly! I'm 5'1, 111lbs and finding pants that fit me boarders on impossible. Anything that fits my waist is way too long. I'm not spending $50-$75 on good quality jeans, just to go spend even MORE to get them tailored to actually fit. I figured try out Prime before you Buy and these won no contest. I tried Wrangler, Lee and Amazon Essentials brands; ranging $24 to $70. I couldn't believe these fit so well and only cost $24 (there was a sale at time of purchase). I've worn them with work boots and also boots with a heel. Both look great! There's no gap in the back, they're the perfect length, the fabric is great quality and they're comfy! My husband also agrees they look fantastic. I've already purchased two more pairs. Just for context, I also tried the 4 short and 4 regular, since it recommended that's the size I should purchase based on reviews. Like a lot of womens pants, I assumed they would run large but, size 4 is huge. So, if you usually find yourself purchasing size 4 pants/shorts/skirts, I'd go with size 2.

Excellent Pants, but be aware of possible defects! Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

GREAT Fit for my "APPLE" shape, Holding Up Well, Comfortable I'm hard to fit - I have the dreaded "apple" shape instead of the more common "pear" body shape. These Lee Plus-Size Relaxed Fit Straight Leg jeans fit. Being almost 6ft I get the tall variation. The fabric is not as heavy as the Lands End relaxed jeans I loved but are now discontinued (darn!). They feel a little skimpy by comparison and did not keep me as comfortable/warm in this exceptional New England winter as those heavier ones. These have elastic built into to fabric itself. The Lands End jeans had a sliding elastic panel. Different solutions and each worked well for me. One thing I like about these Lee Jeans is the pockets are deep enough to use quite useful. My doctor told me to stop carrying a shoulder bag (and my shoulder problems stopped!) so now I have come to rely on my pockets so getting pants with useful and deep pockets is a big plus for me. Besides fitting my waist they seem to fit everywhere else too. Maybe I should get the legs shortened just a little. If I get the regular length they are too short and as my mother used to kid me - I can't cut it longer! I've been wearing these jeans for about a year and I'm very happy. They seem to be wearing quite well with many laundry cycles still look quite good. They keep getting softer and more comfortable with each wash. They have been color-fast so I can just throw them into any load. No worries. I'm really pleased with these Lee Plus-Size Relaxed fit jeans. I don't have a down side for this review so I think they are a solid 5 star item.

Great jean but pants fit differently depending on color which is frustrating Buying jeans is always a trial. When I got these jeans I didn't know what to expect because previous Lee's didn't fit well. As soon as I tried these jeans on I was in love. I'm 5'9 and weight 285. I have an hourglass figure (wide hips and shoulders with a narrower waist). Most pants don't fit my thighs if they fit my hips well but these hugged everything in a flattering and comfortable way. (For this pair of pants I'm referring to the majestic style pants here). I need a pair of work pants so I decided I would order the exact same pair in black because I loved them so much. What a disappointment! They're tight on the waist and when I bend over I can feel them digging into my stomach. There's no way I would be able to sit in these for any extended period. They're literally just the tiniest bit smaller than the first pair I bought and that makes them unwearable for me. The black pants are also really long even though I got the regular length. I got the majestic in the long length (which were too long but I didn't mind) and the black pants in the normal length, but the black pants were nearly the same length! Super disappointed and returning. I would buy the majestic pair again though.

Too bad about the "saddle bags" I really like everything about Lee jeans except that no matter which version I buy, they ALWAYS seem to have extra fabric on the outside of the thighs for so-called "saddlebags." Hey! Not all women have these! I hate the little pockets this extra fabric creates--at times I feel like I'm wearing jodhpurs--and in the past I've even tried to sew them off, which has generally made them look dumb in other ways! Otherwise, these jeans are really nice. They have a comfortable degree of stretch, the length is just right (I'm 5'3", 130 lbs & ordered a 6 short), the blue color is pretty, & for the price, the quality is pretty good. I just wish Lee would ditch the extra fabric on the sides--not all women's figures are the same!

Put them on this morning and when I pulled them up (no, they’re not snug or in need of any force to get them on) my finger went right through the material! Weird cut for me! I ordered these capris in white and in “Sedona,” and they fit differently. First, I currently weigh 160 lbs and waist and hip dimensions are 34” and 40” respectively. See how the rise puckers up into extra layers beneath my waist in the photos showing me sitting down? SO not flattering! The extra hip fabric is visible in the photos of me standing. Anyway, maybe I just have funky measurements, but my “Sedona” capris puckered out on both sides of the hip region. I now seem to recall having the same issue with Lee’s Easy Rider jeans. For whatever reason, I don’t see the same extra fabric in the white capris; those are keepers. Hope this review and photos are to someone!

Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Very disappointed that this didn't fit me how i'd like, they're so comfortable! Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Great buy. I bought two pairs of these a size 8short and a 10short. I am 5’3” and a half and the length of the short size is perfect. I’m not telling you my weight, sorry. I normally buy size 10 jeans at Old navy and in Levi’s. While both sizes fit me (8 and 10), the size 10 is much better for work. The waist is a bit tight on both sizes, but this loosens during the day. The pockets are functional: the two pockets in the front and the pockets in the back. This is a big deal because way too many women’s pants assume we don’t need pockets which is so annoying. I bought the size 8 in dark gray and the 10 in tan. I’m pleased because while I can wear jeans to work, it is always safer to wear actual pants. These are not too stuffy, cheap enough to buy multiple pairs and I can just wash them with everything else. The material isn’t too thin. These are pretty nice. I wouldn’t Hesitate to buy more when I stain these.

Fantastic pants. Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Very comfortable, styling sloppy While they did "fit" as expected, the styling is sloppy and unflattering for me. I have extra weight on my upper thigh and these pants highlight that. Also seemed to be baggy and slouchy in the legs at the bottom. Long length was perfect and I'm 5' 7" but leg is too tapered. Very comfortable and moving around and bending was easy. The sloppy cut to these seems to be lessened when paired with a company polo.

Size depends on color I tried the Charcoal, Plaid, Black, and Flax. The 14 short were perfect in the Charcoal (pictures to show the length difference between “medium” and “short”) and Plaid (I’m 5’4 and 175lbs). The black and flax were too tight in the waist in size 14. The flax in a 16, I could wear but they were still a little tight (tighter than the charcoal/plaid 14s). Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Very disappointed that this didn't fit me how i'd like, they're so comfortable! Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Great buy. I bought two pairs of these a size 8short and a 10short. I am 5’3” and a half and the length of the short size is perfect. I’m not telling you my weight, sorry. I normally buy size 10 jeans at Old navy and in Levi’s. While both sizes fit me (8 and 10), the size 10 is much better for work. The waist is a bit tight on both sizes, but this loosens during the day. The pockets are functional: the two pockets in the front and the pockets in the back. This is a big deal because way too many women’s pants assume we don’t need pockets which is so annoying. I bought the size 8 in dark gray and the 10 in tan. I’m pleased because while I can wear jeans to work, it is always safer to wear actual pants. These are not too stuffy, cheap enough to buy multiple pairs and I can just wash them with everything else. The material isn’t too thin. These are pretty nice. I wouldn’t Hesitate to buy more when I stain these.

Fantastic pants. Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Very comfortable, styling sloppy While they did "fit" as expected, the styling is sloppy and unflattering for me. I have extra weight on my upper thigh and these pants highlight that. Also seemed to be baggy and slouchy in the legs at the bottom. Long length was perfect and I'm 5' 7" but leg is too tapered. Very comfortable and moving around and bending was easy. Extra material in crotch area isn't flattering. With all that said, I'm keeping them to wear to my "khakis required" workplace (corporate America please stop this!) and I will vow to avoid mirrors. The sloppy cut to these seems to be lessened when paired with a company polo.

Size depends on color I tried the Charcoal, Plaid, Black, and Flax. The 14 short were perfect in the Charcoal (pictures to show the length difference between “medium” and “short”) and Plaid (I’m 5’4 and 175lbs). The black and flax were too tight in the waist in size 14. The flax in a 16, I could wear but they were still a little tight (tighter than the charcoal/plaid 14s). They also were shorter than the other colors in the “short” size (the others laid at the bottom of my shoes without dragging, which I prefer. The black and flax were up a bit higher). I gave 4 stars because what I kept are comfortable with decent stretch. It’s just trial an error finding the right size/color combination.

Not sure but will try.... The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

Extremely flattering and comfortable Levi’s gets it right with curvy clothes. I have the worst time finding pants that fit my thighs and butt but also my waist. I have very aggressive dimensions, 44x36x50, and I typically have to wear leggings everywhere. But when I need to be a grown up and look professional, but still feel like a woman, these pants did the job and fit like a dream. For context, I’m about 5’7”, 250 lbs, with ridiculously thunderous thighs and booty. These pants fit and flattered with no significant waist gap. I will be getting more styles.

favorite BUT THEY SHRANK So comfortable! Thick material. Stretchy waistband without buttons. They feel like thick luxurious lounge pants but they look perfect for the office. I worried about them making me look wide since I am short and size 12, but they are the perfect amount of wide. I’m 5’2” and ordered the short. They are perfect to wear with a heel. Be careful how you care for them bc they shrank for me

Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Weight: 170, height: 5’6” I opted for the size 14 regular. I’m Somewhat curvier in the back. I did order 16, but it was too big, especially around the waist. The 14 is not too tight, or too loose. Inseam is perfect for my height. These are pull on with no zipper to deal with. Two side pockets that are deep. Back pockets are fake (cannot access). Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.

New Favorite Pants! I, like most women, have trouble finding pants that fit right. For me, these pants are the perfect fit. I am tall and hourglass shaped. I'm 5' 8 1/2" tall and 133 lbs, and I bought a size 6. 1 - They are long enough without being too long. 2 - They go in at the waist, like I go in at the waist. I have a problem with a gap hanging open in the small of my back, and pants that go in enough to accommodate my not inconsiderable asset in the back without squeezing the bum too much, but also going in where I go in. These fit perfectly. 3 - They have good stretch to them, and are very comfortable. 4 - The material (at least this color) is not wrinkly, but not too stiff either. 5 - Body skimming without being baggy nor too tight. 6 - They are high waisted for tucking in, without being up in your ribcage as some high waisted pants are. These sit right below my belly button.

Pockets Are Real I’m 5’4 and a size 12/14 in most pants. I ordered a 14 just in case they’d fit small around the waistband and I was right. 14 fit perfect though. I should’ve gotten a 14 short despite what some of the reviews said since I’m not wearing them with heels. I’d say if you’re under 5’6 and not wearing heels with these, then go with short sizing or you’ll have to hem them like I do. The pockets are very real. They are business pants, not your average pants. You have to remove the little white seam inside the pocket. Gently pull at one of the corners of the pocket and keep pulling until the string is out and the pocket is open. Or you can be less patient like I am and just take scissors to the seam, but you’ll have to be very careful not to cut the pocket. I can’t speak on durability as I just received them yesterday and will be wearing them to work today, but they feel like they’ll hold up for a while. I’m ordering more soon in another color as well.

Comfortable, very loose fit for all-around wear. On me, baggie more than relaxed. The fit on these pants is soft, comfortable, and to size, although they lean quite baggie not relaxed - Lee Relaxed Fit Straight Leg Pant - Flax - 8. Most notable is the very generous cut for the back of the thighs. I’m 5’5” 130 lbs. and ordered a size 8 and it’s a definite baggie fit on me. However, the waistband, torso and upper rear area lean to a firmer fit, so going down in size may not be an option. (This whole design really takes me to a past decade.) The length is also generous. I can wear these pants with sneakers, but they do have that bigger casual front crease. If I go to 2.5” heels, the length is almost perfect. (See pics.) The Flax color is a nice beige hue. They can dress up, but I can also rely on their durability, they are well made. The material holds it shape well, and there is some give. Note: They do shrink up just a bit (waistband, torso) after the initial wash. YMMV.

Love them I love these way these feel and fit. The cotton is really soft, seams appear solid. The color is bright and they are just comfy. I really could lounge in them, feel like pjs. I'm 5ft 200lbs and bought a size large. Although I liked and wanted the relaxed feel, I could've purchased a small and would still comfortably fit in them. I have a lot of wiggle room and stretch, even in the waistband, which has a drawstring. I would knot the ends to prevent string from going in hole when washed. The waistband stretches another 5-6 inches out for me and my belly is 41 inches. I prefer the gathering at the ankles because tight fits around the ankle aren't flattering on me but with that being said a tall person can rock these maybe up to 5'5 or 5'6. I prefer the roominess of the large as it hides my fupa. I bought four designs and only have to return one as the red plaid has a hole in the right pocket. Oh, it has pockets! Not shallow but not deep enough to hold cell phone unless you had a tighter fit, like if I had gotten the small the phone would fit snug. They are not transparent so no worry about underwear showing. They are lightweight. I'll update the breathability later as I live in Florida and it gets hot. They have found a repeat customer. I'll update after I do my first wash to see how it holds up. Good value for the money.

Comfy Cozy Pants All Day Long I have these pants in 3 patterns, and I've bought more patterns for my friends too because I just love these comfy cozy pants! At 5'4" and 150lbs, the large size fits me comfortably. The medium was a little snug with less freedom of movement. The pockets are an okay size, not too shallow but definitely not very deep. The fabric material is sooo soft and has held up to being washed regularly. Since I've bought so many of them, I noticed the cut of the print is not the same each time, so you do run the risk of having a giant bullseye print right on your crotch or butt, or having the pattern repeat in a weird way also drawing attention to the crotch. So, I've had to return a few pairs as well for bad cuts of the patterns, or for having holes in the pockets or ankle cuffs. These pants are cheap, afterall. But I can't get enough of 'em!

Great Joggers! I absolutely loved these joggers as soon as I opened the package - as per previous reviews they are incredibly soft, the length was perfect. I ordered the grey camo print and the color was exactly as displayed on my monitor. I normally wear size 0-2 and ordered Medium in these pants as most of the reviews mentioned to size up; that didn't quite work for me - they were too baggy in the waist as well as hips most likely because I'm not that curvy (waist - 25" and hips - 36"). I am exchanging them for size Small - I will update the review when the new pair arrives. UPDATE: After receiving the new pair in size Small I can honestly say that these are my favorite joggers ever (I'm going to order more in different colors and prints and I won't break the bank doing so :)). The fit is great : not tight, not baggy, the fabric is thin, buttery soft and washes well (the pants kept their shape after a few washes). The waist band is very elastic and the drawstring is easily removable if that's your preference. The one thing I would mention about these pants is that on my frame (5'4") they sit above my belly button, which would make them high-waisted; if you're much taller than me, that may not be the case for you.

Thrilled I took a chance on this product. I was skeptical about less than 15 dollar pants, not gonna lie, but these are better than I hoped. Pardon the mess but I wanted to show the length and my room is a wreck. I’m 5’7 and 130 lbs and took a chance on size small, and they fit like a glove. Waistband feels secure but has some give, and length(which I was most worried about) is just right, resting on my ankle bone. There is a good bit of stretch too, so I can sit or lay however and they don’t feel stressed or pulled too tight. I do wish there was a little more slouch in the calves above the bands, but purely for aesthetic reasons. They’re also really, really soft. Thin but the kind of soft that’s inherently warm. I’ve ordered two more!

Different fabrics for different styles… I ordered two pair of these (black) and then black with orange zipper pocket. Wish these came in XS… 5 star quality joggers for sure. I’m 5”1 and 105 lbs and I got size small. I was worried they’d be too big because I’m an XS at Old Navy and they are. Granted, I kept them and flip the waistband over once, but I do that on almost all my athletic wear because I’m short, anyway. They’re made out of athletic weight brushed polyester- which, for those of you who liked the old LuLaRoe leggings before they turned to crap, picture that buttery soft fabric, but nice and thick and stretchy with really good drape. They’re solid and opaque, the and the seams are finished well—way better quality than you should expect for $12. I got 3 pairs and I’m pretty sure that isn’t going to be enough. They’re drapey and slim fitting, so you can get the put together “athleisure” look, rather than the “I’m wearing pajamas in public” look. They’re super cute with a solid tee or a tank- really easy to style. Definitely several notches above sweatpants, but with all of the comfort. The gold zippers on this pair make them even more stylish. Pardon my pic- it’s midnight and I’m already comfy in a hoodie but you get the idea. EDIT- after reading a bunch of reviews from customers my height and weight, the consensus is that they’re definitely not for petites. I wish these came in XS because I have no doubt they’d be perfect then.

Not capris, but I love them!! I love these joggers! They are so soft and comfortable, I can see why many of the reviewers ordered multiple colors! I am 5’ tall and weigh in between 145-150 lbs. I normally wear a size medium, but I wasn’t sure which size to get because I wanted these to be loose and comfortable. Based on other reviewer‘s mentioning that these run a little big, I decided to stick with medium size anyway. These are really loose and baggy (but in a good comfy lounge pant way). They are definitely not capris on me and hit right above the ankle. I actually LOVE and prefer this length to capris anyway. I’m sure they would be more cropped or capri for anyone taller than 5’! I’m glad I didn’t order a bigger size because I think they would have been too big, baggy, and frumpy. These are so easy to pull on and off, the waist is elastic with a drawstring tie. The color purple is so deep and vibrant, it reminds me of royalty purple. These pants are great and you can’t beat the price, oh and they even have pockets!!

I was hopeful these would match my Eggplant scrub tops I already owned and was delighted to find they DO! HALLELUJAH! Because let me tell you, they are SO soft and comfy. Great pair of Joggers for the price! I really love the feel of these joggers they are a buttery material. The fit is perfect and the draw strings/waist is cute too. I was looking for an in between of buttery and wind breaker material not so much cotton or fleece and these are perfect. I purchased a lulu lemon dupe before I bought these and I love them they are defiantly thicker than these joggers but I am so happy this this purchase. These aren't see through when stretched and are great for the price! I will stay they smelled pretty heavy of chemicals when I opened the packaging but I washed and air dried them. Pictures are after they were washed and air dried. I am 5'3 (almost 5'4) and about 169lbs I ordered a size M for reference.

Legging-like but comfortable. I just got these pants today and tried them on as soon as I got them! I was so excited after reading reviews but that nervous excited because of the negative reviews also. So they weren’t 110% to my expectations. I was looking for a sweatpant type because I love lounging around and I wear sweatpants EVERYWHERE I go. My first thought when I took them out of packaging: wow these are extremely soft!! Once I put them on, I noticed they were kind of... long.. in the crotch area? So I was like okay, I will pull them up more. I pulled them up more (literally above my belly button and even folded the top of them) and then I had a CAMEL TOE lol 😭 but I pulled them down very slightly and it seemed to help but it still has some extra room in the crotch area. I bought a medium, medium is my normal size. I wear a size 9 in pants and weigh 140 pounds, 5’3 in height. I would definitely be buying a small if I decide to buy with this company again. As others have said, it is a lot like a legging material. I get the name of the company literally has LEGGINGS in the name lol, but the title of the pants are joggers so I assumed it would be less leggingy. I just hate leggings because I have panty lines from them and haven’t yet found a decent underwear that is comfortable, safe for my bits, and is a no show. 🤦🏼‍♀️ Over all I am rating a 4 stars because they are super soft, super comfortable, and I will still definitely be wearing them! I am writing this review on 5/29/20 and will update if anything happens to them!

Omg these are my new favorite pants! I want to wear them every day! They are buttery soft, fit just right, flattering, and perfect for wearing out to run errands or sleeping in as pjs. I was skeptical for $14.99 but I recently broke my pelvis and needed some comfortable pants that were easy to get on, comfortable to wear, don’t put any pressure on my mid section, pelvis or legs, thick enough to wear out of the house, and don’t show off my flabby tummy. These are perfect! The medication I’ve had to take has caused me to gain 60 lbs in the last 6 months so not only did I have no clothing that fit, my body shape has changed drastically (the meds actually redistribute body fat in several areas so almost every article of clothing I’ve tried on has looked terrible except these). I love that they have pockets and the waist is just high/fitted enough so I don’t look like I have a bloated tummy but not so high that I look like I’m wearing old lady clothing (I’m 41). For reference I’m currently 5’6 1/2” about 180 lbs and I bought size Large.

I love these pants true to size. Nice length and fit at waist. I love these joggers they are Fab! I was hesitant to buy them because there was a lot of mixed reviews but thought I would risk it. For reference I am 5 foot 7 and I weigh 159 and wear a size large. I have long legs and a long torso so I was afraid these would be too short because a lot of the joggers say there length are only 28 inch or so and I have a 30 inch inseam. They fit great with length right down to the ankle their high waisted fit above my belly button which is important for me because I have a tummy and it eliminates muffin top. For me they are very true to size. They are a nice fitted look. The only reason I did not give them five stars as they have a slight baggy crotch with cameltoe so you can't tuck in a shirt with them. For the price they're really great. They are a very light material so great for spring and summer. I will definitely be ordering more of these. UPDATE returning these beacause of the camel toe issue. My shirts are not long enough to cover it. Otherwise love these

Joggers for tall women These Womens joggers were perfect for me. I bought them to go with a bright 80’s jacket for a costume and fell in love with them. They are buttery soft and the pink color is very vibrant. I am 5’10” and I always struggle to find bottoms that are long enough. These hit right at the ankle and are super stretchy. They have an elastic waist but also have tie strings for adjusting. Bonus, they have pockets!
