Love these! I’m 5’3 and 140 lbs and the medium fits perfectly! Fabric is super soft and love the pockets. Some reviews say the fabric is thin, but that isn't the case with the polka dotted pattern. I couldn’t see through them when held up to the light. I was worried that the elastic would be tight on my stomach but it’s super comfortable.

Too Thin material. Fit like sweatpant drawstring but leggings stretch with pockets. linty. problem obviously with the tag being sewn in two places that caused the butt seam and the waist seam to be connected and stretched weird. It wouldnt have been a major issue and i wouldnt have minded figuring out cutting the tag off if the pants werent also see thru. The fabric feels like the kind that would quickly fuzz up after washings and catches lots of lint. Super thin stretchy material. Strange combination of thin legging material and feel but sweatpant look?? Awkward. it is a rather a bit see thru for being leggings maybe the color has something to do with it besides the material also being very thin. Got the marigold yellowish color, which is nice. After checking carefully the size chart from the seller, I bought a one size up because i wanted the pants more flowy instead of leggings skin tight, and it still fit a way tad snug on the hips. They would be okay as leggings if worn with a longer shirt to cover bum. They fit like a sweatpants but unfortunately the material is way thin. So thin that you can read a nature valley granola bar thru them. I don’t think they look right on me plus the issue with the same one tag opposite edges, being sewn in to two different seams as of they’re trying to make you choose to cut the tag off based on where you prefer the tag to stay. Weird. ! so im returning. For ref I bought 1xl and my hips are 44, waist 37 and im a bit under 5’2short. Other items I got from leggings depot were great. This one missed the mark.

Soft and long length I am 5’11” 185lbs, size 12. Ordered XL and fits great. A little loose but makes them comfortable. Material very soft and longer length! Would recommend for taller women.

I got my first pair of these 6/7 months ago and didn't like them off the bat because of seam/loose string issues. They gave me a refund so I turned them into work pants anyways. Ended up liking these for work as time went on and just got a new pair. Still a few loose ends from the seams but the pants held up well still considering so I'm not too worried- and I'm gonna designate these for work anyways. Interesting to find out when you first get them (black pair) they fit like leggings and are pretty tight throughout, after repeated use and wash they loosen up a considerable amount to feel more like joggers. As you can see on the gray pair I rubbed thru the thighs on these, but for a cheap pair of work pants (that have decent sized pockets) they do the job just fine. I wear an XL and it fit true to size at 5'9 ~255lbs

Really great for the price! So comfy! For size reference, I’m 5’9” and 130 lbs. I orderEd the medium and that’s a good fit for me. These are pretty cute!!! And super comfortable. So buttery soft!!! And I also love the giant pockets. My only complaints would be 1. The top is a lot looser than the bottom. I realize that is now joggers are supposed to be, but if there could be just a tadddd less fabric in the top, then I think these would be absolutely perfect. And 2. I had hoped I would be able to dress these up for work (I’m a teacher). But I’m not sure if that is doable. The fabric is so soft and loose that I’m just not sure I can pull it off! They do resemble more like sweatpants. But they are PERFECT for running errands or wearing around the house or throwing on after work. Very casual. I’m going to order some more colors for fall (wine and olive) because you seriously cannot beat this price! And I have hopes that I’ll somehow be able to dress those up a little more? We will see!

Most comfortable pants I have ever owned I LOVE THESE PANTS. I bought them on a whim during COVID-19 because I needed more lounge pants because who want to wear real pants for working from home?? They far exceeded my expectations. They are wildly comfortable for sleeping, wearing around the house, running errands, etc. I am 5'3" and ~138lbs, I bought a medium. The pockets are fairly deep and the waistband is very comfortable. It has both elastic and a tie string but the elastic is perfect for my waist without pinching, haven't needed to use the string to keep them up. I am quite curvy in the hips/butt/thigh area so it's a little snug on my rear but my mom told me my butt looks great in them so I'll take it! The calf is also a little snug, not the elastic around the ankle (no problems there) but the material around the calf. I have muscular calves and often struggle to find knee-high boots that zip over my calves so YMMV in these areas. I haven't washed them yet but I've been wearing them all week and they haven't stretched noticeably. For only $14-15 a pair I'm considering dedicating my wardrobe to these pants. Highly recommend.

Wrong color, panty lines, SOFT material They are super soft. This specific color does not seem see through or transparent as some have said. They’re thin but opaque. I’m 5’2, 119 lbs and I got the small, happy with the size. I have a short torso so they ride sort of high, right under my belly button. I wish the didn’t ride so high but on a person with medium torso-length, it would probably sit a couple inches under the belly button. I’m really disappointed with the order because as you can see in my picture, the color isn’t quite as expected. I bought these for the off pink shade but it has a strong purple undertone. I took it under a light that gives a little more of an orange-y yellow hue so it cancels the purple a little, but it just doesn’t look that pink, it’s too dark. The label says “mauve” which is more or less correct. The draw string was also supposed to be white which would have been cute but you’ll notice it’s the mauve color instead. It’s probably a hassle to return them so I won’t bother because they’re at least soft. Another bit you’d want to know is that the smooth material tends to cling in the butt, so I got panty lines wearing these. It would be better with a thong or thin, seamless underwear.

Comfy! *check update* I am 5’4 220 pounds, super wide in the hips so I bought a 2xl because in the past a 1xl fit like a large and I was worried they wouldn’t fit, which was my mistake. They fit a little loose but these things are so dang comfortable I can’t even be mad about it. When they say they’re smooth like butter they are not lying. Besides them being a bit loose I love them. *Update* I’ve had these for a whole 2 years now, they’re still in great condition, haven’t gone see through or warn down in any way and still smooth like butter, I wear them consistently so they’re WELL used. Like I stated previously they were too big when I ordered, but I have gained weight and they fit perfectly now so I would *still* order a size down, Im still 5’4 and weigh 240 with a 40’’ waist and 56’’ hips and 2xl fits me perfectly. I love these pants soooooo much I want to own every single pair. SOOOO COMFY !!!!!

Decent jogger style pants Great price, and actually long enough for me. Material is thinner, not too loose and not tight. I feel they are tts, I normally wear a L and these fit as expected. (I’m 5’9” & 155)

My new favorite! I bought these because I wanted to try a jogger style pant. I am always having trouble because I am short (5'4) have a smaller waist and large hips, booty, and thighs (138 lbs) which make buying pants that fit (and are flattering) terribly difficult. I scanned the sizes, and reviews to try to find MY body type. After my research, I decided to go with a Medium, which I usually don't usually do because of tightness (usually bump to a large). I tried to avoid the whole saggy crotch thing so I figured, give it a shot. They are perfect! They are not super loose but they are not tight. You can wear them as high waisted joggers or at your waist and not look silly. The fabric is very soft (think LuLaRue) but I am concerned it will puncture or come apart easily. So far, they are great- Enough that I bought camo and then a pair in black. If anything else changes, I will be sure to update :)

Tall fat girl says BUY THESE I bought these bc the design was super cute and I've been trying to find replacement joggers for an old pair that's falling apart. As a gal with a gut, if you're trying to hide your stomach or look for compression then these probably aren't what you're looking for. The elastic might also be too tight for some people who wear a 3X like me, depending on how you carry your weight, but that could prob be fixed by taking out the drawstring. The pockets are also comically tiny, but will (barely) hold my phone when I am moving from one room to another (wouldn't recommend anywhere else bc...it barely fits). HOWEVER!!! That said, these are so soft and so comfortable that I'll basically live in them anyway. I'm already trying to decide which design to get next, I might collect them all! They keep me warm but they're not hot, perfect for spring/summer. I'm 5'11" and the length is still great on me which is usually not the case for any pants I buy online. Highly, highly recommend these.

Really soft! I am 5’3” and 170 lbs, normally a size 12 in jeans. I heeded the warnings and got the XL. The calf and ankles aren’t as snug as I’d prefer but it’s understandable since I’m between sizes. I’m comfortable in these but they are honestly not what I expected. Super soft and stretchy, slight snag on both ankle seams and a hole in my pocket, they are more suited to lounge wear than activewear because the material doesn’t breathe well. Well made but... Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Nice leggings I ordered two pairs of the Plus Size: one in white and one in navy. I placed the order on Monday evening, and they arrived today, which was good not having to wait long to get them. I tried both on. They are very soft and stretchy, but fairly clingy on the thighs, behind, and mid-section (and I could see my cellulite with the white pair). I am 5'8" tall, weigh 180 pounds (yes, I'm overweight), and wear a size 14 or XL in bottoms. I don't see how these would fit as comfortably in sizes much larger than my size 14/XL if you're curvy and/or overweight. I liked that they were long enough to reach ankles even after getting them around my hips, behind, and tummy. My ankles are small and lower legs are thin, and these were not way too loose at the bottom. I included a picture my thigh in the white pair with a red paper underneath to show that the white is somewhat sheer, and I/you can see through them some. But that's okay with me, since I'll only be wearing these with long, loose tops, so they won't be visible much above the thighs. The navy pair was not sheer to me. Overall, I like them, would recommend buying them, and will probably reorder in at least one other color. Hopefully, this long review was .

Old leggings are obsolete,look no further this is your NEW generation leggings ! Believe it or not this is my first try with this type of material in leggings, and I love it ! If you remember the old ones did not shape well and kind of left your figure looking odd shaped and very exposed so the best way we deal with it is " time to bring out the big tunic " and cover all that up. Well the material in these are magical , soft and lovely suedy feel they also SMOOTH things out very well and give nice shape to you. Now don't get me wrong I still need to cover my c section shelf belly which easily can be done with a nice loose short top. but that's it don't need to bring out the big (guns) :) tunics for this. You can once again show your great shape without covering everything up and NOT see thru ! One point Im adding is im 5'5 165lbs size 14 and brought plus size, the waist band is tight but the rest is loose not sure this will fit easily on anyone size 18 and up I just wish I could have tried the yoga waist band ones. Hope I helped you.

The softest leggings you will ever find... HOWEVER... I purchased these leggings to make an outfit with the new fad of Kimono tops. I selected the pair in white. First, let me say that when I opened the package of these and touched them for the first time, I totally fell in love. They were so amazingly soft. However, my love instantly turned to disdain after I discovered all of the issues with the leggings. First, the entire seam of the back of the leggings (at the buttocks) was half-ripped and looked as if there was an issue when sewing them. (See photos) I immediately knew that I had to return them but I wanted to get a new pair, so I did an exchange. The other issue with my first order was that it had strange stains in multiple places. I notified Amazon and got my exchange started. The new pair arrived quickly and they were just as soft and luxurious as the first leggings! Thankfully, this time the leggings were free of any defects. They fit perfectly (I am 5'8, 185 pounds and all hips and thighs- I purchased a Plus Size). I probably could have ordered the next size down, too.The white leggings are slightly see-through and be fair warned, if your legs aren't toned, it will look like you got hit in the legs by a bag of nickels. Regardless, I still liked them a ton and will likely buy them in other colors!

Not so flattering on me personally, but very comfy So these just aren’t my favorite leggings, but they may work for you? I got two pair navy & burgundy, kept the navy sent the burgundy back, not bc of the color but bc of the fit. Pros: -price -softness, they are very almost velvety or maybe “brushed” would be the term, they almost feel fuzzy, maybe more appropriate for winter, except they aren’t particularly warm -stitching was fine, no errors/flaws seem well made -colors were nice I got navy which was a very dark midnight blue/navy the photos are a good representation and I got burgundy which was a deep raspberry shade of burgundy pics were a good representation here too. -design-have a nice cut, the lines are good, I like the emblem at the back of waistband, it’s nice Cons: -very long (and I’m 5’8) they wrinkle with the excess length at my ankles (see pic) -loose, maybe they run big and I should have gotten a medium, but you can see in pic how they bunch at the crotch with too much fabric -loose, no compression, I really enjoy compression, these don’t hold you in any... but some people like that. -loose, perhaps comfortable for lounging but if you’re working out, or just vacuuming, I found myself pulling them up a lot and then when I pull them up they get all bunched and look crummy -look wet/show sweat, don’t dry especially fast, so if you sweat it’ll show for a while In the end I did keep one pair, but I kinda wish I’d just sent them both back bc I don’t like these nearly as much as others i have. If you’re just into comfy these might be just your cup of tea, but I’m not wearing them to the gym personally. If you are on the line size-wise I’d go down a size, I’m 5’8, 165-170, waist 30, hip 40. If you are petite these might be super long on you. Hope my thoughts help your decision.

3D knitted with minimal seams = comfort, super warm and super stretchy 3-D knitted fleece legging with soft and plush fleece lining. Feel super warm and cozy. Good stretch and extra thick fabric, 6” high waistband with crotch gusset and no front or back seams for a smooth comfortable fit. Only seams are inside leg that is a raised overlock seam but NOT flat stitched. Super wide waistband without elastic is very comfortable and provides gentle compression to my tummy. I’m very happy that the leg length/in-seam comes down to my ankles even though I am tall.”. These will work perfect as long underwear for my tourist Antarctica trip. Based on the size chart I picked the XL large based on my body size of 40/34/39, 184 pounds. The fit is perfect. I measured the in-seam at 30” where as the size chart says 27.5”, I’m very happy they are longer than advertised. The other chart dimensions are body dimensions and not garment dimensions. My photo has garment dimensions for the XL: waist 25”, Hips 29”. Overall: good heavy weight fleece leggings that fit great and are warm. I would buy again.

LOVE! Gem of a find! I’m 5’5.5” and 133lbs and the Size Small fit great! The high-waist band is sooo comfy and does not dig into my stomach and skin (even while sitting after a big meal) the way so many leggings do. I wear skin-tight leggings most days and have been on the hunt for a pair of joggers that hug my body but are still loose enough to let my lady flower breathe and not give the moose-knuckle effect. IKYK. The bottom bands are tight enough to not slide down your calf (maintaining the jogger look) but not so tight that they squeeze your calf and leave imprints. The material is a thicker spandex polyester material that is quick-drying and is not see through! These pants are great for travel and minimalists alike. So many leggings and joggers I’ve purchased show my leg tattoos through the material in bright light- these do not!! And they’re not baggy like most joggers I’ve tried are. These truly are quality pants that I feel will last many years! And the price point? Perfect!! I cannot believe I’ve spent money on much more expensive name-brand joggers only to be disappointed in the baggy fit or so-so quality of material. The pockets on these are great too! They actually fit my phone and keys! I rarely leave reviews on anything but these deserve a solid review! I will definitely be buying more pairs! Gem of a find! Highly recommend to anyone looking for joggers that still make your butt look great!

Great fit for a pregnant belly! :) To say I love leggings is an understatement. I mean, let’s be honest: what woman doesn’t like being comfortable? I have an active 2 year old boy, and am 27 weeks pregnant with my second child on the way! I bought these and started wearing them early on in pregnancy (when the belly wasn’t really showing) and they fit well. I didn’t buy them with pregnancy in mind, just for general comfort/ looking for a style that was a good midway point from a super loose jogger and a legging. BUT they have surprising been phenomenal during pregnancy as well. So many of my other leggings and shorts have been stowed away but I honestly can see these lasting my whole pregnancy! The band is a TRUE high rise on me- which I really, really appreciate. The material is true work out/ sweat wicking and thick enough to be squat proof. I’ve been very active in these and they’ve held up well so far! I am 5’1 and have gained about 16 pounds in pregnancy so far(staring weigh: 114lbs, currently ~130lbs). I purchase the small (which was the smallest size I saw available when I went to purchase it) and they fit great. Since I’m short they are just at/above my ankles length wise which I love. They basically fit me like a normal jogger should with no bunching. I personally don’t anticipate the belly band portion being stretched out too much/ to the point where it won’t fit well post pregnancy and therefore think these will last me well till the very end, and then after baby arrives! But at this price point I’d be happy to buy another pair if need be! I’m a staunch believer in not keeping ANY clothing piece that you don’t LOVE how they fit. And I wouldn’t be keeping these unless they checked all the boxes. They do! If this helped you out in any way, let me know with the “ ” button below my comment. Happy shopping, lovely ladies. :)

Very stretchy, fit depends on your comfort level 5'5 125-130 pounds size 26 waist, I got these in a Small. I like that they hug my waist in the right way. They look super cute and comfy. The only real downsides are that they feel like bathing suit material so it's like wearing a bathing suit on my legs. I might just get used to that but it was off-putting at first for sure. Flattering but will definitely show cellulite, which is fine but just be aware. And the material is shiny which I wasn't really expecting so that was off-putting as well. I'll have to wear them throughout the day and see if the ankles slide up as well, since they're about to be high waters as is. Overall I feel like the other reviews are accurate and I'm pretty satisfied with them. Also I love that the waist is stretchy in the best way so in case I get pregnant I'll still have at least one pair of comfortable pants that fit.

Happily Surprised! I’m always unsure when purchasing pants. I’m 5’6” and about 145lbs. I carry all my weight in my butt, thighs, and calves. According to the size chart (and most size charts), I’m a medium at my waist measurement, but a large at my hips measurement (42 inches) and I have thick calves. I decided to order a large and I expected it to maybe be loose around the waist and maybe even baggier in the legs, but at least not strained on my thighs. I was sooo happy with this fit! It’s comfortable at my waist, not too tight on my thighs, but still pretty fitted. The fabric is thicker than I would have expected for $20. I felt comfortable squatting at the gym. I’ve worn them to work out in- weight lifting and treadmill- and they’ve been seriously great! I don’t have to pull them up or anything. They stay put. I recommend these, and I plan to buy more in the future. P.S. they do say to hand wash and flat dry. I washed mine in the machine, then flat dried and they were good to go!

Still keeping them! :)

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

WTH? I purchased a pair of these 725 Levi's in a size too small (recently lost a lot of weight)so I'm confused on what size I wear. My next choice was an 8 short, my photo show how they arrived (nice, huh?) These pants aren't close to "high waisted", and they almost look like I'm wearing early 2000's jeans but with a cropped flare. They are MID RISE w/ a flare and close to being long capris. Bottom line.... Shop for clothes in person!!!

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Garment Shrank 4 inches after washing! These are Misses size 16 Short. I am 5'6" 170lbs. I ordered two pair. I tried one of them on and they fit, except were too big in the back waist (as expected). I usually order short, since that is exactly the right length because I never wear heels with jeans. These were the right length and fit in the leg and all around. I planned to have the waist taken in, but decided to wash one pair, to see what the shrinkage was and whether or not I should wash the other pair before having the alteration done. I thought they might shrink some, but was really floored to see how much they shrank! Now, they are way too short and too tight in the legs and too small all around. Do everything jean I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

SHORT, BIGGER GIRLS—buy these jeans! My body type: 5’ 5” and 242# with short, thick legs and a belly. I’m not familiar with this type of sizing, so I went with a 38 Short (I usually wear a 20 short in “regular” sizes). These jeans feel like they were made just for me! The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

They stretch quickly and material is low quality Original review: I liked these when I got them because they're FINALLY a decent length for me. I'm 5'6" and regular length jeans typically have a 29" inseam which is just too short. These have a 30" inseam which is perfect. When I purchased, I bought a 34 and a 33 and the 33 fit perfect. They're reasonably soft while also being a decent weight. However, after wearing just a short while, they're immediately sagging and stretched just a little too much. If I was to buy again, I'd size down to a 32. More disappointing than the sizing though which is just a minor thing is that I've worn them about two weeks and I already have two holes/pulls on the butt. I have worn jeans for 30+ years and never, ever had a pair develop holes like this. For material that I thought was a heavy and good quality, I'm surprised they got damaged so easily. Edited 12/20/23 - more pulls/holes in the butt. See pic I added. I wear jeans literally every day and have never had this happen to a single other pair of jeans I've owned. I have to assume this is a problem specifically with Levi or this batch or something but I will not be ordering another pair. They also shrunk (shortened) after a few washes. I'm moving on and will look elsewhere for my next pair.

Freaking Jackpot! Normally when I buy jeans, I spend 20-30 minutes crying in front of the mirror in a self loathing kind of way. I’ve suffered from BDD since I can remember and pants, jeans especially, have always been my biggest nemesis. All that being said… sweet baby Jesus these jeans are the freaking epitome of comfort. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 130lbs and haven’t seen the inside of a gym in probably 10 years and my ass is clearly nonexistent — I purchased the Maui Views (Waterless) in a size (4) 27 Short. Unpopular opinion but I detest high waisted jeans. I’m “short torso - long leg” so hard pass on anything that even gets near my belly button. I was nervous about the rise of these but am so glad I took the chance because they truly sit just perfectly under my belly button and at my waist. I’ve never felt so comfortable and honestly, like myself, in a pair of jeans. I could not recommend a pair more than these. *only hiccup in my purchase was that the original pair claimed to have been delivered but was in fact not. Amazon customer service quickly rectified that and sent a replacement pair overnight.

AMAZING for athletic builds! Ok, I’m SHOCKED by how much I love these jeans!! For reference, I’m a very athletic build- 5’4” 39-27-39 measurements, very thick thighs. Buying jeans can be a nightmare. I ordered these 721’s in “29 Regular” and they fit true to size. I’m 5’6”, 150#, and normally a size 29 in Levi’s for reference. I ordered this pair in the “soft clean white” and it is a true white, not that dingy white that you typically see in Levi’s shorts and pants. However, this white is see-through. The denim fabric feels thick, but the pocket lining and a nude thong can be seen right through it. So keep that in mind. I am planning to pair these pants with a long enough shirt, so it won’t really matter for me. The denim has some stretch, but also still feels like I’m wearing jeans. These pants are cute and perfect for spring. I just wish they weren’t so sheer. The long hunt for opaque white pants continues.

It was also pretty long, so turning up the bottoms would be necessary for me. For reference, I'm 5'7", around 130ish pounds. I purchased the 6 Medium High Rise Skinny in Soft Black.

Perfect fit and amazing quality As someone who is considered “midsize” and not quite plus, it’s often difficult to find jeans and even harder to find good reliable reviews to help gauge fit when shopping online. Decided to try these out when I saw them on sale… I didn’t own any Levi’s jeans before this. I read the reviews and based on that, I sized up… I’m 5’5” and typically wear a 14 or 16 regular, depending on the brand. They didn’t have any regular length left, so I thought I’d try a 34short and return if it didn’t work out. Shipping was extremely fast and I received them the next day. I immediately tried them on and the fit is PERFECT 🙌🏻 Just enough stretch to feel comfy and perfect length. Glad I sized up too because they fit like a glove 👍🏻 I prefer high rise jeans and these hit at a comfortable spot for me. Highly recommend. Great quality and the perfect staple for any wardrobe. I agree with the recommendation to size up so keep that in mind when ordering.

Great length for short girls, stretchier and looser fit than other 721s The best thing about these jeans for me is the length. I am 5'4, and I always have a hard time finding jeans that are not too long. I have a few other regular Levis 721 in the short length, and these come slightly above where those hit on the ankle, which I like. The waist and bottom area do not fit the same as my other 721 pairs. The waist is bigger and the crotch is much lower on these. I was also not expecting them to have so much stretch to them, so they will only get more loose fitting throughout the day. These pictures were taken after I put them through the wash and dryer. All in all, they are not the most flattering pair of jeans that I own, but they will be fine for throwing on to go run errands because they are comfortable. I will probably try another color/wash because I really love the length of them.

Just OK "jeans" I was really excited to get these pants but was a little less than wowed when I tried them on. Here's why: These pants comfortably fit right at the belly button. I got size 26 short (I am 5'3 & 119lbs) I think I could have gotten a size smaller and it would have been a better fit, because when you sit down in them you get this weird bag in the crotch, but I'm sure they'll be nice after a wash or two. They didn't really feel like denim, though, more like a "jegging" mix. The exact blend is: 68% cotton, 23% polyester, 8% viscose, 1% elastane (compared to a pair of older Levi's 524 blend: 99% cotton, 1% elastane) Another thing to note- they pick up every piece of lint and dog hair around, but that's kind of expected since they are black pants, after all.

Size discrepancy UPDATE: I *did* order the lighter wash (called Fascinating Fact) in a size 29 waist hoping for more room in the thigh, but in the image you can see the gap I circled in the back. it's pretty bad. All you old school Levi's gals, the ones who ordered that Supreme Curvy cut they used to have: these pants are not for you. When I returned the 726 style in store, the manager confirmed: it appears that an ample derriere is no longer in style, and they are not making pants for us right now. ORIGINAL REVIEW: What sadness! I got the 28x30L darker wash (Carbon Glow) and loved them (I measure 24 natural waist, 41 hips). But it seems like a miracle that this pair worked, because I then ordered the lighter wash and I have no idea how they’re the same size or even the same cut. Much less room in the bum area; you can see in this picture where I overlapped them how much of the dark pants sticks out. The crotch, the waist height, the thigh area - everything is so much smaller. I might order the 29 waist and see if they work, but this sizing inconsistency is so sad.

Perfect jeans to elongate legs, but color bleeds 4 stars instead of 5 because it is not colorfast. First pic is after 3-4 rinses in the sink. Second pic is after the 6th rinse. I would not wear this with anything white until it is run through the wash a few times. Otherwise, I love these jeans because they are very flattering to girls with short legs like me. I’m 5’1” and less than 108 lbs but am blessed with ample hips and short legs. If it’s too high waisted I look very bottom heavy. If too low rise my proportions look off. These have just the right amount of high rise. The dark wash, straight leg and positioning of the back pockets all do me favors. I will have to hem these as I do with almost all pants and will not be rolling the ankle because the inner color of fabric is light. Rolling the cuff will create a light band at the bottom that detracts from the elongating effect. Fabric does feel nice and soft yet sturdy. It’s not stiff and there is a slight stretch to accommodate ease of movement. Seams are all well done and pockets seem well reinforced. I expect these to last me many years. Amazon fashion purchases are always hit and miss, but if you are disciplined about returning items you don’t love, true bargains can be had. This is definitely a score despite the color bleeding.

Short length isn’t short at all I’m a 00 short (5’2; ~110lbs), and Levi’s sizing can be very hit and miss. I ended up getting a 25 short (to size up one to be safe), but these are quite large yet. Unfortunately as titled, the “short” (usually have inseam of 26-27”) are 31” long and the back has a lot of gapping. Inseam: Short 30”, Regular 32” I purchased 24 Regular, Carbon Glow. I normally wear W24/L30 in Levi’s (I’m 5’2” and 103 lb). All the Levi’s jeans I bought in the past was L30 and it’s perfect length for me. It says Regular=32” on this page, I knew it would be longer than what I have but I didn’t expect this 724 high rise straight (724 high rise “slim”straight on tag) would be that long. I’m going to return this and order short one. This jeans fits like leggings (it’s not thick material), stretchy and comfortable. It’s 71% Cotton/21% Viscose/6% Polyester/2% Elastane, made in Cambodia. Waist size is perfect, from hip to thigh fits like skinny/slim jeans and relaxed fit from knee down.

Much Longer cut than 721 (High Rise Skinny) I have a pair of 721's (High Rise Skinny) in Soft Black that I wear all the time, and the fit is perfect, so I figured the same size in 724 (also Soft Black) would be a slam dunk! But they are at least 3-4 inches longer! Why, Levi's? Why? The material is exactly the same, the cut is just waaaay longer. For reference, I'm 5'6 145 lbs and size 30 fits great- snug, but stretchy! In Soft Black at least. I've found every wash is a different amount of stretch so this review is for Soft Black only! Update- Chelsea Hour Dark Indigo had a similar fit to Soft Black, although a tad shorter. Even though they're cropped, they're way too long. Even the 10S hit the top of my foot and bunched slightly. They might shrink a bit after washing. Verdict: returning. Update 2: Chelsea Pier Dark Indigo - pics in the ad are super misleading. These are a thin extra stretchy denim - like super stretchy jegging material - and the 10S/30S brushed the top of my feet. The model for this style must be 12' tall bc they look like crops of her! The color is a very cute blue but only shoe I could wear these with is a flip flops or they'll bunch up

I was hoping that these would be great lounge pants, maybe even comfy enough to sleep in. The fabric is not at all what I expected, and it definitely would not be described as soft. The first thing that struck me when I opened this package was a label stating that these were waterproof. What the heck? I somehow missed that in the description, and it was certainly not something I needed. They are also SPF, according to the tags, and that is ok by me, but also not something I was seeking. These pants are a bit too short on me (I'm 5'6") even without shoes. There is a slit on the side, but it is unnecessary for ease of dressing because the fit is very loose. In fact, the legs are too baggy for my taste. I think the relaxed fit would be ok if the fabric was more like soft, lounge pants, but it doesn't work for me in this plasticky feeling fabric. The pants have a high waist, and a drawstring as well as an elasticized waist. The waist fits me fine, without cinching the drawstring, but I suspect that some might find it to be snug. There are two deep pockets, a great idea. This goes in the machine for wash and dry, and that is one thing this odd fabric handles just fine. These pants were not at all what I was expecting in fit, style or feel. If, however, you are looking for SPF pants that are waterproof and loose fitting for outdoors activities, these might be just right for you.

I was hoping that these would be great lounge pants, maybe even comfy enough to sleep in. The fabric is not at all what I expected, and it definitely would not be described as soft. The first thing that struck me when I opened this package was a label stating that these were waterproof. What the heck? I somehow missed that in the description, and it was certainly not something I needed. They are also SPF, according to the tags, and that is ok by me, but also not something I was seeking. These pants are a bit too short on me (I'm 5'6") even without shoes. There is a slit on the side, but it is unnecessary for ease of dressing because the fit is very loose. In fact, the legs are too baggy for my taste. I think the relaxed fit would be ok if the fabric was more like soft, lounge pants, but it doesn't work for me in this plasticky feeling fabric. The pants have a high waist, and a drawstring as well as an elasticized waist. The waist fits me fine, without cinching the drawstring, but I suspect that some might find it to be snug. There are two deep pockets, a great idea. This goes in the machine for wash and dry, and that is one thing this odd fabric handles just fine. These pants were not at all what I was expecting in fit, style or feel. If, however, you are looking for SPF pants that are waterproof and loose fitting for outdoors activities, these might be just right for you.

Poorly made. Faux zipper off centered to the right. These pants are a cheap quality version of slacksweats. They fit pretty well, and are comfortable on, but as soon as I put them on, I immediately noticed that the waist is not centered. You can see in my pictures that it’s off to the right, which is even more noticeable when they’re on. It’s a definite no for me because that’s the only thing I can focus on now; just how much off center they look. So for them to look right with the faux zipper centered on the tummy, I’d have to twist them to one side, which throws off the whole fit of the pants due to the other seams and pockets. Looking back at the other reviews, I can see that many of the other colors are cut/sewn the same way, but may not be as noticeable in a darker color. Bottom line, just not worth it when there are so many other better options with more flowy material that’s not this weird rough waffle weave anyway. I prefer a more sophisticated look, but wanted a pull-on comfortable dress slack. This is definitely not it, even if the seams did line up. FYI, I am 5’6, 190lbs, wear a size medium pant, and these were true to size in a medium.

Pretty waffle knit slacks! Pretty waffle knit long pants, so well made and stylish! The fabric is very soft and the waffle knit is breezy.I like that these are a looser relaxed fit so they are cooling to wear allowing for ventilation. I ordered the size Small in the color Black with 29" inseam and am 5'1" 113 lbs 33-25-37 dimensions and these fit very well, especially the waist and hip areas, the length is a little longer on me by 2 inches so I'd maybe suggest getting the 27" inseam if you have similar dimensions. I cuffed these for a cute look and like how they look! Very happy with these lovely pants! 5/5!

Lightweight, Comfortable, Breathable & Versatile Palazzo Pants I ordered these because I’ve got about 10 pairs of Libin’s cargo joggers, which I love, and these palazzos looked fun. (For reference, I’m 5’2”, 36 inches at the waist, 41” bum). After trying them on here’s my impression: PROS: 1. 100% polyester, so they won’t shrink. The outside is a textured waffle weave that is 100% breathable, but the inside is smooth and soft. 2. SIzing is Spot on. I went by both my usual Libin size (XL) and their sizing chart (36 in waist), and super happy I did. These don’t pinch at the waist, but the top part of the pants aren’t excessively baggy. 3. Versatile and cute. I was just going to try them on this morning, thinking that they’d be great for church, but then I ended up wearing them on today’s errands. Considerations: 1. These are Palazzo pants, meaning there’s a lot of material in the leg. If you’re like me (a bit chunky, significant bum) you’ll want to wear these with a fitted top, to maintain a feminine silhouette. 2. The 27 inch inseam just works on my 5”2” frame (but I have a short waist and long legs for my height). Shoes keep them just off the ground. Anyway, I’m a fan.

Waffle knit, grey, comfortable pants I’m 5’5” and these are a great length for me. They feel like wearing pajamas for sweatpants but look very nice and business professional. I was afraid they would snag easily but they don’t. The grey is neutral but pretty. I wore a wide belt and it fit the holes well. Pleats on me aren’t the most flattering, but that’s what made them so comfy and I didn’t feel they were unattractive on me.

Great for casual wear! I really like these pants. I got them more for casual wear than for work attire, but you could easily dress them up for work. What I really like about these is the waffle knit material. It just gives the pants something different, other than a straight work slack. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and ordered a medium. I almost went with large because I like the waist to feel a little loose, but these are good because the back part is elastic so there’s some stretch to them. I got the 29” so the length is great for me. These pants are definitely worth the price.

Takes awhile to arrive. They fit GReAT and lay very nice. Washed in cold water and dried slightly. The hem at the bottom got weird and I had to iron them flat. I’m a size 14 + and 5’6”, and the XXL fit like a dream. No tight or bunched up areas. This really fits my smaller waist well. I had about 3 inches “extra” all around. I’m ordering 2 more colors in XL. Just hope they fit just as well.

As comfy as leggings, but thicker & dressier (and no cameltoe) As w/ seemingly many others, I bought my first pairs @ CVS (got 3). I don’t normally shop for clothes there, but they were a serendipitous discovery, and having a hard-to-fit body where I don’t often find items that I can wear off-the-rack (I’m an endomorph, or “apple” type, where my waist is short & thick), I wanted to look for more pairs, solids specifically (black, heather grey & indigo w/ gold threading), to have as wardrobe basics. I was THRILLED to find them on Amazon (and they do have their own website, as well), and bought the pairs that I was looking for, after reading reviews from customers who ended up becoming Lildy fans in the same manner. These jeggings are SO comfy that you can literally wear them 24/7, and even for activities that require a broad range of movement (splits, high kicks). I wear them to sleep, because I’m at that stage in my life where I’m too lazy to change in & out of clothes constantly (just turned 47). I have a “uniform” that works for me (wifebeater & jeggings), and it’s nice to be able to simply add accessories, outerwear & shoes, and voila….I’m dressed to go outside. 👌 Prior to finding the Lildy jeggings @ CVS, I was wearing leggings, but would wear a mini over them solely for the coverage to hide the cameltoe. Now, I don’t have to do that because the material for the jeggings is thicker. I save myself from needing to put on an extra garment that constantly shifts from movement, and requires additional labor to adjust & launder. Dressing is much easier, and I effortlessly look pulled together. I always wore bootcut or flared pants (they were more flattering for my silhouette), but these jeggings fit so well that I feel confident about how I look in them (can’t say the same for the ones from Walmart), and unlike w/ flared or bootcut bottoms, these tuck into knee-hi boots w/o any of that extra fabric, and make looking cute such a non-job. Highly recommended, if you feel that the cut will work for your body shape. Great value for the price—you’ll get hooked & end up coming back for more colors & patterns. (For reference, I’m just under 5’2”, ~110 lbs., 28” waist & 28” inseam.) UPDATE: (May 2022) I’m now on my SEVENTH pair of Lildy jeggings! 👖 Just got the olive green solid color. Made just like the rest. I LIVE in these—you can exercise & sleep in these jeggings, and it’s nice that they look like “real pants.” 😆 The grey ones look fairly refined, almost like twill pants, and you just pull’em up like they’re you’re favorite pair of leggings, but might be fine for a “casual nice” work dress code. 🥰

Good quality, not high waisted These are completely opaque, heavier fabric, which I like. The denim is very dark in a good way. I am 5'7", 160 lb and order the L/XL. I was a little disappointed that they were a little more low-rise than they seem to be in the online picture.

No for short people The material is really nice. I am 5’2” and about 200 lbs. These fit just a little loose and I have right much bunching at the ankles as you can see in the photo. Other than that they are great.

Huge and poor construction I’m a curvy size 12/14 and 5’9” so I ordered the large/extra large. These are HUGE. I’d say they’re about a size 16/18. I’m a preschool teacher, so I got them for work and they’ve been falling off all day. The seams on the side are odd as well. I tried to take a picture of that. It’s very wavy and not flattering. Maybe I should have ordered a small/medium? If you’re smaller then a size 10, I wouldn’t even order them. I’m going to try washing them in hot water and dry them. Hopefully they shrink.
Large too big for 165 muscular build Lots of wrinkles behind the knee and around the waistline. I'm 165lbs muscular build and large is too big.

Good Quality Jeggings These denim jeggings feel like they’re made well. The fabric is thick, but still very stretchy and easy to move in. I’m 5’8”, 150lbs, and pear-shaped with a 31.5” waist and 44” hips. The “L-XL” size fits my legs, thighs, and butt perfectly. The waist comes up a bit higher on my torso than I expected and feels a little loose, but I could imagine that if it were tighter, it might create muffin top, which I don’t want. I’m very happy with my purchase and would recommend these jeggings!

Cutest, comfortable pants! These pants are so cute! I weight 130 pounds and 5”3. I ordered a size small and they fit perfectly. Only complaint would be the color. They are definitely orange and not a shade of brown.

Nice design, Wish size X-Small was offered Generous cut. She’ll need to grow into these. I ordered in size small but she’s at that in-between stage of moving from teen clothing to women's. She wears a size women's small in Old Navy brands, but these were not flattering on her 5’ 3” (and growing!), 115# frame. They hung on her. I realize they should drape, but the pleating at the waist, and the length in the leg, gave a look like she was dressing in her mother’s slacks. Worn with a high heel (she doesn’t), and given a press, these would be much better. But out of the package, see photos. Here’s what she liked… She said they were comfortable and airy, but not so airy that you don’t get the coverage you need. She liked that the crotch was not skin tight but thought it could have been a little more fitted because the way it fit on her, it would be creased 24-7. She did like that the waist was fitted but not super tight. And, she liked the style details - flared bottoms, paper bag waist, the button closure, pleats and side pockets. She thought the slacks would make a design statement when you don’t want to wear a dress, and she liked that the bow belt was removable, depending on the occasion. The way she’s growing, it won’t be long before these will fit and drape nicely. Currently, her waist is 29.5”, hip 37”, inseam 28-29” and she is wearing the size small in the photo.
